## AVD_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable Avoidance using ADSBÂ

**Description:**
Enable Avoidance using ADSB

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## AVD_F_ACTION
**Display Name:** Collision Avoidance BehaviorÂ

**Description:**
Specifies aircraft behaviour when a collision is imminent

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Report2Climb Or Descend3Move Horizontally4Move Perpendicularly in 3D5RTL6Hover:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Report
- **2:** Climb Or Descend
- **3:** Move Horizontally
- **4:** Move Perpendicularly in 3D
- **5:** RTL
- **6:** Hover

---

## AVD_W_ACTION
**Display Name:** Collision Avoidance Behavior - WarnÂ

**Description:**
Specifies aircraft behaviour when a collision may occur

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Report:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Report

---

## AVD_F_RCVRY
**Display Name:** Recovery behaviour after a fail eventÂ

**Description:**
Determines what the aircraft will do after a fail event is resolved

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Remain in AVOID_ADSB1Resume previous flight mode2RTL3Resume if AUTO else Loiter:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Remain in AVOID_ADSB
- **1:** Resume previous flight mode
- **2:** RTL
- **3:** Resume if AUTO else Loiter

---

## AVD_OBS_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum number of obstacles to trackÂ

**Description:**
Maximum number of obstacles to track


---

## AVD_W_TIME
**Display Name:** Time Horizon WarnÂ

**Description:**
Aircraft velocity vectors are multiplied by this time to determine closest approach.  If this results in an approach closer than W_DIST_XY or W_DIST_Z then W_ACTION is undertaken (assuming F_ACTION is not undertaken)


---

## AVD_F_TIME
**Display Name:** Time Horizon FailÂ

**Description:**
Aircraft velocity vectors are multiplied by this time to determine closest approach.  If this results in an approach closer than F_DIST_XY or F_DIST_Z then F_ACTION is undertaken


---

## AVD_W_DIST_XY
**Display Name:** Distance Warn XYÂ

**Description:**
Closest allowed projected distance before W_ACTION is undertaken


---

## AVD_F_DIST_XY
**Display Name:** Distance Fail XYÂ

**Description:**
Closest allowed projected distance before F_ACTION is undertaken


---

## AVD_W_DIST_Z
**Display Name:** Distance Warn ZÂ

**Description:**
Closest allowed projected distance before BEHAVIOUR_W is undertaken


---

## AVD_F_DIST_Z
**Display Name:** Distance Fail ZÂ

**Description:**
Closest allowed projected distance before BEHAVIOUR_F is undertaken


---

## AVD_F_ALT_MIN
**Display Name:** ADS-B avoidance minimum altitudeÂ

**Description:**
Minimum AMSL (above mean sea level) altitude for ADS-B avoidance. If the vehicle is below this altitude, no avoidance action will take place. Useful to prevent ADS-B avoidance from activating while below the tree line or around structures. Default of 0 is no minimum.


---

## BARO1_GND_PRESS
**Display Name:** Ground PressureÂ

**Description:**
calibrated ground pressure in Pascals

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** ReadOnly
- **1:** True

---

## BARO_GND_TEMP
**Display Name:** ground temperatureÂ

**Description:**
User provided ambient ground temperature in degrees Celsius. This is used to improve the calculation of the altitude the vehicle is at. This parameter is not persistent and will be reset to 0 every time the vehicle is rebooted. A value of 0 means use the internal measurement ambient temperature.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** degrees Celsius

---

## BARO_ALT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** altitude offsetÂ

**Description:**
altitude offset in meters added to barometric altitude. This is used to allow for automatic adjustment of the base barometric altitude by a ground station equipped with a barometer. The value is added to the barometric altitude read by the aircraft. It is automatically reset to 0 when the barometer is calibrated on each reboot or when a preflight calibration is performed.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** meters

---

## BARO_PRIMARY
**Display Name:** Primary barometerÂ

**Description:**
This selects which barometer will be the primary if multiple barometers are found

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0FirstBaro12ndBaro23rdBaro:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** FirstBaro
- **1:** 2ndBaro
- **2:** 3rdBaro

---

## BARO_EXT_BUS
**Display Name:** External baro busÂ

**Description:**
This selects the bus number for looking for an I2C barometer. When set to -1 it will probe all external i2c buses based on the BARO_PROBE_EXT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled0Bus01Bus16Bus6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **0:** Bus0
- **1:** Bus1
- **6:** Bus6

---

## BARO2_GND_PRESS
**Display Name:** Ground PressureÂ

**Description:**
calibrated ground pressure in Pascals

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** ReadOnly
- **1:** True

---

## BARO3_GND_PRESS
**Display Name:** Absolute PressureÂ

**Description:**
calibrated ground pressure in Pascals

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** ReadOnly
- **1:** True

---

## BARO_FLTR_RNG
**Display Name:** Range in which sample is acceptedÂ

**Description:**
This sets the range around the average value that new samples must be within to be accepted. This can help reduce the impact of noise on sensors that are on long I2C cables. The value is a percentage from the average value. A value of zero disables this filter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 100

---

## BARO_PROBE_EXT
**Display Name:** External barometers to probeÂ

**Description:**
This sets which types of external i2c barometer to look for. It is a bitmask of barometer types. The I2C buses to probe is based on BARO_EXT_BUS. If BARO_EXT_BUS is -1 then it will probe all external buses, otherwise it will probe just the bus number given in BARO_EXT_BUS.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0BMP0851BMP2802MS56113MS56074MS56375FBM3206DPS2807LPS25H8Keller9MS583710BMP38811SPL0612MSP13BMP581:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** BMP085
- **1:** BMP280
- **2:** MS5611
- **3:** MS5607
- **4:** MS5637
- **5:** FBM320
- **6:** DPS280
- **7:** LPS25H
- **8:** Keller
- **9:** MS5837
- **10:** BMP388
- **11:** SPL06
- **12:** MSP
- **13:** BMP581

---

## BARO1_DEVID
**Display Name:** Baro IDÂ

**Description:**
Barometer sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance


---

## BARO2_DEVID
**Display Name:** Baro ID2Â

**Description:**
Barometer2 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance


---

## BARO3_DEVID
**Display Name:** Baro ID3Â

**Description:**
Barometer3 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance


---

## BARO_FIELD_ELV
**Display Name:** field elevationÂ

**Description:**
User provided field elevation in meters. This is used to improve the calculation of the altitude the vehicle is at. This parameter is not persistent and will be reset to 0 every time the vehicle is rebooted. Changes to this parameter will only be used when disarmed. A value of 0 means the EKF origin height is used for takeoff height above sea level.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** meters

---

## BARO_ALTERR_MAX
**Display Name:** Altitude error maximumÂ

**Description:**
This is the maximum acceptable altitude discrepancy between GPS altitude and barometric presssure altitude calculated against a standard atmosphere for arming checks to pass. If you are getting an arming error due to this parameter then you may have a faulty or substituted barometer. A common issue is vendors replacing a MS5611 in a "Pixhawk" with a MS5607. If you have that issue then please see BARO_OPTIONS parameter to force the MS5611 to be treated as a MS5607. This check is disabled if the value is zero.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 5000

---

## BARO_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Barometer optionsÂ

**Description:**
Barometer options

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Treat MS5611 as MS5607:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Treat MS5611 as MS5607

---

## BARO1_WCF_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Wind coefficient enableÂ

**Description:**
This enables the use of wind coefficients for barometer compensation

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## BARO1_WCF_FWD
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in positive X direction (forward)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during forwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO1_WCF_BCK
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in negative X direction (backwards)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during backwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO1_WCF_RGT
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in positive Y direction (right)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the right, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO1_WCF_LFT
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in negative Y direction (left)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the left, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO1_WCF_UP
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in positive Z direction (up)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during climbing flight (or forward flight with a high forwards lean angle), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO1_WCF_DN
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in negative Z direction (down)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during descending flight (or forward flight with a high backwards lean angle, eg braking manoeuvre), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO2_WCF_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Wind coefficient enableÂ

**Description:**
This enables the use of wind coefficients for barometer compensation

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## BARO2_WCF_FWD
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in positive X direction (forward)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during forwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO2_WCF_BCK
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in negative X direction (backwards)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during backwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO2_WCF_RGT
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in positive Y direction (right)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the right, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO2_WCF_LFT
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in negative Y direction (left)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the left, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO2_WCF_UP
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in positive Z direction (up)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during climbing flight (or forward flight with a high forwards lean angle), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO2_WCF_DN
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in negative Z direction (down)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during descending flight (or forward flight with a high backwards lean angle, eg braking manoeuvre), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BARO3_WCF_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Wind coefficient enableÂ

**Description:**
This enables the use of wind coefficients for barometer compensation

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## BARO3_WCF_FWD
**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in positive X direction (forward)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during forwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during backwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range

---

**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in positive Y direction (right)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the right, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range

---

**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in negative Y direction (left)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the left, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in positive Z direction (up)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during climbing flight (or forward flight with a high forwards lean angle), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range

---

**Display Name:** Pressure error coefficient in negative Z direction (down)Â

**Description:**
This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during descending flight (or forward flight with a high backwards lean angle, eg braking manoeuvre), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** -1.0 to 1.0

---

## BATT2_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current
- **5:** Solo
- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic
- **8:** DroneCAN-BatteryInfo
- **9:** ESC
- **10:** Sum Of Selected Monitors
- **11:** FuelFlow
- **12:** FuelLevelPWM
- **13:** SMBUS-SUI3
- **14:** SMBUS-SUI6
- **15:** NeoDesign
- **16:** SMBus-Maxell
- **17:** Generator-Elec
- **18:** Generator-Fuel
- **19:** Rotoye
- **20:** MPPT
- **21:** INA2XX
- **22:** LTC2946
- **23:** Torqeedo
- **24:** FuelLevelAnalog
- **25:** Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage
- **26:** INA239_SPI
- **27:** EFI
- **28:** AD7091R5
- **29:** Scripting

---

## BATT2_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT2_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATT2_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATT2_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATT2_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATT2_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT2_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT2_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT2_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT2_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT2_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT2_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT2_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT2_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT2_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATT2_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATT2_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT2_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT2_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT2_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATT2_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATT2_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATT2_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATT2_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT2_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATT2_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATT2_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

## BATT2_FL_VLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Empty fuel level voltageÂ

**Description:**
The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 10:** volt

---

## BATT2_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATT2_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATT2_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT2_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT2_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT2_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT2_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATT2_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATT2_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT2_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATT2_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATT2_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

## BATT2_ESC_MASK
**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ESC 1
- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3
- **3:** ESC 4
- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8
- **8:** ESC 9
- **9:** ESC 10
- **10:** ESC 11
- **11:** ESC 12
- **12:** ESC 13
- **13:** ESC 14
- **14:** ESC 15
- **15:** ESC 16
- **16:** ESC 17
- **17:** ESC 18
- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## BATT3_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current
- **5:** Solo
- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic
- **8:** DroneCAN-BatteryInfo
- **9:** ESC
- **10:** Sum Of Selected Monitors
- **11:** FuelFlow
- **12:** FuelLevelPWM

- **14:** SMBUS-SUI6

- **23:** Torqeedo

- **25:** Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage
- **26:** INA239_SPI
- **27:** EFI
- **28:** AD7091R5
- **29:** Scripting

---

## BATT3_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT3_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATT3_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATT3_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATT3_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATT3_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT3_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT3_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT3_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT3_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT3_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT3_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT3_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT3_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT3_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT3_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT3_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT3_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT3_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT3_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT3_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATT3_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATT3_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT3_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT3_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT3_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATT3_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATT3_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATT3_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATT3_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT3_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATT3_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**

- **-1 to 1:** hertz

**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT4_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT4_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

## BATT4_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT4_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT4_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT4_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT4_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATT4_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATT4_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT4_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT4_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT4_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATT4_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATT4_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATT4_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATT4_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT4_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATT4_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATT4_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

## BATT4_FL_VLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Empty fuel level voltageÂ

**Description:**
The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 10:** volt

---

## BATT4_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATT4_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATT4_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT4_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT4_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT4_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT4_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATT4_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATT4_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT4_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATT4_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATT4_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

## BATT4_ESC_MASK
**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ESC 1
- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3
- **3:** ESC 4
- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8
- **8:** ESC 9
- **9:** ESC 10
- **10:** ESC 11
- **11:** ESC 12
- **12:** ESC 13
- **13:** ESC 14
- **14:** ESC 15
- **15:** ESC 16
- **16:** ESC 17
- **17:** ESC 18
- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## BATT5_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current
- **5:** Solo
- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic
- **8:** DroneCAN-BatteryInfo
- **9:** ESC
- **10:** Sum Of Selected Monitors
- **11:** FuelFlow
- **12:** FuelLevelPWM

- **14:** SMBUS-SUI6

- **17:** Generator-Elec

- **19:** Rotoye

- **23:** Torqeedo

- **25:** Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage
- **26:** INA239_SPI
- **27:** EFI
- **28:** AD7091R5
- **29:** Scripting

---

## BATT5_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT5_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATT5_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATT5_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATT5_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATT5_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT5_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT5_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT5_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT5_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT5_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT5_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT5_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT5_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT5_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT5_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT5_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT5_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT5_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT5_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT5_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT5_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.

## BATT5_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.

Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.

**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.

- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## BATT6_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current
- **5:** Solo
- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic
- **8:** DroneCAN-BatteryInfo
- **9:** ESC
- **10:** Sum Of Selected Monitors
- **11:** FuelFlow
- **12:** FuelLevelPWM
- **13:** SMBUS-SUI3
- **14:** SMBUS-SUI6
- **15:** NeoDesign
- **16:** SMBus-Maxell
- **17:** Generator-Elec
- **18:** Generator-Fuel
- **19:** Rotoye
- **20:** MPPT
- **21:** INA2XX
- **22:** LTC2946
- **23:** Torqeedo
- **24:** FuelLevelAnalog
- **25:** Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage
- **26:** INA239_SPI
- **27:** EFI
- **28:** AD7091R5
- **29:** Scripting

---

## BATT6_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT6_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATT6_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATT6_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATT6_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATT6_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT6_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT6_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT6_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT6_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT6_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT6_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT6_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT6_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT6_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT6_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT6_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT6_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT6_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT6_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT6_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATT6_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATT6_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT6_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT6_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT6_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATT6_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATT6_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATT6_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATT6_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT6_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATT6_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATT6_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

## BATT6_FL_VLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Empty fuel level voltageÂ

**Description:**
The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 10:** volt

---

## BATT6_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATT6_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATT6_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT6_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT6_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT6_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT6_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATT6_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATT6_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT6_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATT6_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATT6_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

## BATT6_ESC_MASK
**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit

- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3

- **5:** ESC 6

- **7:** ESC 8

- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. ## BATT7_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

## BATT7_AMP_PERVLT
