Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.

## BATT7_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.

Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATT7_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATT7_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

## BATT7_FL_VLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Empty fuel level voltageÂ

**Description:**
The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 10:** volt

---

## BATT7_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATT7_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATT7_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT7_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT7_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT7_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT7_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATT7_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATT7_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT7_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATT7_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATT7_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit

- **1:** ESC 2

- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24

- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28

---

## BATT8_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current

- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic

---

## BATT8_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT8_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATT8_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATT8_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATT8_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATT8_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT8_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT8_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT8_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT8_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT8_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT8_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT8_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT8_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT8_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT8_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT8_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT8_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT8_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT8_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT8_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATT8_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATT8_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT8_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT8_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT8_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATT8_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATT8_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATT8_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATT8_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT8_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATT8_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

- **-1 to 1:** hertz

If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. ---

## BATT9_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATT9_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATT9_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT9_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATT9_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATT9_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATT9_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT9_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT9_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT9_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATT9_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATT9_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATT9_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATT9_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT9_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATT9_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATT9_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

## BATT9_FL_VLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Empty fuel level voltageÂ

**Description:**
The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 10:** volt

---

## BATT9_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATT9_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATT9_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT9_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT9_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT9_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT9_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATT9_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATT9_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT9_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATT9_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATT9_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit

- **1:** ESC 2

- **5:** ESC 6

- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29

- **31:** ESC 32

---

## BATTA_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning

- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current

- **6:** Bebop

---

## BATTA_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTA_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATTA_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATTA_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATTA_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATTA_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTA_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTA_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTA_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTA_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTA_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTA_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTA_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTA_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTA_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTA_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTA_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTA_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTA_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTA_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTA_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.

## BATTB_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current
- **5:** Solo
- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic
- **8:** DroneCAN-BatteryInfo
- **9:** ESC
- **10:** Sum Of Selected Monitors
- **11:** FuelFlow
- **12:** FuelLevelPWM
- **13:** SMBUS-SUI3
- **14:** SMBUS-SUI6
- **15:** NeoDesign
- **16:** SMBus-Maxell
- **17:** Generator-Elec
- **18:** Generator-Fuel
- **19:** Rotoye
- **20:** MPPT
- **21:** INA2XX
- **22:** LTC2946
- **23:** Torqeedo
- **24:** FuelLevelAnalog
- **25:** Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage
- **26:** INA239_SPI
- **27:** EFI
- **28:** AD7091R5
- **29:** Scripting

---

## BATTB_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTB_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATTB_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATTB_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATTB_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATTB_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTB_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTB_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTB_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTB_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTB_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTB_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTB_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTB_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTB_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTB_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTB_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTB_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTB_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTB_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTB_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATTB_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATTB_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTB_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTB_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTB_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATTB_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATTB_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATTB_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATTB_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATTB_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATTB_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATTB_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

## BATTB_FL_VLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Empty fuel level voltageÂ

**Description:**
The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 10:** volt

---

## BATTB_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATTB_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATTB_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTB_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTB_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTB_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTB_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATTB_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATTB_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATTB_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATTB_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATTB_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

## BATTB_ESC_MASK
**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit

- **0:** ESC 1
- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3
- **3:** ESC 4
- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8

- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## BATTC_OPTIONS

- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATTC_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

## BATTC_FL_VLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Empty fuel level voltageÂ

**Description:**
The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 10:** volt

---

## BATTC_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATTC_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATTC_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTC_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTC_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTC_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTC_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATTC_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATTC_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATTC_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATTC_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATTC_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

## BATTC_ESC_MASK
**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ESC 1
- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3
- **3:** ESC 4
- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8
- **8:** ESC 9
- **9:** ESC 10
- **10:** ESC 11
- **11:** ESC 12
- **12:** ESC 13
- **13:** ESC 14
- **14:** ESC 15
- **15:** ESC 16
- **16:** ESC 17
- **17:** ESC 18
- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## BATTD_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current
- **5:** Solo
- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic
- **8:** DroneCAN-BatteryInfo
- **9:** ESC
- **10:** Sum Of Selected Monitors
- **11:** FuelFlow
- **12:** FuelLevelPWM
- **13:** SMBUS-SUI3
- **14:** SMBUS-SUI6
- **15:** NeoDesign
- **16:** SMBus-Maxell
- **17:** Generator-Elec
- **18:** Generator-Fuel
- **19:** Rotoye
- **20:** MPPT
- **21:** INA2XX
- **22:** LTC2946
- **23:** Torqeedo
- **24:** FuelLevelAnalog
- **25:** Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage
- **26:** INA239_SPI
- **27:** EFI
- **28:** AD7091R5
- **29:** Scripting

---

## BATTD_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTD_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATTD_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATTD_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATTD_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATTD_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTD_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTD_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTD_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTD_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTD_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTD_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTD_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTD_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTD_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTD_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTD_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATTD_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATTD_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTD_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTD_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTD_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATTD_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATTD_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATTD_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATTD_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATTD_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATTD_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATTD_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

- **0.01 to 10:** volt

**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTE_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTE_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTE_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATTE_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATTE_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTE_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTE_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTE_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATTE_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATTE_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATTE_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATTE_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATTE_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATTE_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATTE_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

## BATTE_FL_VLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Empty fuel level voltageÂ

**Description:**
The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 10:** volt

---

## BATTE_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATTE_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATTE_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTE_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTE_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTE_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTE_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATTE_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATTE_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATTE_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATTE_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATTE_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

## BATTE_ESC_MASK
**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ESC 1
- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3
- **3:** ESC 4
- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8
