- **11:** ESC 12
- **12:** ESC 13
- **13:** ESC 14
- **14:** ESC 15
- **15:** ESC 16
- **16:** ESC 17
- **17:** ESC 18
- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## BATTF_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current
- **5:** Solo
- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic
- **8:** DroneCAN-BatteryInfo
- **9:** ESC
- **10:** Sum Of Selected Monitors
- **11:** FuelFlow
- **12:** FuelLevelPWM
- **13:** SMBUS-SUI3
- **14:** SMBUS-SUI6
- **15:** NeoDesign
- **16:** SMBus-Maxell
- **17:** Generator-Elec
- **18:** Generator-Fuel
- **19:** Rotoye
- **20:** MPPT
- **21:** INA2XX
- **22:** LTC2946
- **23:** Torqeedo
- **24:** FuelLevelAnalog
- **25:** Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage
- **26:** INA239_SPI
- **27:** EFI
- **28:** AD7091R5
- **29:** Scripting

---

## BATTF_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTF_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATTF_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATTF_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATTF_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATTF_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTF_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTF_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTF_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTF_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTF_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTF_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTF_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTF_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTF_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTF_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTF_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTF_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTF_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTF_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTF_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic
- **8:** DroneCAN-BatteryInfo
- **9:** ESC
- **10:** Sum Of Selected Monitors
- **11:** FuelFlow
- **12:** FuelLevelPWM
- **13:** SMBUS-SUI3
- **14:** SMBUS-SUI6
- **15:** NeoDesign
- **16:** SMBus-Maxell
- **17:** Generator-Elec
- **18:** Generator-Fuel
- **19:** Rotoye
- **20:** MPPT
- **21:** INA2XX
- **22:** LTC2946
- **23:** Torqeedo
- **24:** FuelLevelAnalog
- **25:** Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage
- **26:** INA239_SPI
- **27:** EFI
- **28:** AD7091R5
- **29:** Scripting

---

## BATTG_CAPACITY
**Display Name:** Battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTG_WATT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum allowed power (Watts)Â

**Description:**
If battery wattage (voltage * current) exceeds this value then the system will reduce max throttle (THR_MAX, TKOFF_THR_MAX and THR_MIN for reverse thrust) to satisfy this limit. This helps limit high current to low C rated batteries regardless of battery voltage. The max throttle will slowly grow back to THR_MAX (or TKOFF_THR_MAX ) and THR_MIN if demanding the current max and under the watt max. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** watt

---

## BATTG_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** Battery serial numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.


---

## BATTG_LOW_TIMER
**Display Name:** Low voltage timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## BATTG_FS_VOLTSRC
**Display Name:** Failsafe voltage sourceÂ

**Description:**
Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Raw Voltage1Sag Compensated Voltage:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Raw Voltage
- **1:** Sag Compensated Voltage

---

## BATTG_LOW_VOLT
**Display Name:** Low battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTG_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTG_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTG_LOW_MAH
**Display Name:** Low battery capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTG_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTG_CRT_VOLT
**Display Name:** Critical battery voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTG_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTG_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTG_CRT_MAH
**Display Name:** Battery critical capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTG_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTG_FS_LOW_ACT
**Display Name:** Low battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTG_FS_CRT_ACT
**Display Name:** Critical battery failsafe actionÂ

**Description:**
What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1RTL2Land3Terminate4QLand5Parachute6Loiter to QLand:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** RTL
- **2:** Land
- **3:** Terminate
- **4:** QLand
- **5:** Parachute
- **6:** Loiter to QLand

---

## BATTG_ARM_VOLT
**Display Name:** Required arming voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** volt

---

## BATTG_ARM_MAH
**Display Name:** Required arming remaining capacityÂ

**Description:**
Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTG_ARM_VOLT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **50:** milliampere hour

---

## BATTG_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

---

## BATTG_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BATTG_VOLT_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Voltage sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTG_CURR_PIN
**Display Name:** Battery Current sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled3Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM14CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator14Pixhawk2_PM215CubeOrange17Durandal101PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **3:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **4:** CubeOrange_PM2/Navigator
- **14:** Pixhawk2_PM2
- **15:** CubeOrange
- **17:** Durandal
- **101:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTG_VOLT_MULT
**Display Name:** Voltage MultiplierÂ

**Description:**
Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTG_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.


---

## BATTG_AMP_PERVLT
**Display Name:** Amps per voltÂ

**Description:**
Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.


---

## BATTG_AMP_OFFSET
**Display Name:** AMP offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.


---

## BATTG_VLT_OFFSET
**Display Name:** Voltage offsetÂ

**Description:**
Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.


---

## BATTG_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATTG_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_SMBus)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address


---

## BATTG_SUM_MASK
**Display Name:** Battery Sum maskÂ

**Description:**
0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0monitor 11monitor 22monitor 33monitor 44monitor 55monitor 66monitor 77monitor 88monitor 9:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** monitor 1
- **1:** monitor 2
- **2:** monitor 3
- **3:** monitor 4
- **4:** monitor 5
- **5:** monitor 6
- **6:** monitor 7
- **7:** monitor 8
- **8:** monitor 9

---

## BATTG_CURR_MULT
**Display Name:** Scales reported power monitor currentÂ

**Description:**
Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications


---

## BATTG_FL_VLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Empty fuel level voltageÂ

**Description:**
The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 10:** volt

---

## BATTG_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATTG_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATTG_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATTG_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTG_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTG_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATTG_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATTG_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATTG_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATTG_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATTG_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATTG_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

## BATTG_ESC_MASK
**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ESC 1
- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3
- **3:** ESC 4
- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8
- **8:** ESC 9
- **9:** ESC 10
- **10:** ESC 11
- **11:** ESC 12
- **12:** ESC 13
- **13:** ESC 14
- **14:** ESC 15
- **15:** ESC 16
- **16:** ESC 17
- **17:** ESC 18
- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## BATT_MONITOR
**Display Name:** Battery monitoringÂ

**Description:**
Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled3Analog Voltage Only4Analog Voltage and Current5Solo6Bebop7SMBus-Generic8DroneCAN-BatteryInfo9ESC10Sum Of Selected Monitors11FuelFlow12FuelLevelPWM13SMBUS-SUI314SMBUS-SUI615NeoDesign16SMBus-Maxell17Generator-Elec18Generator-Fuel19Rotoye20MPPT21INA2XX22LTC294623Torqeedo24FuelLevelAnalog25Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage26INA239_SPI27EFI28AD7091R529Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **3:** Analog Voltage Only
- **4:** Analog Voltage and Current
- **5:** Solo
- **6:** Bebop
- **7:** SMBus-Generic
- **8:** DroneCAN-BatteryInfo
- **9:** ESC
- **10:** Sum Of Selected Monitors
- **11:** FuelFlow
- **12:** FuelLevelPWM
- **13:** SMBUS-SUI3
- **14:** SMBUS-SUI6
- **15:** NeoDesign

- **17:** Generator-Elec
- **18:** Generator-Fuel

- **22:** LTC2946
- **23:** Torqeedo

- **25:** Synthetic Current and Analog Voltage
- **26:** INA239_SPI
- **27:** EFI
- **28:** AD7091R5
- **29:** Scripting

## BATT_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore DroneCAN SoC1MPPT reports input voltage and current2MPPT Powered off when disarmed3MPPT Powered on when armed4MPPT Powered off at boot5MPPT Powered on at boot6Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS7Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node8Battery is for internal autopilot use only9Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore DroneCAN SoC
- **1:** MPPT reports input voltage and current
- **2:** MPPT Powered off when disarmed
- **3:** MPPT Powered on when armed
- **4:** MPPT Powered off at boot
- **5:** MPPT Powered on at boot
- **6:** Send resistance compensated voltage to GCS
- **7:** Allow DroneCAN InfoAux to be from a different CAN node
- **8:** Battery is for internal autopilot use only
- **9:** Sum monitor measures minimum voltage instead of average

## BATT_FL_V_MULT
**Display Name:** Fuel level voltage multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.


---

## BATT_FL_FLTR
**Display Name:** Fuel level filter frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 1:** hertz

---

## BATT_FL_PIN
**Display Name:** Fuel level analog pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled2Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM15Navigator13Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM214CubeOrange16Durandal100PX4-v1:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **2:** Pixhawk/Pixracer/Navio2/Pixhawk2_PM1
- **5:** Navigator
- **13:** Pixhawk2_PM2/CubeOrange_PM2
- **14:** CubeOrange
- **16:** Durandal
- **100:** PX4-v1

---

## BATT_FL_FF
**Display Name:** First order termÂ

**Description:**
First order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT_FL_FS
**Display Name:** Second order termÂ

**Description:**
Second order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT_FL_FT
**Display Name:** Third order termÂ

**Description:**
Third order polynomial fit term


---

## BATT_FL_OFF
**Display Name:** Offset termÂ

**Description:**
Offset polynomial fit term


---

## BATT_MAX_VOLT
**Display Name:** Maximum Battery VoltageÂ

**Description:**
Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage


---

## BATT_I2C_BUS (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C bus numberÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C bus number


---

## BATT_I2C_ADDR (AP_BattMonitor_INA2xx)
**Display Name:** Battery monitor I2C addressÂ

**Description:**
Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses


---

## BATT_MAX_AMPS
**Display Name:** Battery monitor max currentÂ

**Description:**
This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 400:** ampere

---

## BATT_SHUNT
**Display Name:** Battery monitor shunt resistorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0001 to 0.01:** Ohm

---

## BATT_ESC_MASK
**Display Name:** ESC maskÂ

**Description:**
If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ESC 1
- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3
- **3:** ESC 4
- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8
- **8:** ESC 9
- **9:** ESC 10
- **10:** ESC 11
- **11:** ESC 12
- **12:** ESC 13
- **13:** ESC 14
- **14:** ESC 15
- **15:** ESC 16
- **16:** ESC 17
- **17:** ESC 18
- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## BRD_SER1_RTSCTS
**Display Name:** Serial 1 flow controlÂ

**Description:**
Enable flow control on serial 1 (telemetry 1). You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup. Note that the PX4v1 does not have hardware flow control pins on this port, so you should leave this disabled.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled2Auto3RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
- **2:** Auto
- **3:** RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin

---

## BRD_SER2_RTSCTS
**Display Name:** Serial 2 flow controlÂ

**Description:**
Enable flow control on serial 2 (telemetry 2). You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled2Auto3RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
- **2:** Auto
- **3:** RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin

---

**Display Name:** Serial 3 flow controlÂ

**Description:**
Enable flow control on serial 3. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled2Auto3RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
- **2:** Auto
- **3:** RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin

---

**Display Name:** Serial 4 flow controlÂ

**Description:**
Enable flow control on serial 4. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled2Auto3RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
- **2:** Auto
- **3:** RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin

---

**Display Name:** Serial 5 flow controlÂ

**Description:**
Enable flow control on serial 5. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled2Auto3RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
- **2:** Auto
- **3:** RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin

---

**Display Name:** Serial 6 flow controlÂ

**Description:**
Enable flow control on serial 6. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled2Auto3RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
- **2:** Auto
- **3:** RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin

---

## BRD_SER7_RTSCTS
**Display Name:** Serial 7 flow controlÂ

**Description:**
Enable flow control on serial 7. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled2Auto3RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
- **2:** Auto
- **3:** RS-485 Driver enable RTS pin

---

## BRD_SER8_RTSCTS
**Display Name:** Serial 8 flow controlÂ

**Description:**
Enable flow control on serial 8. You must have the RTS and CTS pins connected to your radio. The standard DF13 6 pin connector for a 3DR radio does have those pins connected. If this is set to 2 then flow control will be auto-detected by checking for the output buffer filling on startup.


---

## BRD_SAFETY_DEFLT
**Display Name:** Sets default state of the safety switchÂ

**Description:**
This controls the default state of the safety switch at startup. When set to 1 the safety switch will start in the safe state (flashing) at boot. When set to zero the safety switch will start in the unsafe state (solid) at startup. Note that if a safety switch is fitted the user can still control the safety state after startup using the switch. The safety state can also be controlled in software using a MAVLink message.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## BRD_SBUS_OUT
**Display Name:** SBUS output rateÂ

**Description:**
This sets the SBUS output frame rate in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled150Hz275Hz3100Hz4150Hz5200Hz6250Hz7300Hz:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** 50Hz
- **2:** 75Hz
- **3:** 100Hz
- **4:** 150Hz
- **5:** 200Hz
- **6:** 250Hz
- **7:** 300Hz

---

## BRD_SERIAL_NUM
**Display Name:** User-defined serial numberÂ

**Description:**
User-defined serial number of this vehicle, it can be any arbitrary number you want and has no effect on the autopilot


---

## BRD_SAFETY_MASK
**Display Name:** Outputs which ignore the safety switch stateÂ

**Description:**
A bitmask which controls what outputs can move while the safety switch has not been pressed

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Output11Output22Output33Output44Output55Output66Output77Output88Output99Output1010Output1111Output1212Output1313Output14:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Output1
- **1:** Output2
- **2:** Output3
- **3:** Output4
- **4:** Output5
- **5:** Output6
- **6:** Output7
- **7:** Output8
- **8:** Output9
- **9:** Output10
- **10:** Output11
- **11:** Output12
- **12:** Output13
- **13:** Output14

---

## BRD_HEAT_TARG
**Display Name:** Board heater temperature targetÂ

**Description:**
Board heater target temperature for boards with controllable heating units. Set to -1 to disable the heater, please reboot after setting to -1.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 80:** degrees Celsius

---

## BRD_TYPE
**Display Name:** Board typeÂ

**Description:**
This allows selection of a PX4 or VRBRAIN board type. If set to zero then the board type is auto-detected (PX4)

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0AUTO1PX4V12Pixhawk3Cube/Pixhawk24Pixracer5PixhawkMini6Pixhawk2Slim13Intel Aero FC14Pixhawk Pro20AUAV2.121PCNC122MINDPXV223SP0124CUAVv5/FMUV530VRX BRAIN5132VRX BRAIN5233VRX BRAIN52E34VRX UBRAIN5135VRX UBRAIN5236VRX CORE1038VRX BRAIN5439PX4 FMUV6100PX4 OLDDRIVERS:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** AUTO
- **1:** PX4V1
- **2:** Pixhawk
- **3:** Cube/Pixhawk2
- **4:** Pixracer
- **5:** PixhawkMini
- **6:** Pixhawk2Slim
- **13:** Intel Aero FC
- **14:** Pixhawk Pro
- **20:** AUAV2.1
- **21:** PCNC1
- **22:** MINDPXV2
- **23:** SP01
- **24:** CUAVv5/FMUV5
- **30:** VRX BRAIN51
- **32:** VRX BRAIN52
- **33:** VRX BRAIN52E
- **34:** VRX UBRAIN51
- **35:** VRX UBRAIN52
- **36:** VRX CORE10
- **38:** VRX BRAIN54
- **39:** PX4 FMUV6
- **100:** PX4 OLDDRIVERS

---

## BRD_IO_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable IO co-processorÂ

**Description:**
This allows for the IO co-processor on boards with an IOMCU to be disabled. Setting to 2 will enable the IOMCU but not attempt to update firmware on startup

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled2EnableNoFWUpdate:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
- **2:** EnableNoFWUpdate

---

## BRD_SAFETYOPTION
**Display Name:** Options for safety button behaviorÂ

**Description:**
This controls the activation of the safety button. It allows you to control if the safety button can be used for safety enable and/or disable, and whether the button is only active when disarmed

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ActiveForSafetyDisable1ActiveForSafetyEnable2ActiveWhenArmed3Force safety on when the aircraft disarms:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ActiveForSafetyDisable
- **1:** ActiveForSafetyEnable
- **2:** ActiveWhenArmed
- **3:** Force safety on when the aircraft disarms

---

## BRD_VBUS_MIN
**Display Name:** Autopilot board voltage requirementÂ

**Description:**
Minimum voltage on the autopilot power rail to allow the aircraft to arm. 0 to disable the check.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 4.0 to 5.5

---

## BRD_VSERVO_MIN
**Display Name:** Servo voltage requirementÂ

**Description:**
Minimum voltage on the servo rail to allow the aircraft to arm. 0 to disable the check.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 3.3 to 12.0

---

## BRD_SD_SLOWDOWN
**Display Name:** microSD slowdownÂ

**Description:**
This is a scaling factor to slow down microSD operation. It can be used on flight board and microSD card combinations where full speed is not reliable. For normal full speed operation a value of 0 should be used.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 32

---

## BRD_PWM_VOLT_SEL
**Display Name:** Set PWM Out VoltageÂ

**Description:**
This sets the voltage max for PWM output pulses. 0 for 3.3V and 1 for 5V output. On boards with an IOMCU that support this parameter this option only affects the 8 main outputs, not the 6 auxiliary outputs. Using 5V output can help to reduce the impact of ESC noise interference corrupting signals to the ESCs.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning03.3V15V:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** 3.3V
- **1:** 5V

---

## BRD_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Board optionsÂ

**Description:**
Board specific option flags

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Enable hardware watchdog1Disable MAVftp2Enable set of internal parameters3Enable Debug Pins4Unlock flash on reboot5Write protect firmware flash on reboot6Write protect bootloader flash on reboot7Skip board validation8Disable board arming gpio output change on arm/disarm:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Enable hardware watchdog
- **1:** Disable MAVftp
- **2:** Enable set of internal parameters
- **3:** Enable Debug Pins
- **4:** Unlock flash on reboot
- **5:** Write protect firmware flash on reboot
- **6:** Write protect bootloader flash on reboot
- **7:** Skip board validation
- **8:** Disable board arming gpio output change on arm/disarm

---

## BRD_BOOT_DELAY
**Display Name:** Boot delayÂ

**Description:**
This adds a delay in milliseconds to boot to ensure peripherals initialise fully

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 10000:** milliseconds

---

## BRD_HEAT_P
**Display Name:** Board Heater P gainÂ

**Description:**
Board Heater P gain

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 500

---

## BRD_HEAT_I
**Display Name:** Board Heater I gainÂ

**Description:**
Board Heater integrator gain

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0 to 1

---

## BRD_HEAT_IMAX
**Display Name:** Board Heater IMAXÂ

**Description:**
Board Heater integrator maximum

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 100

---

## BRD_ALT_CONFIG
**Display Name:** Alternative HW configÂ

**Description:**
Select an alternative hardware configuration. A value of zero selects the default configuration for this board. Other values are board specific. Please see the documentation for your board for details on any alternative configuration values that may be available.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## BRD_HEAT_LOWMGN
**Display Name:** Board heater temp lower marginÂ

**Description:**
Arming check will fail if temp is lower than this margin below BRD_HEAT_TARG. 0 disables the low temperature check

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 20:** degrees Celsius

---

## BRD_SD_MISSION
**Display Name:** SDCard Mission sizeÂ

**Description:**
This sets the amount of storage in kilobytes reserved on the microsd card in mission.stg for waypoint storage. Each waypoint uses 15 bytes.


---

## BRD_SD_FENCE
**Display Name:** SDCard Fence sizeÂ

**Description:**
This sets the amount of storage in kilobytes reserved on the microsd card in fence.stg for fence storage.


---

## BRD_IO_DSHOT
**Display Name:** Load DShot FW on IOÂ

**Description:**
This loads the DShot firmware on the IO co-processor

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0StandardFW1DshotFW:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** StandardFW
- **1:** DshotFW

---

## BRD_RADIO_TYPE
**Display Name:** Set type of direct attached radioÂ

**Description:**
This enables support for direct attached radio receivers

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1CYRF69362CC25003BK2425:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** CYRF6936
- **2:** CC2500
- **3:** BK2425

---

## BRD_RADIO_PROT
**Display Name:** protocolÂ

**Description:**
Select air protocol

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Auto1DSM22DSMX:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Auto
- **1:** DSM2
- **2:** DSMX

---

## BRD_RADIO_DEBUG
**Display Name:** debug levelÂ

**Description:**
radio debug level


---

## BRD_RADIO_DISCRC
**Display Name:** disable receive CRCÂ

**Description:**
disable receive CRC (for debug)

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0NotDisabled1Disabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** NotDisabled
- **1:** Disabled

---

## BRD_RADIO_SIGCH
**Display Name:** RSSI signal strengthÂ

**Description:**
Channel to show receive RSSI signal strength, or zero for disabled


---

## BRD_RADIO_PPSCH
**Display Name:** Packet rate channelÂ

**Description:**
Channel to show received packet-per-second rate, or zero for disabled


---

## BRD_RADIO_TELEM
**Display Name:** Enable telemetryÂ

**Description:**
If this is non-zero then telemetry packets will be sent over DSM

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## BRD_RADIO_TXPOW
**Display Name:** Telemetry Transmit powerÂ

**Description:**
Set telemetry transmit power. This is the power level (from 1 to 8) for telemetry packets sent from the RX to the TX


---

## BRD_RADIO_FCCTST
**Display Name:** Put radio into FCC test modeÂ

**Description:**
If this is enabled then the radio will continuously transmit as required for FCC testing. The transmit channel is set by the value of the parameter. The radio will not work for RC input while this is enabled

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1MinChannel2MidChannel3MaxChannel4MinChannelCW5MidChannelCW6MaxChannelCW:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** MinChannel
- **2:** MidChannel
- **3:** MaxChannel
- **4:** MinChannelCW
- **5:** MidChannelCW
- **6:** MaxChannelCW

---

## BRD_RADIO_STKMD
**Display Name:** Stick input modeÂ

**Description:**
This selects between different stick input modes. The default is mode2, which has throttle on the left stick and pitch on the right stick. You can instead set mode1, which has throttle on the right stick and pitch on the left stick.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning1Mode12Mode2:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **1:** Mode1
- **2:** Mode2

---

## BRD_RADIO_TESTCH
**Display Name:** Set radio to factory test channelÂ

**Description:**
This sets the radio to a fixed test channel for factory testing. Using a fixed channel avoids the need for binding in factory testing.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1TestChan12TestChan23TestChan34TestChan45TestChan56TestChan67TestChan78TestChan8:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** TestChan1
- **2:** TestChan2
- **3:** TestChan3
- **4:** TestChan4
- **5:** TestChan5
- **6:** TestChan6
- **7:** TestChan7
- **8:** TestChan8

---

## BRD_RADIO_TSIGCH
**Display Name:** RSSI value channel for telemetry data on transmitterÂ

**Description:**
Channel to show telemetry RSSI value as received by TX


---

## BRD_RADIO_TPPSCH
**Display Name:** Telemetry PPS channelÂ

**Description:**
Channel to show telemetry packets-per-second value, as received at TX


---

## BRD_RADIO_TXMAX
**Display Name:** Transmitter transmit powerÂ

**Description:**
Set transmitter maximum transmit power (from 1 to 8)


---

## BRD_RADIO_BZOFS
**Display Name:** Transmitter buzzer adjustmentÂ

**Description:**
Set transmitter buzzer note adjustment (adjust frequency up)


---

## BRD_RADIO_ABTIME
**Display Name:** Auto-bind timeÂ

**Description:**
When non-zero this sets the time with no transmitter packets before we start looking for auto-bind packets.


---

## BRD_RADIO_ABLVL
**Display Name:** Auto-bind levelÂ

**Description:**
This sets the minimum RSSI of an auto-bind packet for it to be accepted. This should be set so that auto-bind will only happen at short range to minimise the change of an auto-bind happening accidentially


---

## BRD_RTC_TYPES
**Display Name:** Allowed sources of RTC timeÂ

**Description:**
Specifies which sources of UTC time will be accepted

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0GPS1MAVLINK_SYSTEM_TIME2HW:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** GPS
- **1:** MAVLINK_SYSTEM_TIME
- **2:** HW

---

## BRD_RTC_TZ_MIN
**Display Name:** Timezone offset from UTCÂ

**Description:**
Adds offset in +- minutes from UTC to calculate local time


---

## BTN_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable button reportingÂ

**Description:**
This enables the button checking module. When this is disabled the parameters for setting button inputs are not visible

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## BTN_PIN1
**Display Name:** First button PinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin number for first button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled50AUXOUT151AUXOUT252AUXOUT353AUXOUT454AUXOUT555AUXOUT6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **50:** AUXOUT1
- **51:** AUXOUT2
- **52:** AUXOUT3
- **53:** AUXOUT4
- **54:** AUXOUT5
- **55:** AUXOUT6

---

## BTN_PIN2
**Display Name:** Second button PinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin number for second button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled50AUXOUT151AUXOUT252AUXOUT353AUXOUT454AUXOUT555AUXOUT6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **50:** AUXOUT1
- **51:** AUXOUT2
- **52:** AUXOUT3
- **53:** AUXOUT4
- **54:** AUXOUT5
- **55:** AUXOUT6

---

## BTN_PIN3
**Display Name:** Third button PinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin number for third button input.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled50AUXOUT151AUXOUT252AUXOUT353AUXOUT454AUXOUT555AUXOUT6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **50:** AUXOUT1
- **51:** AUXOUT2
- **52:** AUXOUT3
- **53:** AUXOUT4
- **54:** AUXOUT5
- **55:** AUXOUT6

---

## BTN_PIN4
**Display Name:** Fourth button PinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin number for fourth button input. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled50AUXOUT151AUXOUT252AUXOUT353AUXOUT454AUXOUT555AUXOUT6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **50:** AUXOUT1
- **51:** AUXOUT2
- **52:** AUXOUT3
- **53:** AUXOUT4
- **54:** AUXOUT5
- **55:** AUXOUT6

---

## BTN_REPORT_SEND
**Display Name:** Report send timeÂ

**Description:**
The duration in seconds that a BUTTON_CHANGE report is repeatedly sent to the GCS regarding a button changing state. Note that the BUTTON_CHANGE message is MAVLink2 only.


---

## BTN_OPTIONS1
**Display Name:** Button Pin 1 OptionsÂ

**Description:**
Options for Pin 1. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0PWM Input1InvertInput:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** PWM Input
- **1:** InvertInput

---

## BTN_OPTIONS2
**Display Name:** Button Pin 2 OptionsÂ

**Description:**
Options for Pin 2. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0PWM Input1InvertInput:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** PWM Input
- **1:** InvertInput

---

## BTN_OPTIONS3
**Display Name:** Button Pin 3 OptionsÂ

**Description:**
Options for Pin 3. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0PWM Input1InvertInput:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** PWM Input
- **1:** InvertInput

---

## BTN_OPTIONS4
**Display Name:** Button Pin 4 OptionsÂ

**Description:**
Options for Pin 4. PWM input detects PWM above or below 1800/1200us instead of logic level. If PWM is not detected or is less than 800us or above 2200us the button will interpreted as low. Invert changes HIGH state to be logic low voltage on pin, or below 1200us, if PWM input.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0PWM Input1InvertInput:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** PWM Input
- **1:** InvertInput

---

## CAM_MAX_ROLL
**Display Name:** Maximum photo roll angle.Â

**Description:**
Postpone shooting if roll is greater than limit. (0=Disable, will shoot regardless of roll).

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 180:** degrees

---

## CAM_AUTO_ONLY
**Display Name:** Distance-trigging in AUTO mode onlyÂ

**Description:**
When enabled, trigging by distance is done in AUTO mode only.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Always1Only when in AUTO:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Always
- **1:** Only when in AUTO

---

## CAM1_TYPE
**Display Name:** Camera shutter (trigger) typeÂ

**Description:**
how to trigger the camera to take a picture

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Servo2Relay3GoPro in Solo Gimbal4Mount (Siyi/Topotek/Viewpro/Xacti)5MAVLink6MAVLinkCamV27Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Servo
- **2:** Relay
- **3:** GoPro in Solo Gimbal
- **4:** Mount (Siyi/Topotek/Viewpro/Xacti)
- **5:** MAVLink
- **6:** MAVLinkCamV2
- **7:** Scripting

---

## CAM1_DURATION
**Display Name:** Camera shutter duration held openÂ

**Description:**
Duration in seconds that the camera shutter is held open

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 5:** seconds

---

## CAM1_SERVO_ON
**Display Name:** Camera servo ON PWM valueÂ

**Description:**
PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is activated

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1000 to 2000:** PWM in microseconds

---

## CAM1_SERVO_OFF
**Display Name:** Camera servo OFF PWM valueÂ

**Description:**
PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is deactivated

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1000 to 2000:** PWM in microseconds

---

## CAM1_TRIGG_DIST
**Display Name:** Camera trigger distanceÂ

**Description:**
Distance in meters between camera triggers. If this value is non-zero then the camera will trigger whenever the position changes by this number of meters regardless of what mode the APM is in. Note that this parameter can also be set in an auto mission using the DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_DIST command, allowing you to enable/disable the triggering of the camera during the flight.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 1000:** meters

---

## CAM1_RELAY_ON
**Display Name:** Camera relay ON valueÂ

**Description:**
This sets whether the relay goes high or low when it triggers. Note that you should also set RELAY_DEFAULT appropriately for your camera

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Low1High:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Low
- **1:** High

---

## CAM1_INTRVAL_MIN
**Display Name:** Camera minimum time interval between photosÂ

**Description:**
Postpone shooting if previous picture was taken less than this many seconds ago

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 10:** seconds

---

## CAM1_FEEDBAK_PIN
**Display Name:** Camera feedback pinÂ

**Description:**
pin number to use for save accurate camera feedback messages. If set to -1 then don't use a pin flag for this, otherwise this is a pin number which if held high after a picture trigger order, will save camera messages when camera really takes a picture. A universal camera hot shoe is needed. The pin should be held high for at least 2 milliseconds for reliable trigger detection.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot. See also the CAMx_FEEDBCK_POL option.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6

---

## CAM1_FEEDBAK_POL
**Display Name:** Camera feedback pin polarityÂ

**Description:**
Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the feedback pin should go high on trigger. If set to 0 then it should go low

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0TriggerLow1TriggerHigh:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** TriggerLow
- **1:** TriggerHigh

---

## CAM1_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Camera optionsÂ

**Description:**
Camera options bitmask

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming

---

## CAM1_MNT_INST
**Display Name:** Camera Mount instanceÂ

**Description:**
Mount instance camera is associated with. 0 means camera and mount have identical instance numbers e.g. camera1 and mount1


---

## CAM1_HFOV
**Display Name:** Camera horizontal field of viewÂ

**Description:**
Camera horizontal field of view. 0 if unknown

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 360:** degrees

---

## CAM1_VFOV
**Display Name:** Camera vertical field of viewÂ

**Description:**
Camera vertical field of view. 0 if unknown

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 180:** degrees

---

## CAM2_TYPE
**Display Name:** Camera shutter (trigger) typeÂ

**Description:**
how to trigger the camera to take a picture

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Servo2Relay3GoPro in Solo Gimbal4Mount (Siyi/Topotek/Viewpro/Xacti)5MAVLink6MAVLinkCamV27Scripting:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Servo
- **2:** Relay
- **3:** GoPro in Solo Gimbal
- **4:** Mount (Siyi/Topotek/Viewpro/Xacti)
- **5:** MAVLink
- **6:** MAVLinkCamV2
- **7:** Scripting

---

## CAM2_DURATION
**Display Name:** Camera shutter duration held openÂ

**Description:**
Duration in seconds that the camera shutter is held open

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 5:** seconds

---

## CAM2_SERVO_ON
**Display Name:** Camera servo ON PWM valueÂ

**Description:**
PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is activated

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1000 to 2000:** PWM in microseconds

---

## CAM2_SERVO_OFF
**Display Name:** Camera servo OFF PWM valueÂ

**Description:**
PWM value in microseconds to move servo to when shutter is deactivated

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1000 to 2000:** PWM in microseconds

---

## CAM2_TRIGG_DIST
**Display Name:** Camera trigger distanceÂ

**Description:**
Distance in meters between camera triggers. If this value is non-zero then the camera will trigger whenever the position changes by this number of meters regardless of what mode the APM is in. Note that this parameter can also be set in an auto mission using the DO_SET_CAM_TRIGG_DIST command, allowing you to enable/disable the triggering of the camera during the flight.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 1000:** meters

---

## CAM2_RELAY_ON
**Display Name:** Camera relay ON valueÂ

**Description:**
This sets whether the relay goes high or low when it triggers. Note that you should also set RELAY_DEFAULT appropriately for your camera

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Low1High:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Low
- **1:** High

---

## CAM2_INTRVAL_MIN
**Display Name:** Camera minimum time interval between photosÂ

**Description:**
Postpone shooting if previous picture was taken less than this many seconds ago

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 10:** seconds

---

## CAM2_FEEDBAK_PIN
**Display Name:** Camera feedback pinÂ

**Description:**
pin number to use for save accurate camera feedback messages. If set to -1 then don't use a pin flag for this, otherwise this is a pin number which if held high after a picture trigger order, will save camera messages when camera really takes a picture. A universal camera hot shoe is needed. The pin should be held high for at least 2 milliseconds for reliable trigger detection.  Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot. See also the CAMx_FEEDBCK_POL option.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6

---

## CAM2_FEEDBAK_POL
**Display Name:** Camera feedback pin polarityÂ

**Description:**
Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the feedback pin should go high on trigger. If set to 0 then it should go low

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0TriggerLow1TriggerHigh:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** TriggerLow
- **1:** TriggerHigh

---

## CAM2_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Camera optionsÂ

**Description:**
Camera options bitmask

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Recording Starts at arming and stops at disarming

---

## CAM2_MNT_INST
**Display Name:** Camera Mount instanceÂ

**Description:**
Mount instance camera is associated with. 0 means camera and mount have identical instance numbers e.g. camera1 and mount1


---

## CAM2_HFOV
**Display Name:** Camera horizontal field of viewÂ

**Description:**
Camera horizontal field of view. 0 if unknown

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 360:** degrees

---

## CAM2_VFOV
**Display Name:** Camera vertical field of viewÂ

**Description:**
Camera vertical field of view. 0 if unknown

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 180:** degrees

---

## CAM_RC_TYPE
**Display Name:** RunCam device typeÂ

**Description:**
RunCam device type used to determine OSD menu structure and shutter options.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1RunCam Split Micro/RunCam with UART2RunCam Split3RunCam Split4 4k4RunCam Hybrid/RunCam Thumb Pro5Runcam 2 4k:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** RunCam Split Micro/RunCam with UART
- **2:** RunCam Split
- **3:** RunCam Split4 4k
- **4:** RunCam Hybrid/RunCam Thumb Pro
- **5:** Runcam 2 4k

---

## CAM_RC_FEATURES
**Display Name:** RunCam features availableÂ

**Description:**
The available features of the attached RunCam device. If 0 then the RunCam device will be queried for the features it supports, otherwise this setting is used.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Power Button1WiFi Button2Change Mode35-Key OSD4Settings Access5DisplayPort6Start Recording7Stop Recording:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Power Button
- **1:** WiFi Button
- **2:** Change Mode
- **3:** 5-Key OSD
- **4:** Settings Access
- **5:** DisplayPort
- **6:** Start Recording
- **7:** Stop Recording

---

## CAM_RC_BT_DELAY
**Display Name:** RunCam boot delay before allowing updatesÂ

**Description:**
Time it takes for the RunCam to become fully ready in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.


---

## CAM_RC_BTN_DELAY
**Display Name:** RunCam button delay before allowing further button pressesÂ

**Description:**
Time it takes for the a RunCam button press to be actived in ms. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.


---

## CAM_RC_MDE_DELAY
**Display Name:** RunCam mode delay before allowing further button pressesÂ

**Description:**
Time it takes for the a RunCam mode button press to be actived in ms. If a mode change first requires a video recording change then double this value is used. If this is too short then commands can get out of sync.


---

## CAM_RC_CONTROL
**Display Name:** RunCam control optionÂ

**Description:**
Specifies the allowed actions required to enter the OSD menu and other option like autorecording

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Stick yaw right1Stick roll right23-position switch32-position switch4Autorecording enabled:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Stick yaw right
- **1:** Stick roll right
- **2:** 3-position switch
- **3:** 2-position switch
- **4:** Autorecording enabled

---

## CAN_LOGLEVEL
**Display Name:** LoglevelÂ

**Description:**
Loglevel for recording initialisation and debug information from CAN Interface

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Values
- **0 to 4:** ValueMeaning0Log None1Log Error2Log Warning and below3Log Info and below4Log Everything
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Log None
- **1:** Log Error
- **2:** Log Warning and below
- **3:** Log Info and below
- **4:** Log Everything

---

## CAN_D1_PROTOCOL
**Display Name:** Enable use of specific protocol over virtual driverÂ

**Description:**
Enabling this option starts selected protocol that will use this virtual driver

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1DroneCAN4PiccoloCAN6EFI_NWPMU7USD18KDECAN10Scripting11Benewake12Scripting213TOFSenseP14RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon):** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** DroneCAN
- **4:** PiccoloCAN
- **6:** EFI_NWPMU
- **7:** USD1
- **8:** KDECAN
- **10:** Scripting
- **11:** Benewake
- **12:** Scripting2
- **13:** TOFSenseP
- **14:** RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon)

---

## CAN_D1_PROTOCOL2
**Display Name:** Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressingÂ

**Description:**
Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled7USD110Scripting11Benewake12Scripting213TOFSenseP14RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon):** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **7:** USD1
- **10:** Scripting
- **11:** Benewake
- **12:** Scripting2
- **13:** TOFSenseP
- **14:** RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon)

---

## CAN_D1_UC_OPTION
**Display Name:** DroneCAN optionsÂ

**Description:**
Option flags

- **1:** IgnoreDNANodeConflicts
- **2:** EnableCanfd

## CAN_D1_UC_NTF_RT
**Display Name:** Notify State rateÂ

**Description:**
Maximum transmit rate for Notify State Message

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 200:** hertz

---

## CAN_D1_UC_ESC_OF
**Display Name:** ESC Output channels offsetÂ

**Description:**
Offset for ESC numbering in DroneCAN ESC RawCommand messages. This allows for more efficient packing of ESC command messages. If your ESCs are on servo functions 5 to 8 and you set this parameter to 4 then the ESC RawCommand will be sent with the first 4 slots filled. This can be used for more efficient usage of CAN bandwidth


---

## CAN_D1_UC_POOL
**Display Name:** CAN pool sizeÂ

**Description:**
Amount of memory in bytes to allocate for the DroneCAN memory pool. More memory is needed for higher CAN bus loads


---

## CAN_D1_UC_ESC_RV
**Display Name:** Bitmask for output channels for reversible ESCs over DroneCAN.Â

**Description:**
Bitmask with one set for each output channel that uses a reversible ESC over DroneCAN. Reversible ESCs use both positive and negative values in RawCommands, with positive commanding the forward direction and negative commanding the reverse direction.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit

- **1:** ESC 2

- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8

## CAN_D1_UC_RLY_RT
**Display Name:** DroneCAN relay output rateÂ

**Description:**
Maximum transmit rate for relay outputs, note that this rate is per message each message does 1 relay, so if with more relays will take longer to update at the same rate, a extra message will be sent when a relay changes state

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 200:** hertz

---

- **1:** Enabled

## CAN_D2_PROTOCOL
**Display Name:** Enable use of specific protocol over virtual driverÂ

**Description:**
Enabling this option starts selected protocol that will use this virtual driver

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1DroneCAN4PiccoloCAN6EFI_NWPMU7USD18KDECAN10Scripting11Benewake12Scripting213TOFSenseP14RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon):** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** DroneCAN
- **4:** PiccoloCAN
- **6:** EFI_NWPMU
- **7:** USD1
- **8:** KDECAN
- **10:** Scripting
- **11:** Benewake
- **12:** Scripting2
- **13:** TOFSenseP
- **14:** RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon)

---

## CAN_D2_PROTOCOL2
**Display Name:** Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressingÂ

**Description:**
Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled7USD110Scripting11Benewake12Scripting213TOFSenseP14RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon):** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **7:** USD1
- **10:** Scripting
- **11:** Benewake
- **12:** Scripting2
- **13:** TOFSenseP
- **14:** RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon)

---

## CAN_D2_PC_ECU_ID
**Display Name:** ECU Node IDÂ

**Description:**
Node ID to send ECU throttle messages to. Set to zero to disable ECU throttle messages. Set to 255 to broadcast to all ECUs.


---

## CAN_D2_PC_ECU_RT
**Display Name:** ECU command output rateÂ

**Description:**
Output rate of ECU command messages

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 500:** hertz

---

**Display Name:** Own node IDÂ

**Description:**
DroneCAN node ID used by the driver itself on this network


---

Maximum transmit rate for servo outputs

**Specifications:**

---

## CAN_D2_UC_OPTION
**Display Name:** DroneCAN optionsÂ

**Description:**
Option flags

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ClearDNADatabase1IgnoreDNANodeConflicts2EnableCanfd3IgnoreDNANodeUnhealthy4SendServoAsPWM5SendGNSS6UseHimarkServo7HobbyWingESC8EnableStats9EnableFlexDebug:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ClearDNADatabase
- **1:** IgnoreDNANodeConflicts
- **2:** EnableCanfd
- **3:** IgnoreDNANodeUnhealthy
- **4:** SendServoAsPWM
- **5:** SendGNSS
- **6:** UseHimarkServo
- **7:** HobbyWingESC
- **8:** EnableStats
- **9:** EnableFlexDebug

---

## CAN_D2_UC_NTF_RT
**Display Name:** Notify State rateÂ

**Description:**
Maximum transmit rate for Notify State Message

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 200:** hertz

---

## CAN_D2_UC_ESC_OF
**Display Name:** ESC Output channels offsetÂ

**Description:**
Offset for ESC numbering in DroneCAN ESC RawCommand messages. This allows for more efficient packing of ESC command messages. If your ESCs are on servo functions 5 to 8 and you set this parameter to 4 then the ESC RawCommand will be sent with the first 4 slots filled. This can be used for more efficient usage of CAN bandwidth


---

## CAN_D2_UC_POOL
**Display Name:** CAN pool sizeÂ

**Description:**
Amount of memory in bytes to allocate for the DroneCAN memory pool. More memory is needed for higher CAN bus loads


---

## CAN_D2_UC_ESC_RV
**Display Name:** Bitmask for output channels for reversible ESCs over DroneCAN.Â

**Description:**
Bitmask with one set for each output channel that uses a reversible ESC over DroneCAN. Reversible ESCs use both positive and negative values in RawCommands, with positive commanding the forward direction and negative commanding the reverse direction.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ESC 1
- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3
- **3:** ESC 4
- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8
- **8:** ESC 9
- **9:** ESC 10
- **10:** ESC 11
- **11:** ESC 12
- **12:** ESC 13
- **13:** ESC 14
- **14:** ESC 15
- **15:** ESC 16
- **16:** ESC 17
- **17:** ESC 18
- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## CAN_D2_UC_RLY_RT
**Display Name:** DroneCAN relay output rateÂ

**Description:**
Maximum transmit rate for relay outputs, note that this rate is per message each message does 1 relay, so if with more relays will take longer to update at the same rate, a extra message will be sent when a relay changes state

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 200:** hertz

---

## CAN_D2_UC_SER_EN
**Display Name:** DroneCAN Serial enableÂ

**Description:**
Enable DroneCAN virtual serial ports

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

## CAN_D3_PROTOCOL
**Display Name:** Enable use of specific protocol over virtual driverÂ

**Description:**
Enabling this option starts selected protocol that will use this virtual driver

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1DroneCAN4PiccoloCAN6EFI_NWPMU7USD18KDECAN10Scripting11Benewake12Scripting213TOFSenseP14RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon):** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** DroneCAN
- **4:** PiccoloCAN
- **6:** EFI_NWPMU
- **7:** USD1
- **8:** KDECAN
- **10:** Scripting
- **11:** Benewake
- **12:** Scripting2
- **13:** TOFSenseP
- **14:** RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon)

---

## CAN_D3_PROTOCOL2
**Display Name:** Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressingÂ

**Description:**
Secondary protocol with 11 bit CAN addressing

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled7USD110Scripting11Benewake12Scripting213TOFSenseP14RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon):** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **7:** USD1
- **10:** Scripting
- **11:** Benewake
- **12:** Scripting2
- **13:** TOFSenseP
- **14:** RadarCAN (NanoRadar/Hexsoon)

---

## CAN_D3_PC_ECU_ID
**Display Name:** ECU Node IDÂ

**Description:**
Node ID to send ECU throttle messages to. Set to zero to disable ECU throttle messages. Set to 255 to broadcast to all ECUs.


---

**Display Name:** ECU command output rateÂ

**Description:**
Output rate of ECU command messages

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 500:** hertz

---

**Display Name:** Own node IDÂ

**Description:**
DroneCAN node ID used by the driver itself on this network


---

## CAN_D3_UC_OPTION
**Display Name:** DroneCAN optionsÂ

**Description:**
Option flags

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ClearDNADatabase1IgnoreDNANodeConflicts2EnableCanfd3IgnoreDNANodeUnhealthy4SendServoAsPWM5SendGNSS6UseHimarkServo7HobbyWingESC8EnableStats9EnableFlexDebug:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ClearDNADatabase
- **1:** IgnoreDNANodeConflicts
- **2:** EnableCanfd
- **3:** IgnoreDNANodeUnhealthy
- **4:** SendServoAsPWM
- **5:** SendGNSS
- **6:** UseHimarkServo
- **7:** HobbyWingESC
- **8:** EnableStats
- **9:** EnableFlexDebug

---

## CAN_D3_UC_NTF_RT
**Display Name:** Notify State rateÂ

**Description:**
Maximum transmit rate for Notify State Message

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 200:** hertz

---

## CAN_D3_UC_ESC_OF
**Display Name:** ESC Output channels offsetÂ

**Description:**
Offset for ESC numbering in DroneCAN ESC RawCommand messages. This allows for more efficient packing of ESC command messages. If your ESCs are on servo functions 5 to 8 and you set this parameter to 4 then the ESC RawCommand will be sent with the first 4 slots filled. This can be used for more efficient usage of CAN bandwidth


---

## CAN_D3_UC_POOL
**Display Name:** CAN pool sizeÂ

**Description:**
Amount of memory in bytes to allocate for the DroneCAN memory pool. More memory is needed for higher CAN bus loads


---

## CAN_D3_UC_ESC_RV
**Display Name:** Bitmask for output channels for reversible ESCs over DroneCAN.Â

**Description:**
Bitmask with one set for each output channel that uses a reversible ESC over DroneCAN. Reversible ESCs use both positive and negative values in RawCommands, with positive commanding the forward direction and negative commanding the reverse direction.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ESC 11ESC 22ESC 33ESC 44ESC 55ESC 66ESC 77ESC 88ESC 99ESC 1010ESC 1111ESC 1212ESC 1313ESC 1414ESC 1515ESC 1616ESC 1717ESC 1818ESC 1919ESC 2020ESC 2121ESC 2222ESC 2323ESC 2424ESC 2525ESC 2626ESC 2727ESC 2828ESC 2929ESC 3030ESC 3131ESC 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ESC 1
- **1:** ESC 2
- **2:** ESC 3
- **3:** ESC 4
- **4:** ESC 5
- **5:** ESC 6
- **6:** ESC 7
- **7:** ESC 8
- **8:** ESC 9
- **9:** ESC 10
- **10:** ESC 11

- **12:** ESC 13
- **13:** ESC 14
- **14:** ESC 15
- **15:** ESC 16
- **16:** ESC 17
- **17:** ESC 18
- **18:** ESC 19
- **19:** ESC 20
- **20:** ESC 21
- **21:** ESC 22
- **22:** ESC 23
- **23:** ESC 24
- **24:** ESC 25
- **25:** ESC 26
- **26:** ESC 27
- **27:** ESC 28
- **28:** ESC 29
- **29:** ESC 30
- **30:** ESC 31
- **31:** ESC 32

---

## CAN_D3_UC_RLY_RT
**Display Name:** DroneCAN relay output rateÂ

**Description:**
Maximum transmit rate for relay outputs, note that this rate is per message each message does 1 relay, so if with more relays will take longer to update at the same rate, a extra message will be sent when a relay changes state

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 200:** hertz

---

## CAN_D3_UC_SER_EN
**Display Name:** DroneCAN Serial enableÂ

**Description:**
Enable DroneCAN virtual serial ports

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
