## CAN_P1_DRIVER
**Display Name:** Index of virtual driver to be used with physical CAN interfaceÂ

**Description:**
Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1First driver2Second driver3Third driver:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** First driver
- **2:** Second driver
- **3:** Third driver

---

## CAN_P1_BITRATE
**Display Name:** Bitrate of CAN interfaceÂ

**Description:**
Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000


---

## CAN_P1_FDBITRATE
**Display Name:** Bitrate of CANFD interfaceÂ

**Description:**
Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning11M22M44M55M88M:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **1:** 1M
- **2:** 2M
- **4:** 4M
- **5:** 5M
- **8:** 8M

---

## CAN_P1_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** CAN per-interface optionsÂ

**Description:**
CAN per-interface options

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0LogAllFrames:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** LogAllFrames

---

## CAN_P2_DRIVER
**Display Name:** Index of virtual driver to be used with physical CAN interfaceÂ

**Description:**
Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1First driver2Second driver3Third driver:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** First driver
- **2:** Second driver
- **3:** Third driver

---

## CAN_P2_BITRATE
**Display Name:** Bitrate of CAN interfaceÂ

**Description:**
Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000


---

## CAN_P2_FDBITRATE
**Display Name:** Bitrate of CANFD interfaceÂ

**Description:**
Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning11M22M44M55M88M:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **1:** 1M
- **2:** 2M
- **4:** 4M
- **5:** 5M
- **8:** 8M

---

## CAN_P2_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** CAN per-interface optionsÂ

**Description:**
CAN per-interface options

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0LogAllFrames:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** LogAllFrames

---

## CAN_P3_DRIVER
**Display Name:** Index of virtual driver to be used with physical CAN interfaceÂ

**Description:**
Enabling this option enables use of CAN buses.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1First driver2Second driver3Third driver:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** First driver
- **2:** Second driver
- **3:** Third driver

---

## CAN_P3_BITRATE
**Display Name:** Bitrate of CAN interfaceÂ

**Description:**
Bit rate can be set up to from 10000 to 1000000


---

## CAN_P3_FDBITRATE
**Display Name:** Bitrate of CANFD interfaceÂ

**Description:**
Bit rate can be set up to from 1000000 to 8000000

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning11M22M44M55M88M:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **1:** 1M
- **2:** 2M
- **4:** 4M
- **5:** 5M
- **8:** 8M

---

## CAN_P3_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** CAN per-interface optionsÂ

**Description:**
CAN per-interface options

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0LogAllFrames:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** LogAllFrames

---

## CAN_SLCAN_CPORT
**Display Name:** SLCAN RouteÂ

**Description:**
CAN Interface ID to be routed to SLCAN, 0 means no routing

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1First interface2Second interface:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** First interface
- **2:** Second interface

---

## CAN_SLCAN_SERNUM
**Display Name:** SLCAN Serial PortÂ

**Description:**
Serial Port ID to be used for temporary SLCAN iface, -1 means no temporary serial. This parameter is automatically reset on reboot or on timeout. See CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT for timeout details

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled0Serial01Serial12Serial23Serial34Serial45Serial56Serial6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **0:** Serial0
- **1:** Serial1
- **2:** Serial2
- **3:** Serial3
- **4:** Serial4
- **5:** Serial5
- **6:** Serial6

---

## CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT
**Display Name:** SLCAN TimeoutÂ

**Description:**
Duration of inactivity after which SLCAN is switched back to original driver in seconds.


---

## CAN_SLCAN_SDELAY
**Display Name:** SLCAN Start DelayÂ

**Description:**
Duration after which slcan starts after setting SERNUM in seconds.


---

## CHUTE_ENABLED
**Display Name:** Parachute release enabled or disabledÂ

**Description:**
Parachute release enabled or disabled

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## CHUTE_TYPE
**Display Name:** Parachute release mechanism type (relay or servo)Â

**Description:**
Parachute release mechanism type (relay number in versions prior to 4.5, or servo). Values 0-3 all are relay. Relay number used for release is set by RELAYx_FUNCTION in 4.5 or later.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Relay10Servo:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Relay
- **10:** Servo

---

## CHUTE_SERVO_ON
**Display Name:** Parachute Servo ON PWM valueÂ

**Description:**
Parachute Servo PWM value in microseconds when parachute is released

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1000 to 2000

---

## CHUTE_SERVO_OFF
**Display Name:** Servo OFF PWM valueÂ

**Description:**
Parachute Servo PWM value in microseconds when parachute is not released

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1000 to 2000

---

## CHUTE_ALT_MIN
**Display Name:** Parachute min altitude in meters above homeÂ

**Description:**
Parachute min altitude above home.  Parachute will not be released below this altitude.  0 to disable alt check.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 32000

---

## CHUTE_DELAY_MS
**Display Name:** Parachute release delayÂ

**Description:**
Delay in millseconds between motor stop and chute release

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 5000

---

## CHUTE_CRT_SINK
**Display Name:** Critical sink speed rate in m/s to trigger emergency parachuteÂ

**Description:**
Release parachute when critical sink rate is reached

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 15

---

## CHUTE_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Parachute optionsÂ

**Description:**
Optional behaviour for parachute

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0hold open forever after release1skip disarm before parachute release:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** hold open forever after release
- **1:** skip disarm before parachute release

---

## COMPASS_OFS_X
**Display Name:** Compass offsets in milligauss on the X axisÂ

**Description:**
Offset to be added to the compass x-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

**Specifications:**
- **Calibration:** Increment
- **1:** 1

---

## COMPASS_OFS_Y
**Display Name:** Compass offsets in milligauss on the Y axisÂ

**Description:**
Offset to be added to the compass y-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

**Specifications:**
- **Calibration:** Increment
- **1:** 1

---

## COMPASS_OFS_Z
**Display Name:** Compass offsets in milligauss on the Z axisÂ

**Description:**
Offset to be added to the compass z-axis values to compensate for metal in the frame

**Specifications:**
- **Calibration:** Increment
- **1:** 1

---

## COMPASS_DEC
**Display Name:** Compass declinationÂ

**Description:**
An angle to compensate between the true north and magnetic north

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -3.142 to 3.142

---

## COMPASS_LEARN
**Display Name:** Learn compass offsets automaticallyÂ

**Description:**
Enable or disable the automatic learning of compass offsets. You can enable learning either using a compass-only method that is suitable only for fixed wing aircraft or using the offsets learnt by the active EKF state estimator. If this option is enabled then the learnt offsets are saved when you disarm the vehicle. If InFlight learning is enabled then the compass with automatically start learning once a flight starts (must be armed). While InFlight learning is running you cannot use position control modes.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Internal-Learning2EKF-Learning3InFlight-Learning:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Internal-Learning
- **2:** EKF-Learning
- **3:** InFlight-Learning

---

## COMPASS_USE
**Display Name:** Use compass for yawÂ

**Description:**
Enable or disable the use of the compass (instead of the GPS) for determining heading

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## COMPASS_AUTODEC
**Display Name:** Auto DeclinationÂ

**Description:**
Enable or disable the automatic calculation of the declination based on gps location

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## COMPASS_MOTCT
**Display Name:** Motor interference compensation typeÂ

**Description:**
Set motor interference compensation type to disabled, throttle or current.  Do not change manually.

**Specifications:**
- **Calibration:** Values
- **1:** Use Throttle
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **2:** Use Current

---

## COMPASS_MOT_X
**Display Name:** Motor interference compensation for body frame X axisÂ

**Description:**
Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's x-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

**Specifications:**
- **Calibration:** Increment
- **1:** 1

---

## COMPASS_MOT_Y
**Display Name:** Motor interference compensation for body frame Y axisÂ

**Description:**
Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's y-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

**Specifications:**
- **Calibration:** Increment
- **1:** 1

---

## COMPASS_MOT_Z
**Display Name:** Motor interference compensation for body frame Z axisÂ

**Description:**
Multiplied by the current throttle and added to the compass's z-axis values to compensate for motor interference (Offset per Amp or at Full Throttle)

**Specifications:**
- **Calibration:** Increment
- **1:** 1

---

## COMPASS_ORIENT
**Display Name:** Compass orientationÂ

**Description:**
The orientation of the first external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

**Specifications:**
- ** 4.1 and older101Custom 1102Custom 2:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Yaw45
- **2:** Yaw90
- **3:** Yaw135
- **4:** Yaw180
- **5:** Yaw225
- **6:** Yaw270
- **7:** Yaw315
- **8:** Roll180
- **9:** Yaw45Roll180
- **10:** Yaw90Roll180
- **11:** Yaw135Roll180
- **12:** Pitch180
- **13:** Yaw225Roll180
- **14:** Yaw270Roll180
- **15:** Yaw315Roll180
- **16:** Roll90
- **17:** Yaw45Roll90
- **18:** Yaw90Roll90
- **19:** Yaw135Roll90
- **20:** Roll270
- **21:** Yaw45Roll270
- **22:** Yaw90Roll270
- **23:** Yaw135Roll270
- **24:** Pitch90
- **25:** Pitch270
- **26:** Yaw90Pitch180
- **27:** Yaw270Pitch180
- **28:** Pitch90Roll90
- **29:** Pitch90Roll180
- **30:** Pitch90Roll270
- **31:** Pitch180Roll90
- **32:** Pitch180Roll270
- **33:** Pitch270Roll90
- **34:** Pitch270Roll180
- **35:** Pitch270Roll270
- **36:** Yaw90Pitch180Roll90
- **37:** Yaw270Roll90
- **38:** Yaw293Pitch68Roll180
- **39:** Pitch315
- **40:** Pitch315Roll90
- **42:** Roll45
- **43:** Roll315
- **100:** Custom 4.1 and older
- **101:** Custom 1
- **102:** Custom 2

---

## COMPASS_EXTERNAL
**Display Name:** Compass is attached via an external cableÂ

**Description:**
Configure compass so it is attached externally. This is auto-detected on most boards. Set to 1 if the compass is externally connected. When externally connected the COMPASS_ORIENT option operates independently of the AHRS_ORIENTATION board orientation option. If set to 0 or 1 then auto-detection by bus connection can override the value. If set to 2 then auto-detection will be disabled.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Internal1External2ForcedExternal:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Internal
- **1:** External
- **2:** ForcedExternal

---

## COMPASS_ORIENT2
**Display Name:** Compass2 orientationÂ

**Description:**
The orientation of a second external compass relative to the vehicle frame. This value will be ignored unless this compass is set as an external compass. When set correctly in the northern hemisphere, pointing the nose and right side down should increase the MagX and MagY values respectively. Rolling the vehicle upside down should decrease the MagZ value. For southern hemisphere, switch increase and decrease. NOTE: For internal compasses, AHRS_ORIENT is used. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the COMPASS_CUS_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for Compass orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

**Display Name:** Compass3 soft-iron diagonal Z componentÂ

**Description:**
DIA_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

**Display Name:** Compass3 soft-iron off-diagonal X componentÂ

ODI_X in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

**Display Name:** Compass3 soft-iron off-diagonal Y componentÂ

ODI_Y in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

## COMPASS_ODI3_Z
**Display Name:** Compass3 soft-iron off-diagonal Z componentÂ

ODI_Z in the compass3 soft-iron calibration matrix: [[DIA_X, ODI_X, ODI_Y], [ODI_X, DIA_Y, ODI_Z], [ODI_Y, ODI_Z, DIA_Z]]

## COMPASS_CAL_FIT
**Display Name:** Compass calibration fitnessÂ

**Description:**
This controls the fitness level required for a successful compass calibration. A lower value makes for a stricter fit (less likely to pass). This is the value used for the primary magnetometer. Other magnetometers get double the value.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 4 to 32
- **Value:** Meaning
- **4:** Very Strict
- **8:** Strict
- **16:** Default
- **32:** Relaxed

---

## COMPASS_OFFS_MAX
**Display Name:** Compass maximum offsetÂ

**Description:**
This sets the maximum allowed compass offset in calibration and arming checks

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 500 to 3000

---

## COMPASS_DISBLMSK
**Display Name:** Compass disable driver type maskÂ

**Description:**
This is a bitmask of driver types to disable. If a driver type is set in this mask then that driver will not try to find a sensor at startup

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0HMC58831LSM303D2AK89633BMM1504LSM9DS15LIS3MDL6AK099167IST83108ICM209489MMC341611DroneCAN12QMC588314MAG311015IST830816RM310017MSP18ExternalAHRS19MMC5XX320QMC5883P21BMM35022IIS2MDC:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** HMC5883
- **1:** LSM303D
- **2:** AK8963
- **3:** BMM150
- **4:** LSM9DS1
- **5:** LIS3MDL
- **6:** AK09916
- **7:** IST8310
- **8:** ICM20948
- **9:** MMC3416
- **11:** DroneCAN
- **12:** QMC5883
- **14:** MAG3110
- **15:** IST8308
- **16:** RM3100
- **17:** MSP
- **18:** ExternalAHRS
- **19:** MMC5XX3
- **20:** QMC5883P
- **21:** BMM350
- **22:** IIS2MDC

---

## COMPASS_FLTR_RNG
**Display Name:** Range in which sample is acceptedÂ

**Description:**
This sets the range around the average value that new samples must be within to be accepted. This can help reduce the impact of noise on sensors that are on long I2C cables. The value is a percentage from the average value. A value of zero disables this filter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 100

---

## COMPASS_AUTO_ROT
**Display Name:** Automatically check orientationÂ

**Description:**
When enabled this will automatically check the orientation of compasses on successful completion of compass calibration. If set to 2 then external compasses will have their orientation automatically corrected.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1CheckOnly2CheckAndFix3use same tolerance to auto rotate 45 deg rotations:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** CheckOnly
- **2:** CheckAndFix
- **3:** use same tolerance to auto rotate 45 deg rotations

---

## COMPASS_PRIO1_ID
**Display Name:** Compass device id with 1st order priorityÂ

**Description:**
Compass device id with 1st order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.


---

## COMPASS_PRIO2_ID
**Display Name:** Compass device id with 2nd order priorityÂ

**Description:**
Compass device id with 2nd order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.


---

## COMPASS_PRIO3_ID
**Display Name:** Compass device id with 3rd order priorityÂ

**Description:**
Compass device id with 3rd order priority, set automatically if 0. Reboot required after change.


---

## COMPASS_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable CompassÂ

**Description:**
Setting this to Enabled(1) will enable the compass. Setting this to Disabled(0) will disable the compass. Note that this is separate from COMPASS_USE. This will enable the low level senor, and will enable logging of magnetometer data. To use the compass for navigation you must also set COMPASS_USE to 1.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## COMPASS_SCALE
**Display Name:** Compass1 scale factorÂ

**Description:**
Scaling factor for first compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done


---

## COMPASS_SCALE2
**Display Name:** Compass2 scale factorÂ

**Description:**
Scaling factor for 2nd compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done


---

## COMPASS_SCALE3
**Display Name:** Compass3 scale factorÂ

**Description:**
Scaling factor for 3rd compass to compensate for sensor scaling errors. If this is 0 then no scaling is done


---

## COMPASS_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Compass optionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options to change the behaviour of the compass

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0CalRequireGPS1Allow missing DroneCAN compasses to be automaticaly replaced (calibration still required):** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** CalRequireGPS
- **1:** Allow missing DroneCAN compasses to be automaticaly replaced (calibration still required)

---

**Display Name:** Compass4 device idÂ

**Description:**
Extra 4th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

## COMPASS_DEV_ID5

**Description:**
Extra 5th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

Extra 7th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually


---

**Display Name:** Compass8 device idÂ

Extra 8th compass's device id.  Automatically detected, do not set manually

## COMPASS_CUS_ROLL
**Display Name:** Custom orientation roll offsetÂ

**Description:**
Compass mounting position roll offset. Positive values = roll right, negative values = roll left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** -180 to 180

---

## COMPASS_CUS_PIT
**Display Name:** Custom orientation pitch offsetÂ

**Description:**
Compass mounting position pitch offset. Positive values = pitch up, negative values = pitch down. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** -180 to 180

---

## COMPASS_CUS_YAW
**Display Name:** Custom orientation yaw offsetÂ

**Description:**
Compass mounting position yaw offset. Positive values = yaw right, negative values = yaw left. This parameter is only used when COMPASS_ORIENT/2/3 is set to CUSTOM.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** -180 to 180

---

## DDS_TIMEOUT_MS

The time in milliseconds the DDS client will wait for a response from the XRCE agent before reattempting.

- **1:** 1 to 10000

**Description:**
The maximum number of times the DDS client will attempt to ping the XRCE agent before exiting. Set to 0 to allow unlimited retries.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 100

External AHRS sensors bitmask

Logging rate for EARHS devices

## EFI_TYPE
**Display Name:** EFI communication typeÂ

**Description:**
What method of communication is used for EFI #1

- **ValueMeaning0None1Serial-MS2NWPMU3Serial-Lutan5DroneCAN6Currawong-ECU7Scripting8Hirth9MAVLink:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning

- **2:** NWPMU
