## NMEA_RATE_MS
**Display Name:** NMEA Output rateÂ

**Description:**
NMEA Output rate. This controls the interval at which all the enabled NMEA messages are sent. Most NMEA systems expect 100ms (10Hz) or slower.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 20 to 2000

---

## NMEA_MSG_EN
**Display Name:** Messages Enable bitmaskÂ

**Description:**
This is a bitmask of enabled NMEA messages. All messages will be sent consecutively at the same rate interval

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0GPGGA1GPRMC2PASHR:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** GPGGA
- **1:** GPRMC
- **2:** PASHR

---

## NTF_LED_BRIGHT
**Display Name:** LED BrightnessÂ

**Description:**
Select the RGB LED brightness level. When USB is connected brightness will never be higher than low regardless of the setting.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Off1Low2Medium3High:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Off
- **1:** Low
- **2:** Medium
- **3:** High

---

## NTF_BUZZ_TYPES
**Display Name:** Buzzer Driver TypesÂ

**Description:**
Controls what types of Buzzer will be enabled

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Built-in buzzer1DShot2DroneCAN:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Built-in buzzer
- **1:** DShot
- **2:** DroneCAN

---

## NTF_LED_OVERRIDE
**Display Name:** Specifies colour source for the RGBLedÂ

**Description:**
Specifies the source for the colours and brightness for the LED.  OutbackChallenge conforms to the MedicalExpress (https://uavchallenge.org/medical-express/) rules, essentially "Green" is disarmed (safe-to-approach), "Red" is armed (not safe-to-approach). Traffic light is a simplified color set, red when armed, yellow when the safety switch is not surpressing outputs (but disarmed), and green when outputs are surpressed and disarmed, the LED will blink faster if disarmed and failing arming checks.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Standard1MAVLink/Scripting/AP_Periph2OutbackChallenge3TrafficLight:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Standard
- **1:** MAVLink/Scripting/AP_Periph
- **2:** OutbackChallenge
- **3:** TrafficLight

---

## NTF_DISPLAY_TYPE
**Display Name:** Type of on-board I2C displayÂ

**Description:**
This sets up the type of on-board I2C display. Disabled by default.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disable1ssd13062sh110610SITL:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disable
- **1:** ssd1306
- **2:** sh1106
- **10:** SITL

---

## NTF_OREO_THEME
**Display Name:** OreoLED ThemeÂ

**Description:**
Enable/Disable Solo Oreo LED driver, 0 to disable, 1 for Aircraft theme, 2 for Rover theme

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Aircraft2Rover:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Aircraft
- **2:** Rover

---

## NTF_BUZZ_PIN
**Display Name:** Buzzer pinÂ

**Description:**
Enables to connect active buzzer to arbitrary pin. Requires 3-pin buzzer or additional MOSFET! Some the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled

---

## NTF_LED_TYPES
**Display Name:** LED Driver TypesÂ

**Description:**
Controls what types of LEDs will be enabled

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Built-in LED1Internal ToshibaLED2External ToshibaLED3External PCA96854Oreo LED5DroneCAN6NCP5623 External7NCP5623 Internal8NeoPixel9ProfiLED10Scripting11DShot12ProfiLED_SPI13LP5562 External14LP5562 Internal15IS31FL3195 External16IS31FL3195 Internal17DiscreteRGB18NeoPixelRGB:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Built-in LED
- **1:** Internal ToshibaLED
- **2:** External ToshibaLED
- **3:** External PCA9685
- **4:** Oreo LED
- **5:** DroneCAN
- **6:** NCP5623 External
- **7:** NCP5623 Internal
- **8:** NeoPixel
- **9:** ProfiLED
- **10:** Scripting
- **11:** DShot
- **12:** ProfiLED_SPI
- **13:** LP5562 External
- **14:** LP5562 Internal
- **15:** IS31FL3195 External
- **16:** IS31FL3195 Internal
- **17:** DiscreteRGB
- **18:** NeoPixelRGB

---

## NTF_BUZZ_ON_LVL
**Display Name:** Buzzer-on pin logic levelÂ

**Description:**
Specifies pin level that indicates buzzer should play

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0LowIsOn1HighIsOn:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** LowIsOn
- **1:** HighIsOn

---

## NTF_BUZZ_VOLUME
**Display Name:** Buzzer volumeÂ

**Description:**
Control the volume of the buzzer

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 100:** percent

---

## NTF_LED_LEN
**Display Name:** Serial LED String LengthÂ

**Description:**
The number of Serial LED's to use for notifications (NeoPixel's and ProfiLED)


---

## OSD_TYPE
**Display Name:** OSD typeÂ

**Description:**
OSD type. TXONLY makes the OSD parameter selection available to other modules even if there is no native OSD support on the board, for instance CRSF.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1MAX74562SITL3MSP4TXONLY5MSP_DISPLAYPORT:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** MAX7456
- **2:** SITL
- **3:** MSP
- **4:** TXONLY
- **5:** MSP_DISPLAYPORT

---

## OSD_CHAN
**Display Name:** Screen switch transmitter channelÂ

**Description:**
This sets the channel used to switch different OSD screens.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disable5Chan56Chan67Chan78Chan89Chan910Chan1011Chan1112Chan1213Chan1314Chan1415Chan1516Chan16:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disable
- **5:** Chan5
- **6:** Chan6
- **7:** Chan7
- **8:** Chan8
- **9:** Chan9
- **10:** Chan10
- **11:** Chan11
- **12:** Chan12
- **13:** Chan13
- **14:** Chan14
- **15:** Chan15
- **16:** Chan16

---

## OSD_SW_METHOD
**Display Name:** Screen switch methodÂ

**Description:**
This sets the method used to switch different OSD screens.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0switch to next screen if channel value was changed1select screen based on pwm ranges specified for each screen2switch to next screen after low to high transition and every 1s while channel value is high:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** switch to next screen if channel value was changed
- **1:** select screen based on pwm ranges specified for each screen
- **2:** switch to next screen after low to high transition and every 1s while channel value is high

---

## OSD_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** OSD OptionsÂ

**Description:**
This sets options that change the display

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0UseDecimalPack1InvertedWindArrow2InvertedAHRoll3Convert feet to miles at 5280ft instead of 10000ft4DisableCrosshair5TranslateArrows6AviationStyleAH7Prefix LQ with RF Mode:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** UseDecimalPack
- **1:** InvertedWindArrow
- **2:** InvertedAHRoll
- **3:** Convert feet to miles at 5280ft instead of 10000ft
- **4:** DisableCrosshair
- **5:** TranslateArrows
- **6:** AviationStyleAH
- **7:** Prefix LQ with RF Mode

---

## OSD_FONT
**Display Name:** OSD FontÂ

**Description:**
This sets which OSD font to use. It is an integer from 0 to the number of fonts available


---

## OSD_V_OFFSET
**Display Name:** OSD vertical offsetÂ

**Description:**
Sets vertical offset of the osd inside image


---

## OSD_H_OFFSET
**Display Name:** OSD horizontal offsetÂ

**Description:**
Sets horizontal offset of the osd inside image


---

## OSD_W_RSSI
**Display Name:** RSSI warn level (in %)Â

**Description:**
Set level at which RSSI item will flash (in positive % or negative dBm values as applicable). 30% or -100dBm are defaults.


---

## OSD_W_NSAT
**Display Name:** NSAT warn levelÂ

**Description:**
Set level at which NSAT item will flash


---

## OSD_W_BATVOLT
**Display Name:** BAT_VOLT warn levelÂ

**Description:**
Set level at which BAT_VOLT item will flash


---

## OSD_UNITS
**Display Name:** Display UnitsÂ

**Description:**
Sets the units to use in displaying items

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Metric1Imperial2SI3Aviation:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Metric
- **1:** Imperial
- **2:** SI
- **3:** Aviation

---

## OSD_MSG_TIME
**Display Name:** Message display duration in secondsÂ

**Description:**
Sets message duration seconds


---

## OSD_ARM_SCR
**Display Name:** Arm screenÂ

**Description:**
Screen to be shown on Arm event. Zero to disable the feature.


---

## OSD_DSARM_SCR
**Display Name:** Disarm screenÂ

**Description:**
Screen to be shown on disarm event. Zero to disable the feature.


---

## OSD_FS_SCR
**Display Name:** Failsafe screenÂ

**Description:**
Screen to be shown on failsafe event. Zero to disable the feature.


---

## OSD_BTN_DELAY
**Display Name:** Button delayÂ

**Description:**
Debounce time in ms for stick commanded parameter navigation.


---

## OSD_W_TERR
**Display Name:** Terrain warn levelÂ

**Description:**
Set level below which TER_HGT item will flash. -1 disables.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 3000:** meters

---

## OSD_W_AVGCELLV
**Display Name:** AVGCELLV warn levelÂ

**Description:**
Set level at which AVGCELLV item will flash


---

## OSD_CELL_COUNT
**Display Name:** Battery cell countÂ

**Description:**
Used for average cell voltage display. -1 disables, 0 uses cell count autodetection for well charged LIPO/LIION batteries at connection, other values manually select cell count used.


---

## OSD_W_RESTVOLT
**Display Name:** RESTVOLT warn levelÂ

**Description:**
Set level at which RESTVOLT item will flash


---

## OSD_W_ACRVOLT
**Display Name:** Avg Cell Resting Volt warn levelÂ

**Description:**
Set level at which ACRVOLT item will flash


---

## OSD_W_LQ
**Display Name:** RC link quality warn level (in %)Â

**Description:**
Set level at which RC_LQ item will flash (%)


---

## OSD_W_SNR
**Display Name:** RC link SNR warn level (in %)Â

**Description:**
Set level at which RC_SNR item will flash (in db)


---

## OSD_SB_H_OFS
**Display Name:** Sidebar horizontal offsetÂ

**Description:**
Extends the spacing between the sidebar elements by this amount of columns. Positive values increases the width to the right of the screen.


---

## OSD_SB_V_EXT
**Display Name:** Sidebar vertical extensionÂ

**Description:**
Increase of vertical length of the sidebar itens by this amount of lines. Applied equally both above and below the default setting.


---

OSD type 2. TXONLY makes the OSD parameter selection available to other modules even if there is no native OSD support on the board, for instance CRSF.

- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** MAX7456

- **3:** MSP
- **4:** TXONLY
- **5:** MSP_DISPLAYPORT

- **1:** 50x18
- **2:** 60x22

- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

## OSD2_TXT_RES
**Display Name:** Sets the overlay text resolution (MSP DisplayPort only)Â

**Description:**
Sets the overlay text resolution for this screen to either SD 30x16 or HD 50x18/60x22 (MSP DisplayPort only)

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning030x16150x18260x22:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** 30x16
- **1:** 50x18
- **2:** 60x22

**Display Name:** LINK_Q_XÂ

**Description:**
Horizontal position on screen


---

## OSD4_LINK_Q_Y
**Display Name:** LINK_Q_YÂ

**Description:**
Vertical position on screen


---

## OSD4_TXT_RES
**Display Name:** Sets the overlay text resolution (MSP DisplayPort only)Â

**Description:**
Sets the overlay text resolution for this screen to either SD 30x16 or HD 50x18/60x22 (MSP DisplayPort only)

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning030x16150x18260x22:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** 30x16
- **1:** 50x18
- **2:** 60x22

---

## OSD4_FONT
**Display Name:** Sets the font index for this screen (MSP DisplayPort only)Â

**Description:**
Sets the font index for this screen (MSP DisplayPort only)


---

## OSD4_RC_PWR_EN
**Display Name:** RC_PWR_ENÂ

**Description:**
Displays the RC link transmit (TX) power in mW or W, depending on level

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## OSD4_RC_PWR_X
**Display Name:** RC_PWR_XÂ

**Description:**
Horizontal position on screen


---

## OSD4_RC_PWR_Y
**Display Name:** RC_PWR_YÂ

**Description:**
Vertical position on screen


---

## OSD4_RSSIDBM_EN
**Display Name:** RSSIDBM_ENÂ

**Description:**
Displays RC link signal strength in dBm

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## OSD4_RSSIDBM_X
**Display Name:** RSSIDBM_XÂ

**Description:**
Horizontal position on screen


---

## OSD4_RSSIDBM_Y
**Display Name:** RSSIDBM_YÂ

**Description:**
Vertical position on screen


---

## OSD4_RC_SNR_EN
**Display Name:** RC_SNR_ENÂ

**Description:**
Displays RC link signal to noise ratio in dB

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## OSD4_RC_SNR_X
**Display Name:** RC_SNR_XÂ

**Description:**
Horizontal position on screen


---

## OSD4_RC_SNR_Y
**Display Name:** RC_SNR_YÂ

**Description:**
Vertical position on screen


---

## OSD4_RC_ANT_EN
**Display Name:** RC_ANT_ENÂ

**Description:**
Displays the current RC link active antenna

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## OSD4_RC_ANT_X
**Display Name:** RC_ANT_XÂ

**Description:**
Horizontal position on screen


---

## OSD4_RC_ANT_Y
**Display Name:** RC_ANT_YÂ

**Description:**
Vertical position on screen


---

## OSD4_RC_LQ_EN
**Display Name:** RC_LQ_ENÂ

**Description:**
Displays the RC link quality (uplink, 0 to 100%) and also RF mode if bit 7 of OSD_OPTIONS is set

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## OSD4_RC_LQ_X
**Display Name:** RC_LQ_XÂ

**Description:**
Horizontal position on screen


---

## OSD4_RC_LQ_Y
**Display Name:** RC_LQ_YÂ

**Description:**
Vertical position on screen


---

## OSD4_ESC_IDX
**Display Name:** ESC_IDXÂ

**Description:**
Index of the ESC to use for displaying ESC information. 0 means use the ESC with the highest value.


---

- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled

## PLND_ENABLED
**Display Name:** Precision Land enabled/disabledÂ

**Description:**
Precision Land enabled/disabled

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## PLND_TYPE
**Display Name:** Precision Land TypeÂ

**Description:**
Precision Land Type

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1MAVLink2IRLock3SITL_Gazebo4SITL:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** MAVLink
- **2:** IRLock
- **3:** SITL_Gazebo
- **4:** SITL

---

## PLND_YAW_ALIGN
**Display Name:** Sensor yaw alignmentÂ

**Description:**
Yaw angle from body x-axis to sensor x-axis.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **10:** 0 to 36000

---

## PLND_LAND_OFS_X
**Display Name:** Land offset forwardÂ

**Description:**
Desired landing position of the camera forward of the target in vehicle body frame

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** -20 to 20

---

## PLND_LAND_OFS_Y
**Display Name:** Land offset rightÂ

**Description:**
desired landing position of the camera right of the target in vehicle body frame

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** -20 to 20

---

## PLND_EST_TYPE
**Display Name:** Precision Land Estimator TypeÂ

**Description:**
Specifies the estimation method to be used

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0RawSensor1KalmanFilter:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** RawSensor
- **1:** KalmanFilter

---

## PLND_ACC_P_NSE
**Display Name:** Kalman Filter Accelerometer NoiseÂ

**Description:**
Kalman Filter Accelerometer Noise, higher values weight the input from the camera more, accels less


---

## PLND_CAM_POS_X
**Display Name:** Camera X position offsetÂ

**Description:**
X position of the camera in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## PLND_CAM_POS_Y
**Display Name:** Camera Y position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Y position of the camera in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## PLND_CAM_POS_Z
**Display Name:** Camera Z position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Z position of the camera in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## PLND_BUS
**Display Name:** Sensor BusÂ

**Description:**
Precland sensor bus for I2C sensors.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1DefaultBus0InternalI2C1ExternalI2C:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** DefaultBus
- **0:** InternalI2C
- **1:** ExternalI2C

---

## PLND_LAG
**Display Name:** Precision Landing sensor lagÂ

**Description:**
Precision Landing sensor lag, to cope with variable landing_target latency

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0.02 to 0.250

---

## PLND_XY_DIST_MAX
**Display Name:** Precision Landing maximum distance to target before descendingÂ

**Description:**
The vehicle will not start descending if the landing target is detected and it is further than this many meters away. Set 0 to always descend.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 10:** meters

---

## PLND_STRICT
**Display Name:** PrecLand strictnessÂ

**Description:**
How strictly should the vehicle land on the target if target is lost

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Land Vertically (Not strict)1Retry Landing(Normal Strictness)2Do not land (just Hover) (Very Strict):** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Land Vertically (Not strict)
- **1:** Retry Landing(Normal Strictness)
- **2:** Do not land (just Hover) (Very Strict)

---

## PLND_RET_MAX
**Display Name:** PrecLand Maximum number of retires for a failed landingÂ

**Description:**
PrecLand Maximum number of retires for a failed landing. Set to zero to disable landing retry.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

## PLND_TIMEOUT
**Display Name:** PrecLand retry timeoutÂ

**Description:**
Time for which vehicle continues descend even if target is lost. After this time period, vehicle will attempt a landing retry depending on PLND_STRICT parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 20:** seconds

---

## PLND_RET_BEHAVE
**Display Name:** PrecLand retry behaviourÂ

**Description:**
Prec Land will do the action selected by this parameter if a retry to a landing is needed

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Go to the last location where landing target was detected1Go towards the approximate location of the detected landing target:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Go to the last location where landing target was detected
- **1:** Go towards the approximate location of the detected landing target

---

## PLND_ALT_MIN
**Display Name:** PrecLand minimum alt for retryÂ

**Description:**
Vehicle will continue landing vertically even if target is lost below this height. This needs a rangefinder to work. Set to zero to disable this.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 5:** meters

---

## PLND_ALT_MAX
**Display Name:** PrecLand maximum alt for retryÂ

**Description:**
Vehicle will continue landing vertically until this height if target is not found. Below this height if landing target is not found, landing retry/failsafe might be attempted. This needs a rangefinder to work. Set to zero to disable this.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 50:** meters

---

## PLND_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Precision Landing Extra OptionsÂ

**Description:**
Precision Landing Extra Options

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Moving Landing Target1Allow Precision Landing after manual reposition2Maintain high speed in final descent:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Moving Landing Target
- **1:** Allow Precision Landing after manual reposition
- **2:** Maintain high speed in final descent

---

## PLND_ORIENT
**Display Name:** Camera OrientationÂ

**Description:**
Orientation of camera/sensor on body

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Forward4Back25Down:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Forward
- **4:** Back
- **25:** Down

---

## PTCH2SRV_TCONST
**Display Name:** Pitch Time ConstantÂ

**Description:**
Time constant in seconds from demanded to achieved pitch angle. Most models respond well to 0.5. May be reduced for faster responses, but setting lower than a model can achieve will not help.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.4 to 1.0

---

## PTCH2SRV_RMAX_UP
**Display Name:** Pitch up max rateÂ

**Description:**
This sets the maximum nose up pitch rate that the attitude controller will demand (degrees/sec) in angle stabilized modes. Setting it to zero disables the limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 100

---

## PTCH2SRV_RMAX_DN
**Display Name:** Pitch down max rateÂ

**Description:**
This sets the maximum nose down pitch rate that the attitude controller will demand (degrees/sec) in angle stabilized modes. Setting it to zero disables the limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 100

---

## PTCH2SRV_RLL
**Display Name:** Roll compensationÂ

**Description:**
Gain added to pitch to keep aircraft from descending or ascending in turns. Increase in increments of 0.05 to reduce altitude loss. Decrease for altitude gain.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** 0.7 to 1.5

---

## PTCH_RATE_P
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller P gain. Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.005:** 0.08 to 0.35

---

## PTCH_RATE_I
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller I gainÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0.01 to 0.6

---

## PTCH_RATE_IMAX
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximumÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## PTCH_RATE_D
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller D gainÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0.001 to 0.03

---

## PTCH_RATE_FF
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller feed forwardÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller feed forward

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 3.0

---

## PTCH_RATE_FLTT
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 2 to 50

---

## PTCH_RATE_FLTE
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 2 to 50

---

## PTCH_RATE_FLTD
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 50

---

## PTCH_RATE_SMAX
**Display Name:** Pitch slew rate limitÂ

**Description:**
Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.5:** 0 to 200

---

## PTCH_RATE_PDMX
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximumÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## PTCH_RATE_D_FF
**Display Name:** Pitch Derivative FeedForward GainÂ

**Description:**
FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 0.03

---

## PTCH_RATE_NTF
**Display Name:** Pitch Target notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Pitch Target notch filter index


---

## PTCH_RATE_NEF
**Display Name:** Pitch Error notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Pitch Error notch filter index


---

## PUP_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable pullup after altitude waitÂ

**Description:**
Enable pullup after altitude wait

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## PUP_ELEV_OFS
**Display Name:** Elevator deflection used before starting pullupÂ

**Description:**
Elevator deflection offset from -1 to 1 while waiting for airspeed to rise before starting close loop control of the pullup.


---

## PUP_NG_LIM
**Display Name:** Maximum normal load factor during pullupÂ

**Description:**
This is the nominal maximum value of normal load factor used during the closed loop pitch rate control of the pullup.


---

## PUP_NG_JERK_LIM
**Display Name:** Maximum normal load factor rate of change during pullupÂ

**Description:**
The normal load factor used for closed loop pitch rate control of the pullup will be ramped up to the value set by PUP_NG_LIM at the rate of change set by this parameter. The parameter value specified will be scaled internally by 1/EAS2TAS.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.1 to 10.0:** per second

---

## PUP_PITCH
**Display Name:** Target pitch angle during pullupÂ

**Description:**
The vehicle will attempt achieve this pitch angle during the pull-up maneouvre.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-5 to 15:** degrees

---

## PUP_ARSPD_START
**Display Name:** Pullup target airspeedÂ

**Description:**
Target airspeed for initial airspeed wait

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 100:** meters per second

---

## PUP_PITCH_START
**Display Name:** Pullup target pitchÂ

**Description:**
Target pitch for initial pullup

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-80 to 0:** degrees

---

## QWIK_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Quicktune enableÂ

**Description:**
Enable quicktune system

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## QWIK_AXES
**Display Name:** Quicktune axesÂ

**Description:**
Axes to tune

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Roll1Pitch2Yaw:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Roll
- **1:** Pitch
- **2:** Yaw

---

## QWIK_DOUBLE_TIME
**Display Name:** Quicktune doubling timeÂ

**Description:**
Time to double a tuning parameter. Raise this for a slower tune.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **5 to 20:** seconds

---

## QWIK_GAIN_MARGIN
**Display Name:** Quicktune gain marginÂ

**Description:**
Reduction in gain after oscillation detected. Raise this number to get a more conservative tune

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **20 to 80:** percent

---

## QWIK_OSC_SMAX
**Display Name:** Quicktune oscillation rate thresholdÂ

**Description:**
Threshold for oscillation detection. A lower value will lead to a more conservative tune.


---

## QWIK_YAW_P_MAX
**Display Name:** Quicktune Yaw P maxÂ

**Description:**
Maximum value for yaw P gain


---

## QWIK_YAW_D_MAX
**Display Name:** Quicktune Yaw D maxÂ

**Description:**
Maximum value for yaw D gain


---

## QWIK_RP_PI_RATIO
**Display Name:** Quicktune roll/pitch PI ratioÂ

**Description:**
Ratio between P and I gains for roll and pitch. Raise this to get a lower I gain


---

## QWIK_Y_PI_RATIO
**Display Name:** Quicktune Yaw PI ratioÂ

**Description:**
Ratio between P and I gains for yaw. Raise this to get a lower I gain


---

## QWIK_AUTO_FILTER
**Display Name:** Quicktune auto filter enableÂ

**Description:**
When enabled the PID filter settings are automatically set based on INS_GYRO_FILTER

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## QWIK_AUTO_SAVE
**Display Name:** Quicktune auto saveÂ

**Description:**
Number of seconds after completion of tune to auto-save. This is useful when using a 2 position switch for quicktune. Zero (the default value) disables automatic saving, and you will need to have a 3 position switch to save or use GCS auxilliary functions.


---

## QWIK_REDUCE_MAX
**Display Name:** Quicktune maximum gain reductionÂ

**Description:**
This controls how much quicktune is allowed to lower gains from the original gains. If the vehicle already has a reasonable tune and is not oscillating then you can set this to zero to prevent gain reductions. The default of 20% is reasonable for most vehicles. Using a maximum gain reduction lowers the chance of an angle P oscillation happening if quicktune gets a false positive oscillation at a low gain, which can result in very low rate gains and a dangerous angle P oscillation.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 100:** percent

---

## QWIK_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Quicktune optionsÂ

**Description:**
Additional options. When the Two Position Switch option is enabled then a high switch position will start the tune, low will disable the tune. you should also set a QUIK_AUTO_SAVE time so that you will be able to save the tune.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0UseTwoPositionSwitch:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** UseTwoPositionSwitch

---

## QWIK_ANGLE_MAX
**Display Name:** maximum angle error for tune abortÂ

**Description:**
If while tuning the angle error goes over this limit then the tune will aborts to prevent a bad oscillation in the case of the tuning algorithm failing. If you get an error "Quicktune: attitude error ABORTING" and you think it is a false positive then you can either raise this parameter or you can try increasing the QWIK_DOUBLE_TIME to do the tune more slowly.


---

## Q_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable QuadPlaneÂ

**Description:**
This enables QuadPlane functionality, assuming multicopter motors start on output 5. If this is set to 2 then when starting AUTO mode it will initially be in VTOL AUTO mode.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disable1Enable2Enable VTOL AUTO:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disable
- **1:** Enable
- **2:** Enable VTOL AUTO

---

## Q_ANGLE_MAX
**Display Name:** Angle MaxÂ

**Description:**
Maximum lean angle in all VTOL flight modes

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **10:** 1000 to 8000

---

## Q_TRANSITION_MS
**Display Name:** Transition timeÂ

**Description:**
Transition time in milliseconds after minimum airspeed is reached

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **500 to 30000:** milliseconds

---

## Q_PILOT_SPD_UP
**Display Name:** Pilot maximum vertical speed upÂ

**Description:**
The maximum ascending vertical velocity the pilot may request in m/s

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.5 to 5

---

## Q_PILOT_SPD_DN
**Display Name:** Pilot maximum vertical speed downÂ

**Description:**
The maximum vertical velocity the pilot may request in m/s going down. If 0, uses Q_PILOT_SPD_UP value.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.5 to 5

---

## Q_PILOT_ACCEL_Z
**Display Name:** Pilot vertical accelerationÂ

**Description:**
The vertical acceleration used when pilot is controlling the altitude

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.5 to 5

---

## Q_RC_SPEED
**Display Name:** RC output speed in HzÂ

**Description:**
This is the PWM refresh rate in Hz for QuadPlane quad motors

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **10:** 50 to 500

---

## Q_ASSIST_SPEED
**Display Name:** Quadplane assistance speedÂ

**Description:**
This is the speed below which the quad motors will provide stability and lift assistance in fixed wing modes. The default value of 0 disables assistance but will generate a pre-arm failure to encourage users to set this parameter to -1, or a positive, non-zero value. If this is set to -1 then all Q_ASSIST features are disabled except during transitions. A high non-zero,positive value will lead to more false positives which can waste battery. A lower value will result in less false positive, but will result in assistance taking longer to trigger. If unsure then set to 3 m/s below the minimum airspeed you will fly at. If you don't have an airspeed sensor then use 5 m/s below the minimum airspeed you fly at.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0 to 100

---

## Q_YAW_RATE_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum yaw rateÂ

**Description:**
This is the maximum yaw rate for pilot input on rudder stick in degrees/second

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 50 to 500

---

## Q_LAND_FINAL_SPD
**Display Name:** Land final speedÂ

**Description:**
The descent speed for the final stage of landing in m/s

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.3 to 2

---

## Q_LAND_FINAL_ALT
**Display Name:** Land final altitudeÂ

**Description:**
The altitude at which we should switch to Q_LAND_SPEED descent rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.5 to 50

---

## Q_TRAN_PIT_MAX
**Display Name:** Transition max pitchÂ

**Description:**
Maximum pitch during transition to auto fixed wing flight

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 30

---

## Q_FRAME_CLASS
**Display Name:** Frame ClassÂ

**Description:**
Controls major frame class for multicopter component

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Undefined1Quad2Hexa3Octa4OctaQuad5Y67Tri10Single/Dual12DodecaHexa14Deca15Scripting Matrix17Dynamic Scripting Matrix:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Undefined
- **1:** Quad
- **2:** Hexa
- **3:** Octa
- **4:** OctaQuad
- **5:** Y6
- **7:** Tri
- **10:** Single/Dual
- **12:** DodecaHexa
- **14:** Deca
- **15:** Scripting Matrix
- **17:** Dynamic Scripting Matrix

---

## Q_FRAME_TYPE
**Display Name:** Frame Type (+, X or V)Â

**Description:**
Controls motor mixing for multicopter component

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Plus1X2V3H4V-Tail5A-Tail10Y6B11Y6F12BetaFlightX13DJIX14ClockwiseX15I16MOTOR_FRAME_TYPE_NYT_PLUS17MOTOR_FRAME_TYPE_NYT_X18BetaFlightXReversed19Y4:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Plus
- **1:** X
- **2:** V
- **3:** H
- **4:** V-Tail
- **5:** A-Tail
- **10:** Y6B
- **11:** Y6F
- **12:** BetaFlightX
- **13:** DJIX
- **14:** ClockwiseX
- **15:** I
- **16:** MOTOR_FRAME_TYPE_NYT_PLUS
- **17:** MOTOR_FRAME_TYPE_NYT_X
- **18:** BetaFlightXReversed
- **19:** Y4

---

## Q_VFWD_GAIN
**Display Name:** Forward velocity hold gainÂ

**Description:**
The use of this parameter is no longer recommended and has been superseded by a method that works in all VTOL modes with the exception of QAUTOTUNE which is controlled by the Q_FWD_THR_USE parameter. This Q_VFD_GAIN parameter controls use of the forward motor in VTOL modes that use the velocity controller. Set to 0 to disable this function. A value of 0.05 is a good place to start if you want to use the forward motor for position control. No forward motor will be used in QSTABILIZE or QHOVER modes. Use with QLOITER for position hold with the forward motor.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 0.5

---

## Q_RTL_ALT
**Display Name:** QRTL return altitudeÂ

**Description:**
The altitude which QRTL mode heads to initially

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 200

---

## Q_RTL_MODE
**Display Name:** VTOL RTL modeÂ

**Description:**
If this is set to 1 then an RTL will change to QRTL when within RTL_RADIUS meters of the RTL destination, VTOL approach: vehicle will RTL at RTL alt and circle with a radius of Q_FW_LND_APR_RAD down to Q_RTL_ALT and then transition into the wind and QRTL, see 'AUTO VTOL Landing', QRTL Always: do a QRTL instead of RTL

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled2VTOL approach3QRTL Always:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled
- **2:** VTOL approach
- **3:** QRTL Always

---

## Q_GUIDED_MODE
**Display Name:** Enable VTOL in GUIDED modeÂ

**Description:**
This enables use of VTOL in guided mode. When enabled the aircraft will switch to VTOL flight when the guided destination is reached and hover at the destination.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## Q_ESC_CAL
**Display Name:** ESC CalibrationÂ

**Description:**
This is used to calibrate the throttle range of the VTOL motors. Please read https://ardupilot.org/plane/docs/quadplane-esc-calibration.html before using. This parameter is automatically set back to 0 on every boot. This parameter only takes effect in QSTABILIZE mode. When set to 1 the output of all motors will come directly from the throttle stick when armed, and will be zero when disarmed. When set to 2 the output of all motors will be maximum when armed and zero when disarmed. Make sure you remove all properllers before using.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1ThrottleInput2FullInput:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** ThrottleInput
- **2:** FullInput

---

## Q_VFWD_ALT
**Display Name:** Forward velocity alt cutoffÂ

**Description:**
Controls altitude to disable forward velocity assist when below this relative altitude. This is useful to keep the forward velocity propeller from hitting the ground. Rangefinder height data is incorporated when available.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.25:** 0 to 10

---

## Q_LAND_ICE_CUT
**Display Name:** Cut IC engine on landingÂ

**Description:**
This controls stopping an internal combustion engine in the final landing stage of a VTOL. This is important for aircraft where the forward thrust engine may experience prop-strike if left running during landing. This requires the engine controls are enabled using the ICE_* parameters.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## Q_ASSIST_ANGLE
**Display Name:** Quadplane assistance angleÂ

**Description:**
This is the angular error in attitude beyond which the quadplane VTOL motors will provide stability assistance. This will only be used if Q_ASSIST_SPEED is also positive and non-zero. Assistance will be given if the attitude is outside the normal attitude limits by at least 5 degrees and the angular error in roll or pitch is greater than this angle for at least Q_ASSIST_DELAY seconds. Set to zero to disable angle assistance.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 90

---

## Q_ASSIST_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Quadplane assistance optionsÂ

**Description:**
Options for special QAssist features

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Disable force fixed wing controller recovery1Disable quadplane spin recovery:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Disable force fixed wing controller recovery
- **1:** Disable quadplane spin recovery

---

## Q_MAV_TYPE
**Display Name:** MAVLink type identifierÂ

**Description:**
This controls the mavlink type given in HEARTBEAT messages. For some GCS types a particular setting will be needed for correct operation.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0AUTO1FIXED_WING2QUADROTOR3COAXIAL4HELICOPTER7AIRSHIP8FREE_BALLOON9ROCKET10GROUND_ROVER11SURFACE_BOAT12SUBMARINE16FLAPPING_WING17KITE19VTOL_DUOROTOR20VTOL_QUADROTOR21VTOL_TILTROTOR:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** AUTO
- **1:** FIXED_WING
- **2:** QUADROTOR
- **3:** COAXIAL
- **4:** HELICOPTER
- **7:** AIRSHIP
- **8:** FREE_BALLOON
- **9:** ROCKET
- **10:** GROUND_ROVER
- **11:** SURFACE_BOAT
- **12:** SUBMARINE
- **16:** FLAPPING_WING
- **17:** KITE
- **19:** VTOL_DUOROTOR
- **20:** VTOL_QUADROTOR
- **21:** VTOL_TILTROTOR

---

## Q_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** quadplane optionsÂ

**Description:**
See description for each bitmask bit description

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Level Transition-keep wings within LEVEL_ROLL_LIMIT and only use forward motor(s) for climb during transition1Allow FW Takeoff-if bit is not set then NAV_TAKEOFF command on quadplanes will instead perform a NAV_VTOL takeoff2Allow FW Land-if bit is not set then NAV_LAND command on quadplanes will instead perform a NAV_VTOL_LAND3Vtol Takeoff Frame-command NAV_VTOL_TAKEOFF alt set by the commandâs reference frame not above current location4Always use FW spiral approach-always use Use a fixed wing spiral approach for VTOL landings5USE QRTL-instead of QLAND for rc failsafe when in VTOL modes6Use Governor-use ICE Idle Governor in MANUAL for forward motor7Force Qassist-on always8Mtrs_Only_Qassist-in tailsitters only uses VTOL motors and not flying surfaces for QASSIST10Disarmed Yaw Tilt-enable motor tilt for yaw when disarmed11Delay Spoolup-delay VTOL spoolup for 2 seconds after arming12Disable speed based Qassist when using synthethic airspeed estimates13Disable Ground Effect Compensation-on baro altitude reports14Ignore forward flight angle limits-in Qmodes and use Q_ANGLE_MAX exclusively15ThrLandControl-enable throttle stick control of landing rate16DisableApproach-disable use of approach and airbrake stages in VTOL landing17EnableLandResposition-enable pilot controlled repositioning in AUTO land.Descent will pause while repositioning18ARMVTOL-arm only in VTOL or AUTO modes19CompleteTransition-to fixed wing if Q_TRANS_FAIL timer times out instead of QLAND20Force RTL mode-forces RTL mode on rc failsafe in VTOL modes overriding bit 5(USE_QRTL)21Tilt rotor-tilt motors up when disarmed in FW modes (except manual) to prevent ground strikes.22Scale FF by the ratio of VTOL to plane angle P gains in Position 1 phase of transition into VTOL flight as well as reducing VTOL angle P based on airspeed.:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Level Transition-keep wings within LEVEL_ROLL_LIMIT and only use forward motor(s) for climb during transition
- **1:** Allow FW Takeoff-if bit is not set then NAV_TAKEOFF command on quadplanes will instead perform a NAV_VTOL takeoff
- **2:** Allow FW Land-if bit is not set then NAV_LAND command on quadplanes will instead perform a NAV_VTOL_LAND
- **3:** Vtol Takeoff Frame-command NAV_VTOL_TAKEOFF alt set by the commandâs reference frame not above current location
- **4:** Always use FW spiral approach-always use Use a fixed wing spiral approach for VTOL landings
- **5:** USE QRTL-instead of QLAND for rc failsafe when in VTOL modes
- **6:** Use Governor-use ICE Idle Governor in MANUAL for forward motor
- **7:** Force Qassist-on always
- **8:** Mtrs_Only_Qassist-in tailsitters only uses VTOL motors and not flying surfaces for QASSIST
- **10:** Disarmed Yaw Tilt-enable motor tilt for yaw when disarmed
- **11:** Delay Spoolup-delay VTOL spoolup for 2 seconds after arming
- **12:** Disable speed based Qassist when using synthethic airspeed estimates
- **13:** Disable Ground Effect Compensation-on baro altitude reports
- **14:** Ignore forward flight angle limits-in Qmodes and use Q_ANGLE_MAX exclusively
- **15:** ThrLandControl-enable throttle stick control of landing rate
- **16:** DisableApproach-disable use of approach and airbrake stages in VTOL landing
- **17:** EnableLandResposition-enable pilot controlled repositioning in AUTO land.Descent will pause while repositioning
- **18:** ARMVTOL-arm only in VTOL or AUTO modes
- **19:** CompleteTransition-to fixed wing if Q_TRANS_FAIL timer times out instead of QLAND
- **20:** Force RTL mode-forces RTL mode on rc failsafe in VTOL modes overriding bit 5(USE_QRTL)
- **21:** Tilt rotor-tilt motors up when disarmed in FW modes (except manual) to prevent ground strikes.
- **22:** Scale FF by the ratio of VTOL to plane angle P gains in Position 1 phase of transition into VTOL flight as well as reducing VTOL angle P based on airspeed.

---

## Q_TRANS_DECEL
**Display Name:** Transition decelerationÂ

**Description:**
This is deceleration rate that will be used in calculating the stopping distance when transitioning from fixed wing flight to multicopter flight.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.2 to 5

---

## Q_TRIM_PITCH
**Display Name:** Quadplane AHRS trim pitchÂ

**Description:**
This sets the compensation for the pitch angle trim difference between calibrated AHRS level and vertical flight pitch. NOTE! this is relative to calibrated AHRS trim, not forward flight trim which includes PTCH_TRIM_DEG. For tailsitters, this is relative to a baseline of 90 degrees in AHRS.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** -10 to +10

---

## Q_FW_LND_APR_RAD
**Display Name:** Quadplane fixed wing landing approach radiusÂ

**Description:**
This provides the radius used, when using a fixed wing landing approach. If set to 0 then the WP_LOITER_RAD will be selected.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **5:** 0 to 200

---

## Q_TRANS_FAIL
**Display Name:** Quadplane transition failure timeÂ

**Description:**
Maximum time allowed for forward transitions, exceeding this time will cancel the transition and the aircraft will immediately change to the mode set by Q_TRANS_FAIL_ACT or finish the transition depending on Q_OPTIONS bit 19. 0 for no limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 20

---

## Q_THROTTLE_EXPO
**Display Name:** Throttle expo strengthÂ

**Description:**
Amount of curvature in throttle curve: 0 is linear, 1 is cubic

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **.1:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_ACRO_RLL_RATE
**Display Name:** QACRO mode roll rateÂ

**Description:**
The maximum roll rate at full stick deflection in QACRO mode

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 10 to 500

---

## Q_ACRO_PIT_RATE
**Display Name:** QACRO mode pitch rateÂ

**Description:**
The maximum pitch rate at full stick deflection in QACRO mode

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 10 to 500

---

## Q_ACRO_YAW_RATE
**Display Name:** QACRO mode yaw rateÂ

**Description:**
The maximum yaw rate at full stick deflection in QACRO mode

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 10 to 500

---

## Q_TKOFF_FAIL_SCL
**Display Name:** Takeoff time failure scalarÂ

**Description:**
Scalar for how long past the expected takeoff time a takeoff should be considered as failed and the vehicle will switch to QLAND. If set to 0 there is no limit on takeoff time.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **5.1:** 1.1 to 5.0

---

## Q_TKOFF_ARSP_LIM
**Display Name:** Takeoff airspeed limitÂ

**Description:**
Airspeed limit during takeoff. If the airspeed exceeds this level the vehicle will switch to QLAND. This is useful for ensuring that you don't takeoff into excessively strong wind. If set to 0 there is no limit on airspeed during takeoff.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 20

---

## Q_ASSIST_ALT
**Display Name:** Quadplane assistance altitudeÂ

**Description:**
This is the altitude below which quadplane assistance will be triggered. This acts the same way as Q_ASSIST_ANGLE and Q_ASSIST_SPEED, but triggers if the aircraft drops below the given altitude while the VTOL motors are not running. A value of zero disables this feature. The altitude is calculated as being above ground level. The height above ground is given from a Lidar used if available and RNGFND_LANDING=1. Otherwise it comes from terrain data if TERRAIN_FOLLOW=1 and comes from height above home otherwise.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 120

---

## Q_ASSIST_DELAY
**Display Name:** Quadplane assistance delayÂ

**Description:**
This is delay between the assistance thresholds being met and the assistance starting.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0 to 2

---

## Q_FWD_MANTHR_MAX
**Display Name:** VTOL manual forward throttle max percentÂ

**Description:**
Maximum value for manual forward throttle; used with RC option FWD_THR (209)


---

## Q_BACKTRANS_MS
**Display Name:** SLT and Tiltrotor back transition pitch limit durationÂ

**Description:**
Pitch angle will increase from 0 to angle max over this duration when switching into VTOL flight in a postion control mode. 0 Disables.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 10000:** milliseconds

---

## Q_TRANS_FAIL_ACT
**Display Name:** Quadplane transition failure actionÂ

**Description:**
This sets the mode that is changed to when Q_TRANS_FAIL time elapses, if set. See also Q_OPTIONS bit 19: CompleteTransition if Q_TRANS_FAIL

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Warn only0QLand1QRTL:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Warn only
- **0:** QLand
- **1:** QRTL

---

## Q_LAND_ALTCHG
**Display Name:** Land detection altitude change thresholdÂ

**Description:**
The maximum altitude change allowed during land detection. You can raise this value if you find that landing detection takes a long time to complete. It is the maximum change in altitude over a period of 4 seconds for landing to be detected

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** 0.1 to 0.6

---

## Q_NAVALT_MIN
**Display Name:** Minimum navigation altitudeÂ

**Description:**
This is the altitude in meters above which navigation begins in auto takeoff. Below this altitude the target roll and pitch will be zero. A value of zero disables the feature


---

## Q_PLT_Y_RATE
**Display Name:** Pilot controlled yaw rateÂ

**Description:**
Pilot controlled yaw rate max. Used in all pilot controlled modes except QAcro

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 360:** degrees per second

---

## Q_PLT_Y_EXPO
**Display Name:** Pilot controlled yaw expoÂ

**Description:**
Pilot controlled yaw expo to allow faster rotation when stick at edges

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Values
- **-0.5 to 1.0:** ValueMeaning0Disabled0.1Very Low0.2Low0.3Medium0.4High0.5Very High
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **0.1:** Very Low
- **0.2:** Low
- **0.3:** Medium
- **0.4:** High
- **0.5:** Very High

---

## Q_PLT_Y_RATE_TC
**Display Name:** Pilot yaw rate control input time constantÂ

**Description:**
Pilot yaw rate control input time constant. Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0.5:** Very Soft
- **0.2:** Soft
- **0.15:** Medium
- **0.1:** Crisp
- **0.05:** Very Crisp

---

## Q_RTL_ALT_MIN
**Display Name:** QRTL minimum altitudeÂ

**Description:**
If VTOL motors are active QRTL mode will VTOL climb to at least this altitude before returning home. If outside 150% the larger of WP_LOITER_RAD and RTL_RADIUS the vehicle will VTOL climb to Q_RTL_ALT. This parameter has no effect if the vehicle is in forward flight. Should be between Q_LAND_FINAL_ALT and Q_RTL_ALT

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 200

---

## Q_FWD_THR_GAIN
**Display Name:** Q mode fwd throttle gainÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the gain from forward accel/tilt to forward throttle in certain Q modes. The Q modes this feature operates in is controlled by the Q_FWD_THR_USE parameter. Vehicles using separate forward thrust motors, eg quadplanes, should set this parameter to (all up weight) / (maximum combined thrust of forward motors) with a value of 2 being typical. Vehicles that tilt lifting rotors to provide forward thrust should set this parameter to (all up weight) / (weight lifted by tilting rotors) which for most aircraft can be approximated as (total number of lifting rotors) / (number of lifting rotors that tilt). When using this method of forward throttle control, the forward tilt angle limit is controlled by the Q_FWD_PIT_LIM parameter.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.0 to 5.0

---

## Q_FWD_PIT_LIM
**Display Name:** Q mode forward pitch limitÂ

**Description:**
When forward throttle is being controlled by the Q_FWD_THR_GAIN parameter in Q modes, the vehicle forward (nose down) pitch rotation will be limited to the value specified by this parameter and the any additional forward acceleration required will be produced by use of the forward thrust motor(s) or tilting of moveable rotors. Larger values allow the vehicle to pitch more nose down. Set initially to the amount of nose down pitch required to remove wing lift.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.0 to 5.0

---

## Q_FWD_THR_USE
**Display Name:** Q mode forward throttle useÂ

**Description:**
This parameter determines when the feature that uses forward throttle instead of forward tilt is used. The amount of forward throttle is controlled by the Q_FWD_THR_GAIN parameter. The maximum amount of forward pitch allowed is controlled by the Q_FWD_PIT_LIM parameter. Q_FWD_THR_USE = 0 disables the feature. Q_FWD_THR_USE = 1 enables the feature in all position controlled modes such as QLOITER, QLAND, QRTL and VTOL TAKEOFF. Q_FWD_THR_USE = 2 enables the feature in all Q modes except QAUTOTUNE and QACRO. When enabling the feature, the legacy method of controlling forward throttle use via velocity controller error should be disabled by setting Q_VFWD_GAIN to 0. Do not use this feature with tailsitters.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Off1On in all position controlled Q modes2On in all Q modes except QAUTOTUNE and QACRO:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Off
- **1:** On in all position controlled Q modes
- **2:** On in all Q modes except QAUTOTUNE and QACRO

---

## Q_BCK_PIT_LIM
**Display Name:** Q mode rearward pitch limitÂ

**Description:**
This sets the maximum number of degrees of back or pitch up in Q modes when the airspeed is at AIRSPEED_MIN, and is used to prevent excessive sutructural loads when pitching up decelerate. If airspeed is above or below AIRSPEED_MIN, the pitch up/back will be adjusted according to the formula pitch_limit = Q_BCK_PIT_LIM * (AIRSPEED_MIN / IAS)^2. The backwards/up pitch limit controlled by this parameter is in addition to limiting applied by PTCH_LIM_MAX_DEG and Q_ANGLE_MAX. The BCK_PIT_LIM limit is only applied when Q_FWD_THR_USE is set to 1 or 2 and the vehicle is flying in a mode that uses forward throttle instead of forward tilt to generate forward speed. Set to a non positive value 0 to deactivate this limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.0 to 15.0

---

## Q_APPROACH_DIST
**Display Name:** Q mode approach distanceÂ

**Description:**
The minimum distance from the destination to use the fixed wing airbrake and approach code for landing approach. This is useful if you don't want the fixed wing approach logic to be used when you are close to the destination. Set to zero to always use fixed wing approach.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0.0 to 1000

---

## Q_AUTOTUNE_AXES
**Display Name:** Autotune axis bitmaskÂ

**Description:**
1-byte bitmap of axes to autotune

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Roll1Pitch2Yaw3YawD:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Roll
- **1:** Pitch
- **2:** Yaw
- **3:** YawD

---

## Q_AUTOTUNE_AGGR
**Display Name:** Autotune aggressivenessÂ

**Description:**
Autotune aggressiveness. Defines the bounce back used to detect size of the D term.


---

## Q_AUTOTUNE_MIN_D
**Display Name:** AutoTune minimum DÂ

**Description:**
Defines the minimum D gain


---

## Q_A_SLEW_YAW
**Display Name:** Yaw target slew rateÂ

**Description:**
Maximum rate the yaw target can be updated in RTL and Auto flight modes

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **100:** 500 to 18000

---

**Display Name:** Acceleration Max for YawÂ

Maximum acceleration in yaw axis

**Specifications:**

- **1000:** 0 to 72000
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **9000:** VerySlow
- **18000:** Slow
- **36000:** Medium
- **54000:** Fast

---

## Q_A_RATE_FF_ENAB
**Display Name:** Rate Feedforward EnableÂ

**Description:**
Controls whether body-frame rate feedforward is enabled or disabled

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## Q_A_ACCEL_R_MAX
**Display Name:** Acceleration Max for RollÂ

**Description:**
Maximum acceleration in roll axis

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1000:** 0 to 180000
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **30000:** VerySlow
- **72000:** Slow
- **108000:** Medium
- **162000:** Fast

---

## Q_A_ACCEL_P_MAX
**Display Name:** Acceleration Max for PitchÂ

**Description:**
Maximum acceleration in pitch axis

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1000:** 0 to 180000
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **30000:** VerySlow
- **72000:** Slow
- **108000:** Medium
- **162000:** Fast

---

## Q_A_ANGLE_BOOST
**Display Name:** Angle BoostÂ

**Description:**
Angle Boost increases output throttle as the vehicle leans to reduce loss of altitude

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## Q_A_ANG_RLL_P
**Display Name:** Roll axis angle controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired roll angle and actual angle to a desired roll rate


---

## Q_A_ANG_PIT_P
**Display Name:** Pitch axis angle controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired pitch angle and actual angle to a desired pitch rate

## Q_A_ANG_YAW_P
**Display Name:** Yaw axis angle controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired yaw angle and actual angle to a desired yaw rate
