## Q_A_ANG_LIM_TC
**Display Name:** Angle Limit (to maintain altitude) Time ConstantÂ

**Description:**
Angle Limit (to maintain altitude) Time Constant


---

## Q_A_RATE_R_MAX
**Display Name:** Angular Velocity Max for RollÂ

**Description:**
Maximum angular velocity in roll axis

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 1080
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **60:** Slow
- **180:** Medium
- **360:** Fast

---

## Q_A_RATE_P_MAX
**Display Name:** Angular Velocity Max for PitchÂ

**Description:**
Maximum angular velocity in pitch axis

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 1080
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **60:** Slow
- **180:** Medium
- **360:** Fast

---

## Q_A_RATE_Y_MAX
**Display Name:** Angular Velocity Max for YawÂ

**Description:**
Maximum angular velocity in yaw axis

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 1080
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **60:** Slow
- **180:** Medium
- **360:** Fast

---

## Q_A_INPUT_TC
**Display Name:** Attitude control input time constantÂ

**Description:**
Attitude control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0.5:** Very Soft
- **0.2:** Soft
- **0.15:** Medium
- **0.1:** Crisp
- **0.05:** Very Crisp

---

## Q_A_LAND_R_MULT
**Display Name:** Landed roll gain multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Roll gain multiplier active when landed. A factor of 1.0 means no reduction in gain while landed. Reduce this factor to reduce ground oscitation in the roll axis.


---

## Q_A_LAND_P_MULT
**Display Name:** Landed pitch gain multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Pitch gain multiplier active when landed. A factor of 1.0 means no reduction in gain while landed. Reduce this factor to reduce ground oscitation in the pitch axis.


---

## Q_A_LAND_Y_MULT
**Display Name:** Landed yaw gain multiplierÂ

**Description:**
Yaw gain multiplier active when landed. A factor of 1.0 means no reduction in gain while landed. Reduce this factor to reduce ground oscitation in the yaw axis.


---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_P
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.005:** 0.01 to 0.5

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_I
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller I gainÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0.01 to 2.0

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_IMAX
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller I gain maximumÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_D
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller D gainÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0.0 to 0.05

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_FF
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller feed forwardÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller feed forward

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 0.5

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_FLTT
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller target frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 100

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_FLTE
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller error frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 100

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_FLTD
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 100

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_SMAX
**Display Name:** Roll slew rate limitÂ

**Description:**
Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.5:** 0 to 200

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_PDMX
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximumÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_D_FF
**Display Name:** Roll Derivative FeedForward GainÂ

**Description:**
FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.0001:** 0 to 0.02

---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_NTF
**Display Name:** Roll Target notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Roll Target notch filter index


---

## Q_A_RAT_RLL_NEF
**Display Name:** Roll Error notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Roll Error notch filter index


---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_P
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.005:** 0.01 to 0.50

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_I
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller I gainÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0.01 to 2.0

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_IMAX
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximumÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_D
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller D gainÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0.0 to 0.05

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_FF
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller feed forwardÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller feed forward

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 0.5

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_FLTT
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 100

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_FLTE
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 100

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_FLTD
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 100

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_SMAX
**Display Name:** Pitch slew rate limitÂ

**Description:**
Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.5:** 0 to 200

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_PDMX
**Display Name:** Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximumÂ

**Description:**
Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_D_FF
**Display Name:** Pitch Derivative FeedForward GainÂ

**Description:**
FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.0001:** 0 to 0.02

---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_NTF
**Display Name:** Pitch Target notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Pitch Target notch filter index


---

## Q_A_RAT_PIT_NEF
**Display Name:** Pitch Error notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Pitch Error notch filter index


---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_P
**Display Name:** Yaw axis rate controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.005:** 0.10 to 2.50

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_I
**Display Name:** Yaw axis rate controller I gainÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0.010 to 1.0

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_IMAX
**Display Name:** Yaw axis rate controller I gain maximumÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_D
**Display Name:** Yaw axis rate controller D gainÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0.000 to 0.02

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_FF
**Display Name:** Yaw axis rate controller feed forwardÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis rate controller feed forward

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 0.5

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_FLTT
**Display Name:** Yaw axis rate controller target frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 50

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_FLTE
**Display Name:** Yaw axis rate controller error frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 20

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_FLTD
**Display Name:** Yaw axis rate controller derivative frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 50

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_SMAX
**Display Name:** Yaw slew rate limitÂ

**Description:**
Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.5:** 0 to 200

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_PDMX
**Display Name:** Yaw axis rate controller PD sum maximumÂ

**Description:**
Yaw axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_D_FF
**Display Name:** Yaw Derivative FeedForward GainÂ

**Description:**
FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.0001:** 0 to 0.02

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_NTF
**Display Name:** Yaw Target notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Yaw Target notch filter index

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 8:** hertz

---

## Q_A_RAT_YAW_NEF
**Display Name:** Yaw Error notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Yaw Error notch filter index


---

## Q_A_THR_MIX_MIN
**Display Name:** Throttle Mix MinimumÂ

**Description:**
Throttle vs attitude control prioritisation used when landing (higher values mean we prioritise attitude control over throttle)


---

## Q_A_THR_MIX_MAX
**Display Name:** Throttle Mix MaximumÂ

**Description:**
Throttle vs attitude control prioritisation used during active flight (higher values mean we prioritise attitude control over throttle)


---

## Q_A_THR_MIX_MAN
**Display Name:** Throttle Mix ManualÂ

**Description:**
Throttle vs attitude control prioritisation used during manual flight (higher values mean we prioritise attitude control over throttle)


---

## Q_A_THR_G_BOOST
**Display Name:** Throttle-gain boostÂ

**Description:**
Throttle-gain boost ratio. A value of 0 means no boosting is applied, a value of 1 means full boosting is applied. Describes the ratio increase that is applied to angle P and PD on pitch and roll.


---

## Q_LOIT_ANG_MAX
**Display Name:** Loiter pilot angle maxÂ

**Description:**
Loiter maximum pilot requested lean angle. Set to zero for 2/3 of Q_P_ANGLE_MAX/Q_ANGLE_MAX. The maximum vehicle lean angle is still limited by Q_P_ANGLE_MAX/Q_ANGLE_MAX

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 45

---

## Q_LOIT_SPEED
**Display Name:** Loiter Horizontal Maximum SpeedÂ

**Description:**
Defines the maximum speed in cm/s which the aircraft will travel horizontally while in loiter mode

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **50:** 20 to 3500

---

## Q_LOIT_ACC_MAX
**Display Name:** Loiter maximum correction accelerationÂ

**Description:**
Loiter maximum correction acceleration in cm/s/s.  Higher values cause the copter to correct position errors more aggressively.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 100 to 981

---

## Q_LOIT_BRK_ACCEL
**Display Name:** Loiter braking accelerationÂ

**Description:**
Loiter braking acceleration in cm/s/s. Higher values stop the copter more quickly when the stick is centered.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 25 to 250

---

## Q_LOIT_BRK_JERK
**Display Name:** Loiter braking jerkÂ

**Description:**
Loiter braking jerk in cm/s/s/s. Higher values will remove braking faster if the pilot moves the sticks during a braking maneuver.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 500 to 5000

---

## Q_LOIT_BRK_DELAY
**Display Name:** Loiter brake start delay (in seconds)Â

**Description:**
Loiter brake start delay (in seconds)

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0 to 2

---

## Q_M_YAW_HEADROOM
**Display Name:** Matrix Yaw MinÂ

**Description:**
Yaw control is given at least this pwm in microseconds range

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 500:** PWM in microseconds

---

## Q_M_THST_EXPO
**Display Name:** Thrust Curve ExpoÂ

**Description:**
Motor thrust curve exponent (0.0 for linear to 1.0 for second order curve)


---

## Q_M_SPIN_MAX
**Display Name:** Motor Spin maximumÂ

**Description:**
Point at which the thrust saturates expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0.9Low0.95Default1.0High:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0.9:** Low
- **0.95:** Default
- **1.0:** High

---

## Q_M_BAT_VOLT_MAX
**Display Name:** Battery voltage compensation maximum voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage compensation maximum voltage (voltage above this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 4.2 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **6 to 53:** volt

---

## Q_M_BAT_VOLT_MIN
**Display Name:** Battery voltage compensation minimum voltageÂ

**Description:**
Battery voltage compensation minimum voltage (voltage below this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 3.3 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **6 to 42:** volt

---

## Q_M_BAT_CURR_MAX
**Display Name:** Motor Current MaxÂ

**Description:**
Maximum current over which maximum throttle is limited (0 = Disabled)

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 200:** ampere

---

## Q_M_PWM_TYPE
**Display Name:** Output PWM typeÂ

**Description:**
This selects the output PWM type, allowing for normal PWM continuous output, OneShot, brushed or DShot motor output.PWMRange and PWMAngle are PWM special/rare cases for ESCs that dont calibrate normally (some Sub motors) or where each ESC must have its PWM range set individually using the Servo params instead of PWM_MIN/MAX parameters.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Normal1OneShot2OneShot1253Brushed4DShot1505DShot3006DShot6007DShot12008PWMRange9PWMAngle:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Normal
- **1:** OneShot
- **2:** OneShot125
- **3:** Brushed
- **4:** DShot150
- **5:** DShot300
- **6:** DShot600
- **7:** DShot1200
- **8:** PWMRange
- **9:** PWMAngle

---

## Q_M_PWM_MIN
**Display Name:** PWM output minimumÂ

**Description:**
This sets the min PWM output value in microseconds that will ever be output to the motors

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 2000:** PWM in microseconds

---

## Q_M_PWM_MAX
**Display Name:** PWM output maximumÂ

**Description:**
This sets the max PWM value in microseconds that will ever be output to the motors

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 2000:** PWM in microseconds

---

## Q_M_SPIN_MIN
**Display Name:** Motor Spin minimumÂ

**Description:**
Point at which the thrust starts expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.  Should be higher than MOT_SPIN_ARM.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0.0Low0.15Default0.25High:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0.0:** Low
- **0.15:** Default
- **0.25:** High

---

## Q_M_SPIN_ARM
**Display Name:** Motor Spin armedÂ

**Description:**
Point at which the motors start to spin expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.  Should be lower than MOT_SPIN_MIN.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0.0Low0.1Default0.2High:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0.0:** Low
- **0.1:** Default
- **0.2:** High

---

## Q_M_BAT_CURR_TC
**Display Name:** Motor Current Max Time ConstantÂ

**Description:**
Time constant used to limit the maximum current

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 10:** seconds

---

## Q_M_THST_HOVER
**Display Name:** Thrust Hover ValueÂ

**Description:**
Motor thrust needed to hover expressed as a number from 0 to 1


---

## Q_M_HOVER_LEARN
**Display Name:** Hover Value LearningÂ

**Description:**
Enable/Disable automatic learning of hover throttle

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Learn2Learn and Save:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Learn
- **2:** Learn and Save

---

## Q_M_SAFE_DISARM
**Display Name:** Motor PWM output disabled when disarmedÂ

**Description:**
Disables motor PWM output when disarmed

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0PWM enabled while disarmed1PWM disabled while disarmed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** PWM enabled while disarmed
- **1:** PWM disabled while disarmed

---

## Q_M_YAW_SV_ANGLE
**Display Name:** Yaw Servo Max Lean AngleÂ

**Description:**
Yaw servo's maximum lean angle (Tricopter only)

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 80

---

## Q_M_SPOOL_TIME
**Display Name:** Spool up timeÂ

**Description:**
Time in seconds to spool up the motors from zero to min throttle.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.05 to 2

---

## Q_M_BOOST_SCALE
**Display Name:** Motor boost scaleÂ

**Description:**
Booster motor output scaling factor vs main throttle.  The output to the BoostThrottle servo will be the main throttle times this scaling factor. A higher scaling factor will put more of the load on the booster motor. A value of 1 will set the BoostThrottle equal to the main throttle.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0 to 5

---

## Q_M_BAT_IDX
**Display Name:** Battery compensation indexÂ

**Description:**
Which battery monitor should be used for doing compensation

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0First battery1Second battery:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** First battery
- **1:** Second battery

---

## Q_M_SLEW_UP_TIME
**Display Name:** Output slew time for increasing throttleÂ

**Description:**
Time in seconds to slew output from zero to full. This is used to limit the rate at which output can change. Range is constrained between 0 and 0.5.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to .5

---

## Q_M_SLEW_DN_TIME
**Display Name:** Output slew time for decreasing throttleÂ

**Description:**
Time in seconds to slew output from full to zero. This is used to limit the rate at which output can change.  Range is constrained between 0 and 0.5.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to .5

---

## Q_M_SAFE_TIME
**Display Name:** Time taken to disable and enable the motor PWM output when disarmed and armed.Â

**Description:**
Time taken to disable and enable the motor PWM output when disarmed and armed.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 5

---

## Q_M_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Motor optionsÂ

**Description:**
Motor options

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Voltage compensation uses raw voltage:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Voltage compensation uses raw voltage

---

## Q_M_SPOOL_TIM_DN
**Display Name:** Spool down timeÂ

**Description:**
Time taken to spool down the motors from min to zero throttle. If set to 0 then SPOOL_TIME is used instead.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 2

---

## Q_P_ACC_XY_FILT
**Display Name:** XY Acceleration filter cutoff frequencyÂ

**Description:**
Lower values will slow the response of the navigation controller and reduce twitchiness

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.5 to 5

---

## Q_P_POSZ_P
**Display Name:** Position (vertical) controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Position (vertical) controller P gain.  Converts the difference between the desired altitude and actual altitude into a climb or descent rate which is passed to the throttle rate controller


---

## Q_P_VELZ_P
**Display Name:** Velocity (vertical) controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (vertical) controller P gain.  Converts the difference between desired vertical speed and actual speed into a desired acceleration that is passed to the throttle acceleration controller


---

## Q_P_VELZ_I
**Display Name:** Velocity (vertical) controller I gainÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (vertical) controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired velocity to a target acceleration

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0.02 to 1.00

---

## Q_P_VELZ_IMAX
**Display Name:** Velocity (vertical) controller I gain maximumÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (vertical) controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the target acceleration that the I gain will output


---

## Q_P_VELZ_D
**Display Name:** Velocity (vertical) controller D gainÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (vertical) controller D gain.  Corrects short-term changes in velocity

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0.00 to 1.00

---

## Q_P_VELZ_FF
**Display Name:** Velocity (vertical) controller Feed Forward gainÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (vertical) controller Feed Forward gain.  Produces an output that is proportional to the magnitude of the target

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_P_VELZ_FLTE
**Display Name:** Velocity (vertical) error filterÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (vertical) error filter.  This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for P and I terms

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 100:** hertz

---

## Q_P_VELZ_FLTD
**Display Name:** Velocity (vertical) input filter for D termÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (vertical) input filter for D term.  This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for D terms

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 100:** hertz

---

## Q_P_ACCZ_P
**Display Name:** Acceleration (vertical) controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Acceleration (vertical) controller P gain.  Converts the difference between desired vertical acceleration and actual acceleration into a motor output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.05:** 0.200 to 1.500

---

## Q_P_ACCZ_I
**Display Name:** Acceleration (vertical) controller I gainÂ

**Description:**
Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired vertical acceleration and actual acceleration


---

## Q_P_ACCZ_IMAX
**Display Name:** Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain maximumÂ

**Description:**
Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum pwm that the I term will generate

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 1000:** decipercent

---

## Q_P_ACCZ_D
**Display Name:** Acceleration (vertical) controller D gainÂ

**Description:**
Acceleration (vertical) controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired vertical acceleration vs actual acceleration


---

## Q_P_ACCZ_FF
**Display Name:** Acceleration (vertical) controller feed forwardÂ

**Description:**
Acceleration (vertical) controller feed forward

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 0.5

---

## Q_P_ACCZ_FLTT
**Display Name:** Acceleration (vertical) controller target frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Acceleration (vertical) controller target frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 50

---

## Q_P_ACCZ_FLTE
**Display Name:** Acceleration (vertical) controller error frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Acceleration (vertical) controller error frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 100

---

## Q_P_ACCZ_FLTD
**Display Name:** Acceleration (vertical) controller derivative frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Acceleration (vertical) controller derivative frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 100

---

## Q_P_ACCZ_SMAX
**Display Name:** Accel (vertical) slew rate limitÂ

**Description:**
Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.5:** 0 to 200

**Display Name:** Acceleration (vertical) controller PD sum maximumÂ

**Description:**
Acceleration (vertical) controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 1000:** decipercent

**Display Name:** Accel (vertical) Derivative FeedForward GainÂ

FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

- **Increment:** Range

Position controller P gain.  Converts the distance (in the latitude direction) to the target location into a desired speed which is then passed to the loiter latitude rate controller

**Display Name:** Velocity (horizontal) P gainÂ

Velocity (horizontal) P gain.  Converts the difference between desired and actual velocity to a target acceleration

- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.1 to 6.0

---

## Q_P_VELXY_I
**Display Name:** Velocity (horizontal) I gainÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (horizontal) I gain.  Corrects long-term difference between desired and actual velocity to a target acceleration

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0.02 to 1.00

---

## Q_P_VELXY_D
**Display Name:** Velocity (horizontal) D gainÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (horizontal) D gain.  Corrects short-term changes in velocity

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0.00 to 1.00

**Display Name:** Velocity (horizontal) integrator maximumÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (horizontal) integrator maximum.  Constrains the target acceleration that the I gain will output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **10:** 0 to 4500

---

## Q_P_VELXY_FLTE
**Display Name:** Velocity (horizontal) input filterÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (horizontal) input filter.  This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for P and I terms

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 100:** hertz

---

## Q_P_VELXY_FLTD
**Display Name:** Velocity (horizontal) input filterÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (horizontal) input filter.  This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for D term

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 100:** hertz

---

## Q_P_VELXY_FF
**Display Name:** Velocity (horizontal) feed forward gainÂ

**Description:**
Velocity (horizontal) feed forward gain.  Converts the difference between desired velocity to a target acceleration

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 6

## Q_P_ANGLE_MAX
**Display Name:** Position Control Angle MaxÂ

**Description:**
Maximum lean angle autopilot can request.  Set to zero to use ANGLE_MAX parameter value

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 45

---

## Q_P_JERK_XY
**Display Name:** Jerk limit for the horizontal kinematic input shapingÂ

**Description:**
Jerk limit of the horizontal kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the aircraft varies the acceleration target

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 20

---

## Q_P_JERK_Z
**Display Name:** Jerk limit for the vertical kinematic input shapingÂ

**Description:**
Jerk limit of the vertical kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the aircraft varies the acceleration target

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 50

---

## Q_TAILSIT_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable TailsitterÂ

**Description:**
This enables Tailsitter functionality. A value of 2 forces Qassist active and always stabilize in forward flight with airmode for stabilisation at 0 throttle, for use on vehicles with no control surfaces, vehicle will not arm in forward flight modes, see also Q_OPTIONS "Mtrs_Only_Qassist"

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disable1Enable2Enable Always:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disable
- **1:** Enable
- **2:** Enable Always

---

## Q_TAILSIT_ANGLE
**Display Name:** Tailsitter fixed wing transition angleÂ

**Description:**
This is the pitch angle at which tailsitter aircraft will change from VTOL control to fixed wing control.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **5 to 80:** degrees

---

## Q_TAILSIT_ANG_VT
**Display Name:** Tailsitter VTOL transition angleÂ

**Description:**
This is the pitch angle at which tailsitter aircraft will change from fixed wing control to VTOL control, if zero Q_TAILSIT_ANGLE will be used

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **5 to 80:** degrees

---

## Q_TAILSIT_INPUT
**Display Name:** Tailsitter input type bitmaskÂ

**Description:**
This controls whether stick input when hovering as a tailsitter follows the conventions for fixed wing hovering or multicopter hovering. When PlaneMode is not enabled (bit0 = 0) the roll stick will roll the aircraft in earth frame and yaw stick will yaw in earth frame. When PlaneMode input is enabled, the roll and yaw sticks are swapped so that the roll stick controls earth-frame yaw and rudder controls earth-frame roll. When body-frame roll is enabled (bit1 = 1), the yaw stick controls earth-frame yaw rate and the roll stick controls roll in the tailsitter's body frame when flying level.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0PlaneMode1BodyFrameRoll:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** PlaneMode
- **1:** BodyFrameRoll

---

## Q_TAILSIT_VFGAIN
**Display Name:** Tailsitter vector thrust gain in forward flightÂ

**Description:**
This controls the amount of vectored thrust control used in forward flight for a vectored tailsitter

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_TAILSIT_VHGAIN
**Display Name:** Tailsitter vector thrust gain in hoverÂ

**Description:**
This controls the amount of vectored thrust control used in hover for a vectored tailsitter

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## Q_TAILSIT_VHPOW
**Display Name:** Tailsitter vector thrust gain powerÂ

**Description:**
This controls the amount of extra pitch given to the vectored control when at high pitch errors

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0 to 4

---

## Q_TAILSIT_GSCMAX
**Display Name:** Maximum tailsitter gain scalingÂ

**Description:**
Maximum gain scaling for tailsitter Q_TAILSIT_GSCMSK options


---

## Q_TAILSIT_RLL_MX
**Display Name:** Maximum Roll angleÂ

**Description:**
Maximum Allowed roll angle for tailsitters. If this is zero then Q_ANGLE_MAX is used.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 80:** degrees

---

## Q_TAILSIT_MOTMX
**Display Name:** Tailsitter motor maskÂ

**Description:**
Bitmask of motors to remain active in forward flight for a 'Copter' tailsitter. Non-zero indicates airframe is a Copter tailsitter and uses copter style motor layouts determined by Q_FRAME_CLASS and Q_FRAME_TYPE. This should be zero for non-Copter tailsitters.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Motor 11Motor 22Motor 33Motor 44Motor 55Motor 66Motor 77Motor 88Motor 99Motor 1010Motor 1111Motor 12:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Motor 1
- **1:** Motor 2
- **2:** Motor 3
- **3:** Motor 4
- **4:** Motor 5
- **5:** Motor 6
- **6:** Motor 7
- **7:** Motor 8
- **8:** Motor 9
- **9:** Motor 10
- **10:** Motor 11
- **11:** Motor 12

---

## Q_TAILSIT_GSCMSK
**Display Name:** Tailsitter gain scaling maskÂ

**Description:**
Bitmask of gain scaling methods to be applied: Throttle: scale gains with throttle, ATT_THR: reduce gain at high throttle/tilt, 2:Disk theory velocity calculation, requires Q_TAILSIT_DSKLD to be set, ATT_THR must not be set, 3:Altitude correction, scale with air density

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Throttle1ATT_THR2Disk Theory3Altitude correction:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Throttle
- **1:** ATT_THR
- **2:** Disk Theory
- **3:** Altitude correction

---

## Q_TAILSIT_GSCMIN
**Display Name:** Minimum tailsitter gain scalingÂ

**Description:**
Minimum gain scaling for tailsitter Q_TAILSIT_GSCMSK options


---

## Q_TAILSIT_DSKLD
**Display Name:** Tailsitter disk loadingÂ

**Description:**
This is the vehicle weight in kg divided by the total disk area of all propellers in m^2. Only used with Q_TAILSIT_GSCMSK = 4

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 50:** kilograms per square meter

---

## Q_TAILSIT_RAT_FW
**Display Name:** Tailsitter VTOL to forward flight transition rateÂ

**Description:**
The pitch rate at which tailsitter aircraft will pitch down in the transition from VTOL to forward flight

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **10 to 500:** degrees per second

---

## Q_TAILSIT_RAT_VT
**Display Name:** Tailsitter forward flight to VTOL transition rateÂ

**Description:**
The pitch rate at which tailsitter aircraft will pitch up in the transition from forward flight to VTOL

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **10 to 500:** degrees per second

---

## Q_TAILSIT_THR_VT
**Display Name:** Tailsitter forward flight to VTOL transition throttleÂ

**Description:**
Throttle used during FW->VTOL transition, -1 uses hover throttle

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **-1 to 100:** percent

---

## Q_TAILSIT_VT_R_P
**Display Name:** Tailsitter VTOL control surface roll gainÂ

**Description:**
Scale from PID output to control surface, for use where a single axis is actuated by both motors and Tilt/control surface on a copter style tailsitter, increase to favor control surfaces and reduce motor output by reducing gains


---

## Q_TAILSIT_VT_P_P
**Display Name:** Tailsitter VTOL control surface pitch gainÂ

**Description:**
Scale from PID output to control surface, for use where a single axis is actuated by both motors and Tilt/control surface on a copter style tailsitter, increase to favor control surfaces and reduce motor output by reducing gains


---

## Q_TAILSIT_VT_Y_P
**Display Name:** Tailsitter VTOL control surface yaw gainÂ

**Description:**
Scale from PID output to control surface, for use where a single axis is actuated by both motors and Tilt/control surface on a copter style tailsitter, increase to favor control surfaces and reduce motor output by reducing gains


---

## Q_TAILSIT_MIN_VO
**Display Name:** Tailsitter Disk loading minimum outflow speedÂ

**Description:**
Use in conjunction with disk theory gain scaling and Q_TAILSIT_DSKLD to specify minumum airspeed over control surfaces, this will be used to boost throttle, when descending for example, 0 disables


---

## Q_TILT_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable Tiltrotor functionalityÂ

**Description:**
This enables Tiltrotor functionality

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disable1Enable:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disable
- **1:** Enable

---

## Q_TILT_MASK
**Display Name:** Tiltrotor maskÂ

**Description:**
This is a bitmask of motors that are tiltable in a tiltrotor (or tiltwing). The mask is in terms of the standard motor order for the frame type.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Motor 11Motor 22Motor 33Motor 44Motor 55Motor 66Motor 77Motor 88Motor 99Motor 1010Motor 1111Motor 12:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Motor 1
- **1:** Motor 2
- **2:** Motor 3
- **3:** Motor 4
- **4:** Motor 5
- **5:** Motor 6
- **6:** Motor 7
- **7:** Motor 8
- **8:** Motor 9
- **9:** Motor 10
- **10:** Motor 11
- **11:** Motor 12

---

## Q_TILT_RATE_UP
**Display Name:** Tiltrotor upwards tilt rateÂ

**Description:**
This is the maximum speed at which the motor angle will change for a tiltrotor when moving from forward flight to hover

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 10 to 300

---

## Q_TILT_MAX
**Display Name:** Tiltrotor maximum VTOL angleÂ

**Description:**
This is the maximum angle of the tiltable motors at which multicopter control will be enabled. Beyond this angle the plane will fly solely as a fixed wing aircraft and the motors will tilt to their maximum angle at the TILT_RATE

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 20 to 80

---

## Q_TILT_TYPE
**Display Name:** Tiltrotor typeÂ

**Description:**
This is the type of tiltrotor when TILT_MASK is non-zero. A continuous tiltrotor can tilt the rotors to any angle on demand. A binary tiltrotor assumes a retract style servo where the servo is either fully forward or fully up. In both cases the servo can't move faster than Q_TILT_RATE. A vectored yaw tiltrotor will use the tilt of the motors to control yaw in hover, Bicopter tiltrotor must use the tailsitter frame class (10)

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Continuous1Binary2VectoredYaw3Bicopter:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Continuous
- **1:** Binary
- **2:** VectoredYaw
- **3:** Bicopter

---

## Q_TILT_RATE_DN
**Display Name:** Tiltrotor downwards tilt rateÂ

**Description:**
This is the maximum speed at which the motor angle will change for a tiltrotor when moving from hover to forward flight. When this is zero the Q_TILT_RATE_UP value is used.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 10 to 300

---

## Q_TILT_YAW_ANGLE
**Display Name:** Tilt minimum angle for vectored yawÂ

**Description:**
This is the angle of the tilt servos when in VTOL mode and at minimum output (fully back). This needs to be set in addition to Q_TILT_TYPE=2, to enable vectored control for yaw in tilt quadplanes. This is also used to limit the forward travel of bicopter tilts(Q_TILT_TYPE=3) when in VTOL modes.


---

## Q_TILT_FIX_ANGLE
**Display Name:** Fixed wing tiltrotor angleÂ

**Description:**
This is the angle the motors tilt down when at maximum output for forward flight. Set this to a non-zero value to enable vectoring for roll/pitch in forward flight on tilt-vectored aircraft

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 30:** degrees

---

## Q_TILT_FIX_GAIN
**Display Name:** Fixed wing tiltrotor gainÂ

**Description:**
This is the gain for use of tilting motors in fixed wing flight for tilt vectored quadplanes


---

## Q_TILT_WING_FLAP
**Display Name:** Tiltrotor tilt angle that will be used as flapÂ

**Description:**
For use on tilt wings, the wing will tilt up to this angle for flap, transition will be complete when the wing reaches this angle from the forward fight position, 0 disables

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 15

---

## Q_WP_SPEED
**Display Name:** Waypoint Horizontal Speed TargetÂ

**Description:**
Defines the speed in cm/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain horizontally during a WP mission

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **50:** 10 to 2000

---

## Q_WP_RADIUS
**Display Name:** Waypoint RadiusÂ

**Description:**
Defines the distance from a waypoint, that when crossed indicates the wp has been hit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 1000

---

## Q_WP_SPEED_UP
**Display Name:** Waypoint Climb Speed TargetÂ

**Description:**
Defines the speed in cm/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain while climbing during a WP mission

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **50:** 10 to 1000

---

## Q_WP_SPEED_DN
**Display Name:** Waypoint Descent Speed TargetÂ

**Description:**
Defines the speed in cm/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain while descending during a WP mission

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **10:** 10 to 500

---

## Q_WP_ACCEL
**Display Name:** Waypoint AccelerationÂ

**Description:**
Defines the horizontal acceleration in cm/s/s used during missions

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **10:** 50 to 500

---

## Q_WP_ACCEL_Z
**Display Name:** Waypoint Vertical AccelerationÂ

**Description:**
Defines the vertical acceleration in cm/s/s used during missions

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **10:** 50 to 500

---

## Q_WP_RFND_USE
**Display Name:** Waypoint missions use rangefinder for terrain followingÂ

**Description:**
This controls if waypoint missions use rangefinder for terrain following

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disable1Enable:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disable
- **1:** Enable

---

## Q_WP_JERK
**Display Name:** Waypoint JerkÂ

**Description:**
Defines the horizontal jerk in m/s/s used during missions

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **1 to 20:** meters per cubic second

---

## Q_WP_TER_MARGIN
**Display Name:** Waypoint Terrain following altitude marginÂ

**Description:**
Waypoint Terrain following altitude margin.  Vehicle will stop if distance from target altitude is larger than this margin (in meters)

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.1 to 100:** meters

---

## Q_WP_ACCEL_C
**Display Name:** Waypoint Cornering AccelerationÂ

**Description:**
Defines the maximum cornering acceleration in cm/s/s used during missions.  If zero uses 2x accel value.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **10:** 0 to 500

---

## Q_WVANE_ENABLE
**Display Name:** EnableÂ

**Description:**
Enable weather vaning.  When active, the aircraft will automatically yaw into wind when in a VTOL position controlled mode. Pilot yaw commands override the weathervaning action.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Only use during takeoffs or landing see weathervane takeoff and land override parameters0Disabled1Nose into wind2Nose or tail into wind3Side into wind4tail into wind:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Only use during takeoffs or landing see weathervane takeoff and land override parameters
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Nose into wind
- **2:** Nose or tail into wind
- **3:** Side into wind
- **4:** tail into wind

---

## Q_WVANE_GAIN
**Display Name:** Weathervaning gainÂ

**Description:**
This converts the target roll/pitch angle of the aircraft into the correcting (into wind) yaw rate. e.g. Gain = 2, roll = 30 deg, pitch = 0 deg, yaw rate = 60 deg/s.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.5 to 4

---

## Q_WVANE_ANG_MIN
**Display Name:** Weathervaning min angleÂ

**Description:**
The minimum target roll/pitch angle before active weathervaning will start.  This provides a dead zone that is particularly useful for poorly trimmed quadplanes.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0 to 10

---

## Q_WVANE_HGT_MIN
**Display Name:** Weathervaning min heightÂ

**Description:**
Above this height weathervaning is permitted.  If RNGFND_LANDING is enabled or terrain is enabled then this parameter sets height AGL. Otherwise this parameter sets height above home.  Set zero to ignore minimum height requirement to activate weathervaning

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 50

---

## Q_WVANE_SPD_MAX
**Display Name:** Weathervaning max ground speedÂ

**Description:**
Below this ground speed weathervaning is permitted. Set to 0 to ignore this condition when checking if vehicle should weathervane.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0 to 50

---

## Q_WVANE_VELZ_MAX
**Display Name:** Weathervaning max vertical speedÂ

**Description:**
The maximum climb or descent speed that the vehicle will still attempt to weathervane. Set to 0 to ignore this condition to get the aircraft to weathervane at any climb/descent rate.  This is particularly useful for aircraft with low disc loading that struggle with yaw control in decent.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0 to 5

---

## Q_WVANE_TAKEOFF
**Display Name:** Takeoff overrideÂ

**Description:**
Override the weather vaning behaviour when in takeoffs

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1No override0Disabled1Nose into wind2Nose or tail into wind3Side into wind4tail into wind:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** No override
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Nose into wind
- **2:** Nose or tail into wind
- **3:** Side into wind
- **4:** tail into wind

---

## Q_WVANE_LAND
**Display Name:** Landing overrideÂ

**Description:**
Override the weather vaning behaviour when in landing

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1No override0Disabled1Nose into wind2Nose or tail into wind3Side into wind4tail into wind:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** No override
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Nose into wind
- **2:** Nose or tail into wind
- **3:** Side into wind
- **4:** tail into wind

---

## Q_WVANE_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Weathervaning optionsÂ

**Description:**
Options impacting weathervaning behaviour

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Use pitch when nose or tail-in for faster weathervaning:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Use pitch when nose or tail-in for faster weathervaning

---

## RALLY_TOTAL
**Display Name:** Rally TotalÂ

**Description:**
Number of rally points currently loaded


---

## RALLY_LIMIT_KM
**Display Name:** Rally LimitÂ

**Description:**
Maximum distance to rally point. If the closest rally point is more than this number of kilometers from the current position and the home location is closer than any of the rally points from the current position then do RTL to home rather than to the closest rally point. This prevents a leftover rally point from a different airfield being used accidentally. If this is set to 0 then the closest rally point is always used.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.1:** kilometers

---

## RALLY_INCL_HOME
**Display Name:** Rally Include HomeÂ

**Description:**
Controls if Home is included as a Rally point (i.e. as a safe landing place) for RTL

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0DoNotIncludeHome1IncludeHome:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** DoNotIncludeHome
- **1:** IncludeHome

---

## RC_OVERRIDE_TIME
**Display Name:** RC override timeoutÂ

**Description:**
Timeout after which RC overrides will no longer be used, and RC input will resume, 0 will disable RC overrides, -1 will never timeout, and continue using overrides until they are disabled

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.0 to 120.0:** seconds

---

## RC_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** RC optionsÂ

**Description:**
RC input options

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Ignore RC Receiver1Ignore MAVLink Overrides2Ignore Receiver Failsafe bit but allow other RC failsafes if setup3FPort Pad4Log RC input bytes5Arming check throttle for 0 input6Skip the arming check for neutral Roll/Pitch/Yaw sticks7Allow Switch reverse8Use passthrough for CRSF telemetry9Suppress CRSF mode/rate message for ELRS systems10Enable multiple receiver support11Use Link Quality for RSSI with CRSF12Annotate CRSF flight mode with * on disarm13Use 420kbaud for ELRS protocol:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Ignore RC Receiver
- **1:** Ignore MAVLink Overrides
- **2:** Ignore Receiver Failsafe bit but allow other RC failsafes if setup
- **3:** FPort Pad
- **4:** Log RC input bytes
- **5:** Arming check throttle for 0 input
- **6:** Skip the arming check for neutral Roll/Pitch/Yaw sticks
- **7:** Allow Switch reverse
- **8:** Use passthrough for CRSF telemetry
- **9:** Suppress CRSF mode/rate message for ELRS systems
- **10:** Enable multiple receiver support
- **11:** Use Link Quality for RSSI with CRSF
- **12:** Annotate CRSF flight mode with * on disarm
- **13:** Use 420kbaud for ELRS protocol

---

## RC_PROTOCOLS
**Display Name:** RC protocols enabledÂ

**Description:**
Bitmask of enabled RC protocols. Allows narrowing the protocol detection to only specific types of RC receivers which can avoid issues with incorrect detection. Set to 1 to enable all protocols.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0All1PPM2IBUS3SBUS4SBUS_NI5DSM6SUMD7SRXL8SRXL29CRSF10ST2411FPORT12FPORT213FastSBUS14DroneCAN15Ghost16MAVRadio:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** All
- **1:** PPM
- **2:** IBUS
- **3:** SBUS
- **4:** SBUS_NI
- **5:** DSM
- **6:** SUMD
- **7:** SRXL
- **8:** SRXL2
- **9:** CRSF
- **10:** ST24
- **11:** FPORT
- **12:** FPORT2
- **13:** FastSBUS
- **14:** DroneCAN
- **15:** Ghost
- **16:** MAVRadio

---

## RC_FS_TIMEOUT
**Display Name:** RC Failsafe timeoutÂ

**Description:**
RC failsafe will trigger this many seconds after loss of RC

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.5 to 10.0:** seconds

---

## RC10_MIN
**Display Name:** RC min PWMÂ

**Description:**
RC minimum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 800 to 2200

---

## RC10_TRIM
**Display Name:** RC trim PWMÂ

**Description:**
RC trim (neutral) PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 800 to 2200

---

## RC10_MAX
**Display Name:** RC max PWMÂ

**Description:**
RC maximum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 800 to 2200

---

## RC10_REVERSED
**Display Name:** RC reversedÂ

**Description:**
Reverse channel input. Set to 0 for normal operation. Set to 1 to reverse this input channel.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Normal1Reversed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Normal
- **1:** Reversed

---

## RC10_DZ
**Display Name:** RC dead-zoneÂ

**Description:**
PWM dead zone in microseconds around trim or bottom

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 200:** PWM in microseconds

---

Function assigned to this RC channel

- **ValueMeaning0Do Nothing4RTL9Camera Trigger11Fence Enable16AUTO Mode22Parachute Release24Auto Mission Reset27Retract Mount128Relay1 On/Off29Landing Gear30Lost Plane Sound31Motor Emergency Stop34Relay2 On/Off35Relay3 On/Off36Relay4 On/Off38ADSB Avoidance Enable41ArmDisarm (4.1 and lower)43InvertedFlight Enable46RC Override Enable51MANUAL Mode52ACRO Mode55GUIDED Mode56LOITER Mode58Clear Waypoints62Compass Learn64Reverse Throttle65GPS Disable66Relay5 On/Off67Relay6 On/Off72CIRCLE Mode77TAKEOFF Mode78RunCam Control79RunCam OSD Control81Disarm82QAssist 3pos84AirMode85Generator86Non Auto Terrain Follow Disable87Crow Select88Soaring Enable89Landing Flare90EKF Source Set91Airspeed Ratio Calibration92FBWA Mode94VTX Power95FBWA taildragger takeoff mode96Trigger re-reading of mode switch98TRAINING Mode100KillIMU1101KillIMU2102Camera Mode Toggle105GPS Disable Yaw106Disable Airspeed Use107Enable FW Autotune108QRTL Mode110KillIMU3112SwitchExternalAHRS113Retract Mount2150CRUISE Mode153ArmDisarm (4.2 and higher)154ArmDisarm with Quadplane AirMode (4.2 and higher)155Set roll pitch and yaw trim to current servo and RC157Force FS Action to FBWA158Optflow Calibration160Weathervane Enable162FFT Tune163Mount Lock164Pause Stream Logging165Arm/Emergency Motor Stop166Camera Record Video167Camera Zoom168Camera Manual Focus169Camera Auto Focus170QSTABILIZE Mode171Calibrate Compasses172Battery MPPT Enable173Plane AUTO Mode Landing Abort174Camera Image Tracking175Camera Lens176Quadplane Fwd Throttle Override enable177Mount LRF enable179ICEngine start / stop180Test autotuned gains after tune is complete181QuickTune184System ID Chirp (Quadplane only)208Flap209VTOL Forward Throttle210Airbrakes212Mount1 Roll213Mount1 Pitch214Mount1 Yaw215Mount2 Roll216Mount2 Pitch217Mount2 Yaw300Scripting1301Scripting2302Scripting3303Scripting4304Scripting5305Scripting6306Scripting7307Scripting8:** Value

## RC11_MIN
**Display Name:** RC min PWMÂ

## RCMAP_ROLL
**Display Name:** Roll channelÂ

**Description:**
Roll channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Roll is normally on channel 1, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 16

---

## RCMAP_PITCH
**Display Name:** Pitch channelÂ

**Description:**
Pitch channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Pitch is normally on channel 2, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 16

---

## RCMAP_THROTTLE
**Display Name:** Throttle channelÂ

**Description:**
Throttle channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Throttle is normally on channel 3, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter. Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 16

---

## RCMAP_YAW
**Display Name:** Yaw channelÂ

**Description:**
Yaw channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Yaw (also known as rudder) is normally on channel 4, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 1 to 16

---

## RELAY10_FUNCTION
**Display Name:** Relay functionÂ

**Description:**
The function the relay channel is mapped to.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Relay2Ignition3Parachute4Camera9ICE Starter:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Relay
- **2:** Ignition
- **3:** Parachute
- **4:** Camera
- **9:** ICE Starter

---

## RELAY10_PIN
**Display Name:** Relay pinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin number for relay control. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled49BB Blue GP0 pin 450AUXOUT151AUXOUT252AUXOUT353AUXOUT454AUXOUT555AUXOUT657BB Blue GP0 pin 3113BB Blue GP0 pin 6116BB Blue GP0 pin 562BBBMini Pin P8.13101MainOut1102MainOut2103MainOut3104MainOut4105MainOut5106MainOut6107MainOut7108MainOut81000DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 01001DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 11002DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 21003DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 31004DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 41005DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 51006DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 61007DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 71008DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 81009DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 91010DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 101011DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 111012DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 121013DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 131014DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 141015DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 15:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **49:** BB Blue GP0 pin 4
- **50:** AUXOUT1
- **51:** AUXOUT2
- **52:** AUXOUT3
- **53:** AUXOUT4
- **54:** AUXOUT5
- **55:** AUXOUT6
- **57:** BB Blue GP0 pin 3
- **113:** BB Blue GP0 pin 6
- **116:** BB Blue GP0 pin 5
- **62:** BBBMini Pin P8.13
- **101:** MainOut1
- **102:** MainOut2
- **103:** MainOut3
- **104:** MainOut4
- **105:** MainOut5
- **106:** MainOut6
- **107:** MainOut7
- **108:** MainOut8
- **1000:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 0
- **1001:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 1
- **1002:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 2
- **1003:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 3
- **1004:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 4
- **1005:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 5
- **1006:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 6
- **1007:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 7
- **1008:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 8
- **1009:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 9
- **1010:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 10
- **1011:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 11
- **1012:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 12
- **1013:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 13
- **1014:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 14
- **1015:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 15

---

## RELAY10_DEFAULT
**Display Name:** Relay default stateÂ

**Description:**
Should the relay default to on or off, this only applies to RELAYx_FUNC "Relay" (1). All other uses will pick the appropriate default output state from within the controlling function's parameters. Note that if INVERTED is set then the default is inverted.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Off1On2NoChange:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Off
- **1:** On
- **2:** NoChange

---

## RELAY10_INVERTED
**Display Name:** Relay invert output signalÂ

**Description:**
Should the relay output signal be inverted. If enabled, relay on would be pin low and relay off would be pin high. NOTE: this impact's DEFAULT.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Normal1Inverted:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Normal
- **1:** Inverted

---

## RELAY11_DEFAULT
**Display Name:** Relay default stateÂ

**Description:**
Should the relay default to on or off, this only applies to RELAYx_FUNC "Relay" (1). All other uses will pick the appropriate default output state from within the controlling function's parameters. Note that if INVERTED is set then the default is inverted.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Off1On2NoChange:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Off
- **1:** On
- **2:** NoChange

---

- **ValueMeaning0Normal1Inverted:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Normal
- **1:** Inverted

## RELAY2_DEFAULT
**Display Name:** Relay default stateÂ

**Description:**
Should the relay default to on or off, this only applies to RELAYx_FUNC "Relay" (1). All other uses will pick the appropriate default output state from within the controlling function's parameters. Note that if INVERTED is set then the default is inverted.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Off1On2NoChange:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Off
- **1:** On
- **2:** NoChange

---

## RELAY2_INVERTED
**Display Name:** Relay invert output signalÂ

**Description:**
Should the relay output signal be inverted. If enabled, relay on would be pin low and relay off would be pin high. NOTE: this impact's DEFAULT.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Normal1Inverted:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Normal
- **1:** Inverted

---

- **1002:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 2
- **1003:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 3
- **1004:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 4
- **1005:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 5
- **1006:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 6
- **1007:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 7
- **1008:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 8
- **1009:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 9
- **1010:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 10
- **1011:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 11
- **1012:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 12
- **1013:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 13
- **1014:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 14
- **1015:** DroneCAN Hardpoint ID 15

---

## RELAY9_DEFAULT
**Display Name:** Relay default stateÂ

**Description:**
Should the relay default to on or off, this only applies to RELAYx_FUNC "Relay" (1). All other uses will pick the appropriate default output state from within the controlling function's parameters. Note that if INVERTED is set then the default is inverted.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Off1On2NoChange:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Off
- **1:** On
- **2:** NoChange

---

## RELAY9_INVERTED
**Display Name:** Relay invert output signalÂ

**Description:**
Should the relay output signal be inverted. If enabled, relay on would be pin low and relay off would be pin high. NOTE: this impact's DEFAULT.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Normal1Inverted:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Normal
- **1:** Inverted

---

## RLL2SRV_TCONST
**Display Name:** Roll Time ConstantÂ

**Description:**
Time constant in seconds from demanded to achieved roll angle. Most models respond well to 0.5. May be reduced for faster responses, but setting lower than a model can achieve will not help.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.1:** 0.4 to 1.0

---

## RLL2SRV_RMAX
**Display Name:** Maximum Roll RateÂ

**Description:**
This sets the maximum roll rate that the attitude controller will demand (degrees/sec) in angle stabilized modes. Setting it to zero disables this limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 180

---

## RLL_RATE_P
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller P gainÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller P gain. Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.005:** 0.08 to 0.35

---

## RLL_RATE_I
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller I gainÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0.01 to 0.6

---

## RLL_RATE_IMAX
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller I gain maximumÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## RLL_RATE_D
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller D gainÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0.001 to 0.03

---

## RLL_RATE_FF
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller feed forwardÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller feed forward

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 3.0

---

## RLL_RATE_FLTT
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller target frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 2 to 50

---

## RLL_RATE_FLTE
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller error frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 2 to 50

---

## RLL_RATE_FLTD
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in HzÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 50

---

## RLL_RATE_SMAX
**Display Name:** Roll slew rate limitÂ

**Description:**
Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.5:** 0 to 200

---

## RLL_RATE_PDMX
**Display Name:** Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximumÂ

**Description:**
Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 1

---

## RLL_RATE_D_FF
**Display Name:** Roll Derivative FeedForward GainÂ

**Description:**
FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.001:** 0 to 0.03

---

## RLL_RATE_NTF
**Display Name:** Roll Target notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Roll Target notch filter index


---

## RLL_RATE_NEF
**Display Name:** Roll Error notch filter indexÂ

**Description:**
Roll Error notch filter index


---

## RNGFND1_TYPE
**Display Name:** Rangefinder typeÂ

**Description:**
Type of connected rangefinder

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Analog2MaxbotixI2C3LidarLite-I2C5PWM6BBB-PRU7LightWareI2C8LightWareSerial9Bebop10MAVLink11USD1_Serial12LeddarOne13MaxbotixSerial14TeraRangerI2C15LidarLiteV3-I2C16VL53L0X or VL53L1X17NMEA18WASP-LRF19BenewakeTF0220Benewake-Serial21LidarLightV3HP22PWM23BlueRoboticsPing24DroneCAN25BenewakeTFminiPlus-I2C26LanbaoPSK-CM8JL65-CC527BenewakeTF0328VL53L1X-ShortRange29LeddarVu8-Serial30HC-SR0431GYUS42v232MSP33USD1_CAN34Benewake_CAN35TeraRangerSerial36Lua_Scripting37NoopLoop_TOFSense38NoopLoop_TOFSense_CAN39NRA24_CAN40NoopLoop_TOFSenseF_I2C41JRE_Serial42Ainstein_LR_D143RDS02UF44HexsoonRadar100SITL:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Analog
- **2:** MaxbotixI2C
- **3:** LidarLite-I2C
- **5:** PWM
- **6:** BBB-PRU
- **7:** LightWareI2C
- **8:** LightWareSerial
- **9:** Bebop
- **10:** MAVLink

- **12:** LeddarOne
- **13:** MaxbotixSerial
- **14:** TeraRangerI2C
- **15:** LidarLiteV3-I2C

- **17:** NMEA
- **18:** WASP-LRF
- **19:** BenewakeTF02
- **20:** Benewake-Serial
- **21:** LidarLightV3HP
- **22:** PWM
- **23:** BlueRoboticsPing
- **24:** DroneCAN
- **25:** BenewakeTFminiPlus-I2C
- **26:** LanbaoPSK-CM8JL65-CC5
- **27:** BenewakeTF03
- **28:** VL53L1X-ShortRange
- **29:** LeddarVu8-Serial
- **30:** HC-SR04
- **31:** GYUS42v2
- **32:** MSP
- **33:** USD1_CAN
- **34:** Benewake_CAN
- **35:** TeraRangerSerial
- **36:** Lua_Scripting
- **37:** NoopLoop_TOFSense
- **38:** NoopLoop_TOFSense_CAN
- **39:** NRA24_CAN
- **40:** NoopLoop_TOFSenseF_I2C
- **41:** JRE_Serial
- **42:** Ainstein_LR_D1
- **43:** RDS02UF
- **44:** HexsoonRadar
- **100:** SITL

---

## RNGFND1_PIN
**Display Name:** Rangefinder pinÂ

**Description:**
Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used11Pixracer13Pixhawk ADC414Pixhawk ADC315Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6103Pixhawk SBUS:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **11:** Pixracer
- **13:** Pixhawk ADC4
- **14:** Pixhawk ADC3
- **15:** Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **103:** Pixhawk SBUS

---

## RNGFND1_SCALING
**Display Name:** Rangefinder scalingÂ

**Description:**
Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** meters per volt

---

## RNGFND1_OFFSET
**Display Name:** rangefinder offsetÂ

**Description:**
Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** volt

---

## RNGFND1_FUNCTION
**Display Name:** Rangefinder functionÂ

**Description:**
Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Linear1Inverted2Hyperbolic:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Linear
- **1:** Inverted
- **2:** Hyperbolic

---

## RNGFND1_MIN_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder minimum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Minimum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFND1_MAX_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder maximum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Maximum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFND1_STOP_PIN
**Display Name:** Rangefinder stop pinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6111PX4 FMU Relay1112PX4 FMU Relay2113PX4IO Relay1114PX4IO Relay2115PX4IO ACC1116PX4IO ACC2:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **111:** PX4 FMU Relay1
- **112:** PX4 FMU Relay2
- **113:** PX4IO Relay1
- **114:** PX4IO Relay2
- **115:** PX4IO ACC1
- **116:** PX4IO ACC2

---

## RNGFND1_RMETRIC
**Display Name:** RatiometricÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0No1Yes:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** No
- **1:** Yes

---

## RNGFND1_PWRRNG
**Display Name:** Powersave rangeÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 32767:** meters

---

## RNGFND1_GNDCLEAR
**Display Name:** Distance (in cm) from the range finder to the groundÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the expected range measurement(in cm) that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 127

---

## RNGFND1_ADDR
**Display Name:** Bus address of sensorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 127

---

## RNGFND1_POS_X
**Display Name:** X position offsetÂ

**Description:**
X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND1_POS_Y
**Display Name:** Y position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND1_POS_Z
**Display Name:** Z position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND1_ORIENT
**Display Name:** Rangefinder orientationÂ

**Description:**
Orientation of rangefinder

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Forward1Forward-Right2Right3Back-Right4Back5Back-Left6Left7Forward-Left24Up25Down:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Forward
- **1:** Forward-Right
- **2:** Right
- **3:** Back-Right
- **4:** Back
- **5:** Back-Left
- **6:** Left
- **7:** Forward-Left
- **24:** Up
- **25:** Down

---

## RNGFND1_WSP_MAVG
**Display Name:** Moving Average RangeÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results


---

## RNGFND1_WSP_MEDF
**Display Name:** Moving Median FilterÂ

**Description:**
Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active


---

## RNGFND1_WSP_FRQ
**Display Name:** FrequencyÂ

**Description:**
Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.


---

## RNGFND1_WSP_AVG
**Display Name:** Multi-pulse averagesÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement


---

## RNGFND1_WSP_THR
**Display Name:** Sensitivity thresholdÂ

**Description:**
Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments


---

## RNGFND1_WSP_BAUD
**Display Name:** Baud rateÂ

**Description:**
Desired baud rate

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Low Speed1High Speed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Low Speed
- **1:** High Speed

---

## RNGFND1_RECV_ID
**Display Name:** RangeFinder CAN receive IDÂ

**Description:**
The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.


---

## RNGFND1_SNR_MIN
**Display Name:** RangeFinder Minimum signal strengthÂ

**Description:**
RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance


---

**Display Name:** Rangefinder typeÂ

Type of connected rangefinder
