## RNGFND2_WSP_BAUD
**Display Name:** Baud rateÂ

**Description:**
Desired baud rate

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Low Speed1High Speed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Low Speed
- **1:** High Speed

---

## RNGFND2_RECV_ID
**Display Name:** RangeFinder CAN receive IDÂ

**Description:**
The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.


---

## RNGFND2_SNR_MIN
**Display Name:** RangeFinder Minimum signal strengthÂ

**Description:**
RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance


---

## RNGFND3_TYPE
**Display Name:** Rangefinder typeÂ

**Description:**
Type of connected rangefinder

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Analog2MaxbotixI2C3LidarLite-I2C5PWM6BBB-PRU7LightWareI2C8LightWareSerial9Bebop10MAVLink11USD1_Serial12LeddarOne13MaxbotixSerial14TeraRangerI2C15LidarLiteV3-I2C16VL53L0X or VL53L1X17NMEA18WASP-LRF19BenewakeTF0220Benewake-Serial21LidarLightV3HP22PWM23BlueRoboticsPing24DroneCAN25BenewakeTFminiPlus-I2C26LanbaoPSK-CM8JL65-CC527BenewakeTF0328VL53L1X-ShortRange29LeddarVu8-Serial30HC-SR0431GYUS42v232MSP33USD1_CAN34Benewake_CAN35TeraRangerSerial36Lua_Scripting37NoopLoop_TOFSense38NoopLoop_TOFSense_CAN39NRA24_CAN40NoopLoop_TOFSenseF_I2C41JRE_Serial42Ainstein_LR_D143RDS02UF44HexsoonRadar100SITL:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Analog
- **2:** MaxbotixI2C
- **3:** LidarLite-I2C
- **5:** PWM
- **6:** BBB-PRU
- **7:** LightWareI2C
- **8:** LightWareSerial
- **9:** Bebop
- **10:** MAVLink
- **11:** USD1_Serial
- **12:** LeddarOne
- **13:** MaxbotixSerial
- **14:** TeraRangerI2C
- **15:** LidarLiteV3-I2C
- **16:** VL53L0X or VL53L1X
- **17:** NMEA
- **18:** WASP-LRF
- **19:** BenewakeTF02
- **20:** Benewake-Serial
- **21:** LidarLightV3HP
- **22:** PWM
- **23:** BlueRoboticsPing
- **24:** DroneCAN
- **25:** BenewakeTFminiPlus-I2C
- **26:** LanbaoPSK-CM8JL65-CC5
- **27:** BenewakeTF03
- **28:** VL53L1X-ShortRange
- **29:** LeddarVu8-Serial
- **30:** HC-SR04
- **31:** GYUS42v2
- **32:** MSP
- **33:** USD1_CAN
- **34:** Benewake_CAN
- **35:** TeraRangerSerial
- **36:** Lua_Scripting
- **37:** NoopLoop_TOFSense
- **38:** NoopLoop_TOFSense_CAN
- **39:** NRA24_CAN
- **40:** NoopLoop_TOFSenseF_I2C
- **41:** JRE_Serial
- **42:** Ainstein_LR_D1
- **43:** RDS02UF
- **44:** HexsoonRadar
- **100:** SITL

---

## RNGFND3_PIN
**Display Name:** Rangefinder pinÂ

**Description:**
Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used11Pixracer13Pixhawk ADC414Pixhawk ADC315Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6103Pixhawk SBUS:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **11:** Pixracer
- **13:** Pixhawk ADC4
- **14:** Pixhawk ADC3
- **15:** Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **103:** Pixhawk SBUS

---

## RNGFND3_SCALING
**Display Name:** Rangefinder scalingÂ

**Description:**
Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** meters per volt

---

## RNGFND3_OFFSET
**Display Name:** rangefinder offsetÂ

**Description:**
Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** volt

---

## RNGFND3_FUNCTION
**Display Name:** Rangefinder functionÂ

**Description:**
Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Linear1Inverted2Hyperbolic:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Linear
- **1:** Inverted
- **2:** Hyperbolic

---

## RNGFND3_MIN_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder minimum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Minimum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFND3_MAX_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder maximum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Maximum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFND3_STOP_PIN
**Display Name:** Rangefinder stop pinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6111PX4 FMU Relay1112PX4 FMU Relay2113PX4IO Relay1114PX4IO Relay2115PX4IO ACC1116PX4IO ACC2:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **111:** PX4 FMU Relay1
- **112:** PX4 FMU Relay2
- **113:** PX4IO Relay1
- **114:** PX4IO Relay2
- **115:** PX4IO ACC1
- **116:** PX4IO ACC2

---

## RNGFND3_RMETRIC
**Display Name:** RatiometricÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0No1Yes:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** No
- **1:** Yes

---

## RNGFND3_PWRRNG
**Display Name:** Powersave rangeÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 32767:** meters

---

## RNGFND3_GNDCLEAR
**Display Name:** Distance (in cm) from the range finder to the groundÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the expected range measurement(in cm) that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 127

---

## RNGFND3_ADDR
**Display Name:** Bus address of sensorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 127

---

## RNGFND3_POS_X
**Display Name:** X position offsetÂ

**Description:**
X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND3_POS_Y
**Display Name:** Y position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND3_POS_Z
**Display Name:** Z position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND3_ORIENT
**Display Name:** Rangefinder orientationÂ

**Description:**
Orientation of rangefinder

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Forward1Forward-Right2Right3Back-Right4Back5Back-Left6Left7Forward-Left24Up25Down:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Forward
- **1:** Forward-Right
- **2:** Right
- **3:** Back-Right
- **4:** Back
- **5:** Back-Left
- **6:** Left
- **7:** Forward-Left
- **24:** Up
- **25:** Down

---

## RNGFND3_WSP_MAVG
**Display Name:** Moving Average RangeÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results


---

## RNGFND3_WSP_MEDF
**Display Name:** Moving Median FilterÂ

**Description:**
Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active


---

## RNGFND3_WSP_FRQ
**Display Name:** FrequencyÂ

**Description:**
Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.


---

## RNGFND3_WSP_AVG
**Display Name:** Multi-pulse averagesÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement


---

## RNGFND3_WSP_THR
**Display Name:** Sensitivity thresholdÂ

**Description:**
Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments


---

## RNGFND3_WSP_BAUD
**Display Name:** Baud rateÂ

**Description:**
Desired baud rate

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Low Speed1High Speed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Low Speed
- **1:** High Speed

---

## RNGFND3_RECV_ID
**Display Name:** RangeFinder CAN receive IDÂ

**Description:**
The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.


---

## RNGFND3_SNR_MIN
**Display Name:** RangeFinder Minimum signal strengthÂ

**Description:**
RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance


---

- **0.001:** meters per volt

---

## RNGFND5_OFFSET
**Display Name:** rangefinder offsetÂ

**Description:**
Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** volt

---

## RNGFND5_FUNCTION
**Display Name:** Rangefinder functionÂ

**Description:**
Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Linear1Inverted2Hyperbolic:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Linear
- **1:** Inverted
- **2:** Hyperbolic

---

## RNGFND5_MIN_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder minimum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Minimum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFND5_MAX_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder maximum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Maximum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFND5_STOP_PIN
**Display Name:** Rangefinder stop pinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6111PX4 FMU Relay1112PX4 FMU Relay2113PX4IO Relay1114PX4IO Relay2115PX4IO ACC1116PX4IO ACC2:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **111:** PX4 FMU Relay1
- **112:** PX4 FMU Relay2
- **113:** PX4IO Relay1
- **114:** PX4IO Relay2
- **115:** PX4IO ACC1
- **116:** PX4IO ACC2

---

## RNGFND5_RMETRIC
**Display Name:** RatiometricÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0No1Yes:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** No
- **1:** Yes

---

## RNGFND5_PWRRNG
**Display Name:** Powersave rangeÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 32767:** meters

---

## RNGFND5_GNDCLEAR
**Display Name:** Distance (in cm) from the range finder to the groundÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the expected range measurement(in cm) that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 127

---

## RNGFND5_ADDR
**Display Name:** Bus address of sensorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 127

---

## RNGFND5_POS_X
**Display Name:** X position offsetÂ

**Description:**
X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND5_POS_Y
**Display Name:** Y position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND5_POS_Z
**Display Name:** Z position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND5_ORIENT
**Display Name:** Rangefinder orientationÂ

**Description:**
Orientation of rangefinder

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Forward1Forward-Right2Right3Back-Right4Back5Back-Left6Left7Forward-Left24Up25Down:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Forward
- **1:** Forward-Right
- **2:** Right
- **3:** Back-Right
- **4:** Back
- **5:** Back-Left
- **6:** Left
- **7:** Forward-Left
- **24:** Up
- **25:** Down

---

## RNGFND5_WSP_MAVG
**Display Name:** Moving Average RangeÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results


---

## RNGFND5_WSP_MEDF
**Display Name:** Moving Median FilterÂ

**Description:**
Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active


---

## RNGFND5_WSP_FRQ
**Display Name:** FrequencyÂ

**Description:**
Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.


---

## RNGFND5_WSP_AVG
**Display Name:** Multi-pulse averagesÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement


---

## RNGFND5_WSP_THR
**Display Name:** Sensitivity thresholdÂ

**Description:**
Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments


---

## RNGFND5_WSP_BAUD
**Display Name:** Baud rateÂ

**Description:**
Desired baud rate

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Low Speed1High Speed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Low Speed
- **1:** High Speed

---

## RNGFND5_RECV_ID
**Display Name:** RangeFinder CAN receive IDÂ

**Description:**
The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.


---

## RNGFND5_SNR_MIN
**Display Name:** RangeFinder Minimum signal strengthÂ

**Description:**
RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance


---

## RNGFND6_TYPE
**Display Name:** Rangefinder typeÂ

**Description:**
Type of connected rangefinder

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Analog2MaxbotixI2C3LidarLite-I2C5PWM6BBB-PRU7LightWareI2C8LightWareSerial9Bebop10MAVLink11USD1_Serial12LeddarOne13MaxbotixSerial14TeraRangerI2C15LidarLiteV3-I2C16VL53L0X or VL53L1X17NMEA18WASP-LRF19BenewakeTF0220Benewake-Serial21LidarLightV3HP22PWM23BlueRoboticsPing24DroneCAN25BenewakeTFminiPlus-I2C26LanbaoPSK-CM8JL65-CC527BenewakeTF0328VL53L1X-ShortRange29LeddarVu8-Serial30HC-SR0431GYUS42v232MSP33USD1_CAN34Benewake_CAN35TeraRangerSerial36Lua_Scripting37NoopLoop_TOFSense38NoopLoop_TOFSense_CAN39NRA24_CAN40NoopLoop_TOFSenseF_I2C41JRE_Serial42Ainstein_LR_D143RDS02UF44HexsoonRadar100SITL:** Value

- **20:** Benewake-Serial

- **22:** PWM

- **37:** NoopLoop_TOFSense
- **38:** NoopLoop_TOFSense_CAN

- **43:** RDS02UF
- **44:** HexsoonRadar

---

## RNGFND6_PIN
**Display Name:** Rangefinder pinÂ

**Description:**
Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used11Pixracer13Pixhawk ADC414Pixhawk ADC315Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6103Pixhawk SBUS:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **11:** Pixracer
- **13:** Pixhawk ADC4
- **14:** Pixhawk ADC3
- **15:** Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **103:** Pixhawk SBUS

---

**Display Name:** Rangefinder scalingÂ

**Description:**
Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** meters per volt

Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

- **2:** Right
- **3:** Back-Right
- **4:** Back
- **5:** Back-Left
- **6:** Left
- **7:** Forward-Left
- **24:** Up
- **25:** Down

---

## RNGFND7_WSP_MAVG
**Display Name:** Moving Average RangeÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results


---

## RNGFND7_WSP_MEDF
**Display Name:** Moving Median FilterÂ

**Description:**
Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active


---

## RNGFND7_WSP_FRQ
**Display Name:** FrequencyÂ

**Description:**
Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.


---

## RNGFND7_WSP_AVG
**Display Name:** Multi-pulse averagesÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement


---

## RNGFND7_WSP_THR
**Display Name:** Sensitivity thresholdÂ

**Description:**
Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments


---

## RNGFND7_WSP_BAUD
**Display Name:** Baud rateÂ

**Description:**
Desired baud rate

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Low Speed1High Speed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Low Speed
- **1:** High Speed

---

## RNGFND7_RECV_ID
**Display Name:** RangeFinder CAN receive IDÂ

**Description:**
The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.


---

## RNGFND7_SNR_MIN
**Display Name:** RangeFinder Minimum signal strengthÂ

**Description:**
RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance


---

## RNGFND8_TYPE
**Display Name:** Rangefinder typeÂ

**Description:**
Type of connected rangefinder

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Analog2MaxbotixI2C3LidarLite-I2C5PWM6BBB-PRU7LightWareI2C8LightWareSerial9Bebop10MAVLink11USD1_Serial12LeddarOne13MaxbotixSerial14TeraRangerI2C15LidarLiteV3-I2C16VL53L0X or VL53L1X17NMEA18WASP-LRF19BenewakeTF0220Benewake-Serial21LidarLightV3HP22PWM23BlueRoboticsPing24DroneCAN25BenewakeTFminiPlus-I2C26LanbaoPSK-CM8JL65-CC527BenewakeTF0328VL53L1X-ShortRange29LeddarVu8-Serial30HC-SR0431GYUS42v232MSP33USD1_CAN34Benewake_CAN35TeraRangerSerial36Lua_Scripting37NoopLoop_TOFSense38NoopLoop_TOFSense_CAN39NRA24_CAN40NoopLoop_TOFSenseF_I2C41JRE_Serial42Ainstein_LR_D143RDS02UF44HexsoonRadar100SITL:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Analog
- **2:** MaxbotixI2C
- **3:** LidarLite-I2C
- **5:** PWM
- **6:** BBB-PRU
- **7:** LightWareI2C
- **8:** LightWareSerial
- **9:** Bebop
- **10:** MAVLink
- **11:** USD1_Serial
- **12:** LeddarOne
- **13:** MaxbotixSerial
- **14:** TeraRangerI2C
- **15:** LidarLiteV3-I2C
- **16:** VL53L0X or VL53L1X
- **17:** NMEA
- **18:** WASP-LRF
- **19:** BenewakeTF02
- **20:** Benewake-Serial
- **21:** LidarLightV3HP
- **22:** PWM
- **23:** BlueRoboticsPing
- **24:** DroneCAN
- **25:** BenewakeTFminiPlus-I2C
- **26:** LanbaoPSK-CM8JL65-CC5
- **27:** BenewakeTF03
- **28:** VL53L1X-ShortRange
- **29:** LeddarVu8-Serial
- **30:** HC-SR04
- **31:** GYUS42v2
- **32:** MSP
- **33:** USD1_CAN
- **34:** Benewake_CAN
- **35:** TeraRangerSerial
- **36:** Lua_Scripting
- **37:** NoopLoop_TOFSense
- **38:** NoopLoop_TOFSense_CAN
- **39:** NRA24_CAN
- **40:** NoopLoop_TOFSenseF_I2C
- **41:** JRE_Serial
- **42:** Ainstein_LR_D1
- **43:** RDS02UF
- **44:** HexsoonRadar
- **100:** SITL

---

## RNGFND8_PIN
**Display Name:** Rangefinder pinÂ

**Description:**
Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used11Pixracer13Pixhawk ADC414Pixhawk ADC315Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6103Pixhawk SBUS:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **11:** Pixracer
- **13:** Pixhawk ADC4
- **14:** Pixhawk ADC3
- **15:** Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **103:** Pixhawk SBUS

---

## RNGFND8_SCALING
**Display Name:** Rangefinder scalingÂ

**Description:**
Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** meters per volt

---

## RNGFND8_OFFSET
**Display Name:** rangefinder offsetÂ

**Description:**
Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** volt

---

## RNGFND8_FUNCTION
**Display Name:** Rangefinder functionÂ

**Description:**
Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Linear1Inverted2Hyperbolic:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Linear
- **1:** Inverted
- **2:** Hyperbolic

---

## RNGFND8_MIN_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder minimum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Minimum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFND8_MAX_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder maximum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Maximum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFND8_STOP_PIN
**Display Name:** Rangefinder stop pinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6111PX4 FMU Relay1112PX4 FMU Relay2113PX4IO Relay1114PX4IO Relay2115PX4IO ACC1116PX4IO ACC2:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **111:** PX4 FMU Relay1
- **112:** PX4 FMU Relay2
- **113:** PX4IO Relay1
- **114:** PX4IO Relay2
- **115:** PX4IO ACC1
- **116:** PX4IO ACC2

---

## RNGFND8_RMETRIC
**Display Name:** RatiometricÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0No1Yes:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** No
- **1:** Yes

---

## RNGFND8_PWRRNG
**Display Name:** Powersave rangeÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 32767:** meters

---

## RNGFND8_GNDCLEAR
**Display Name:** Distance (in cm) from the range finder to the groundÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the expected range measurement(in cm) that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 127

---

## RNGFND8_ADDR
**Display Name:** Bus address of sensorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 127

---

## RNGFND8_POS_X
**Display Name:** X position offsetÂ

**Description:**
X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND8_POS_Y
**Display Name:** Y position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND8_POS_Z
**Display Name:** Z position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFND8_ORIENT
**Display Name:** Rangefinder orientationÂ

**Description:**
Orientation of rangefinder

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Forward1Forward-Right2Right3Back-Right4Back5Back-Left6Left7Forward-Left24Up25Down:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Forward
- **1:** Forward-Right
- **2:** Right
- **3:** Back-Right
- **4:** Back
- **5:** Back-Left
- **6:** Left
- **7:** Forward-Left
- **24:** Up
- **25:** Down

---

Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results

**Description:**
Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active

**Description:**
Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.


---

## RNGFND8_WSP_AVG
**Display Name:** Multi-pulse averagesÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement


---

## RNGFND8_WSP_THR
**Display Name:** Sensitivity thresholdÂ

**Description:**
Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments


---

**Display Name:** Baud rateÂ

**Description:**
Desired baud rate

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Low Speed1High Speed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Low Speed
- **1:** High Speed

---

## RNGFND8_RECV_ID
**Display Name:** RangeFinder CAN receive IDÂ

**Description:**
The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

**Display Name:** RangeFinder Minimum signal strengthÂ

**Description:**
RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance

**Display Name:** Rangefinder pinÂ

**Description:**
Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used11Pixracer13Pixhawk ADC414Pixhawk ADC315Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6103Pixhawk SBUS:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **11:** Pixracer
- **13:** Pixhawk ADC4
- **14:** Pixhawk ADC3
- **15:** Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **103:** Pixhawk SBUS

---

## RNGFNDA_SCALING
**Display Name:** Rangefinder scalingÂ

**Description:**
Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** meters per volt

---

## RNGFNDA_OFFSET
**Display Name:** rangefinder offsetÂ

**Description:**
Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **0.001:** volt

---

## RNGFNDA_FUNCTION
**Display Name:** Rangefinder functionÂ

**Description:**
Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Linear1Inverted2Hyperbolic:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Linear
- **1:** Inverted
- **2:** Hyperbolic

---

## RNGFNDA_MIN_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder minimum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Minimum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFNDA_MAX_CM
**Display Name:** Rangefinder maximum distanceÂ

**Description:**
Maximum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Units
- **1:** centimeters

---

## RNGFNDA_STOP_PIN
**Display Name:** Rangefinder stop pinÂ

**Description:**
Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Not Used50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6111PX4 FMU Relay1112PX4 FMU Relay2113PX4IO Relay1114PX4IO Relay2115PX4IO ACC1116PX4IO ACC2:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Not Used
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **111:** PX4 FMU Relay1
- **112:** PX4 FMU Relay2
- **113:** PX4IO Relay1
- **114:** PX4IO Relay2
- **115:** PX4IO ACC1
- **116:** PX4IO ACC2

---

## RNGFNDA_RMETRIC
**Display Name:** RatiometricÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0No1Yes:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** No
- **1:** Yes

---

## RNGFNDA_PWRRNG
**Display Name:** Powersave rangeÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 32767:** meters

---

## RNGFNDA_GNDCLEAR
**Display Name:** Distance (in cm) from the range finder to the groundÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the expected range measurement(in cm) that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 5 to 127

---

## RNGFNDA_ADDR
**Display Name:** Bus address of sensorÂ

**Description:**
This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 127

---

## RNGFNDA_POS_X
**Display Name:** X position offsetÂ

**Description:**
X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFNDA_POS_Y
**Display Name:** Y position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFNDA_POS_Z
**Display Name:** Z position offsetÂ

**Description:**
Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** -5 to 5

---

## RNGFNDA_ORIENT
**Display Name:** Rangefinder orientationÂ

**Description:**
Orientation of rangefinder

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Forward1Forward-Right2Right3Back-Right4Back5Back-Left6Left7Forward-Left24Up25Down:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Forward
- **1:** Forward-Right
- **2:** Right
- **3:** Back-Right
- **4:** Back
- **5:** Back-Left
- **6:** Left
- **7:** Forward-Left
- **24:** Up
- **25:** Down

---

## RNGFNDA_WSP_MAVG
**Display Name:** Moving Average RangeÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results


---

## RNGFNDA_WSP_MEDF
**Display Name:** Moving Median FilterÂ

**Description:**
Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active


---

## RNGFNDA_WSP_FRQ
**Display Name:** FrequencyÂ

**Description:**
Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.


---

## RNGFNDA_WSP_AVG
**Display Name:** Multi-pulse averagesÂ

**Description:**
Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement


---

## RNGFNDA_WSP_THR
**Display Name:** Sensitivity thresholdÂ

**Description:**
Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments


---

## RNGFNDA_WSP_BAUD
**Display Name:** Baud rateÂ

**Description:**
Desired baud rate

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Low Speed1High Speed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Low Speed
- **1:** High Speed

---

## RNGFNDA_RECV_ID
**Display Name:** RangeFinder CAN receive IDÂ

**Description:**
The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.


---

## RNGFNDA_SNR_MIN
**Display Name:** RangeFinder Minimum signal strengthÂ

**Description:**
RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance


---

## RPM1_TYPE
**Display Name:** RPM typeÂ

**Description:**
What type of RPM sensor is connected

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Not Used2GPIO3EFI4Harmonic Notch5ESC Telemetry Motors Bitmask6Generator7DroneCAN:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Not Used
- **2:** GPIO
- **3:** EFI
- **4:** Harmonic Notch
- **5:** ESC Telemetry Motors Bitmask
- **6:** Generator
- **7:** DroneCAN

---

**Display Name:** RPM scalingÂ

**Description:**
Scaling factor between sensor reading and RPM.


---

## RPM1_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum RPMÂ

**Description:**
Maximum RPM to report. Only used on type = GPIO.


---

## RPM1_MIN
**Display Name:** Minimum RPMÂ

**Description:**
Minimum RPM to report. Only used on type = GPIO.


---

## RPM1_MIN_QUAL
**Display Name:** Minimum QualityÂ

**Description:**
Minimum data quality to be used


---

## RPM1_PIN
**Display Name:** Input pin numberÂ

**Description:**
Which digital GPIO pin to use. Only used on type = GPIO. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6

---

**Display Name:** Bitmask of ESC telemetry channels to averageÂ

**Description:**
Mask of channels which support ESC rpm telemetry. RPM telemetry of the selected channels will be averaged

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Channel11Channel22Channel33Channel44Channel55Channel66Channel77Channel88Channel99Channel1010Channel1111Channel1212Channel1313Channel1414Channel1515Channel16:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Channel1
- **1:** Channel2
- **2:** Channel3
- **3:** Channel4
- **4:** Channel5
- **5:** Channel6
- **6:** Channel7
- **7:** Channel8
- **8:** Channel9
- **9:** Channel10
- **10:** Channel11
- **11:** Channel12
- **12:** Channel13
- **13:** Channel14
- **14:** Channel15
- **15:** Channel16

---

## RPM1_ESC_INDEX
**Display Name:** ESC Telemetry Index to write RPM toÂ

**Description:**
ESC Telemetry Index to write RPM to. Use 0 to disable.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 0 to 10

---

**Display Name:** DroneCAN Sensor IDÂ

**Description:**
DroneCAN sensor ID to assign to this backend

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** -1 to 10

---

## RSSI_TYPE
**Display Name:** RSSI TypeÂ

**Description:**
Radio Receiver RSSI type. If your radio receiver supports RSSI of some kind, set it here, then set its associated RSSI_XXXXX parameters, if any.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1AnalogPin2RCChannelPwmValue3ReceiverProtocol4PWMInputPin5TelemetryRadioRSSI:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** AnalogPin
- **2:** RCChannelPwmValue
- **3:** ReceiverProtocol
- **4:** PWMInputPin
- **5:** TelemetryRadioRSSI

---

## RSSI_ANA_PIN
**Display Name:** Receiver RSSI sensing pinÂ

**Description:**
Pin used to read the RSSI voltage or PWM value. Analog Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning8V5 Nano11Pixracer13Pixhawk ADC414Pixhawk ADC315Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC50AUX151AUX252AUX353AUX454AUX555AUX6103Pixhawk SBUS:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **8:** V5 Nano
- **11:** Pixracer
- **13:** Pixhawk ADC4
- **14:** Pixhawk ADC3
- **15:** Pixhawk ADC6/Pixhawk2 ADC
- **50:** AUX1
- **51:** AUX2
- **52:** AUX3
- **53:** AUX4
- **54:** AUX5
- **55:** AUX6
- **103:** Pixhawk SBUS

---

## RSSI_PIN_LOW
**Display Name:** RSSI pin's lowest voltageÂ

**Description:**
RSSI pin's voltage received on the RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the weakest. Some radio receivers put out inverted values so this value may be higher than RSSI_PIN_HIGH. When using pin 103, the maximum value of the parameter is 3.3V.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 5.0

---

## RSSI_PIN_HIGH
**Display Name:** RSSI pin's highest voltageÂ

**Description:**
RSSI pin's voltage received on the RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the strongest. Some radio receivers put out inverted values so this value may be lower than RSSI_PIN_LOW. When using pin 103, the maximum value of the parameter is 3.3V.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **0.01:** 0 to 5.0

---

## RSSI_CHANNEL
**Display Name:** Receiver RSSI channel numberÂ

**Description:**
The channel number where RSSI will be output by the radio receiver (5 and above).


---

## RSSI_CHAN_LOW
**Display Name:** RSSI PWM low valueÂ

**Description:**
PWM value that the radio receiver will put on the RSSI_CHANNEL or RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the weakest. Some radio receivers output inverted values so this value may be lower than RSSI_CHAN_HIGH

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 2000:** PWM in microseconds

---

## RSSI_CHAN_HIGH
**Display Name:** Receiver RSSI PWM high valueÂ

**Description:**
PWM value that the radio receiver will put on the RSSI_CHANNEL or RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the strongest. Some radio receivers output inverted values so this value may be higher than RSSI_CHAN_LOW

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 2000:** PWM in microseconds

---

## SCHED_DEBUG
**Display Name:** Scheduler debug levelÂ

**Description:**
Set to non-zero to enable scheduler debug messages. When set to show "Slips" the scheduler will display a message whenever a scheduled task is delayed due to too much CPU load. When set to ShowOverruns the scheduled will display a message whenever a task takes longer than the limit promised in the task table.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled2ShowSlips3ShowOverruns:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **2:** ShowSlips
- **3:** ShowOverruns

---

## SCHED_LOOP_RATE
**Display Name:** Scheduling main loop rateÂ

**Description:**
This controls the rate of the main control loop in Hz. This should only be changed by developers. This only takes effect on restart. Values over 400 are considered highly experimental.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **50 to 400:** hertz

---

## SCHED_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Scheduling optionsÂ

**Description:**
This controls optional aspects of the scheduler.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Enable per-task perf info:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Enable per-task perf info

---

## SCR_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable ScriptingÂ

**Description:**
Controls if scripting is enabled

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Lua Scripts:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Lua Scripts

---

## SCR_VM_I_COUNT
**Display Name:** Scripting Virtual Machine Instruction CountÂ

**Description:**
The number virtual machine instructions that can be run before considering a script to have taken an excessive amount of time

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **10000:** 1000 to 1000000

---

## SCR_HEAP_SIZE
**Display Name:** Scripting Heap SizeÂ

**Description:**
Amount of memory available for scripting

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1024:** 1024 to 1048576

---

## SCR_DEBUG_OPTS
**Display Name:** Scripting Debug LevelÂ

**Description:**
Debugging options

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0No Scripts to run message if all scripts have stopped1Runtime messages for memory usage and execution time2Suppress logging scripts to dataflash3log runtime memory usage and execution time4Disable pre-arm check5Save CRC of current scripts to loaded and running checksum parameters enabling pre-arm6Disable heap expansion on allocation failure:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** No Scripts to run message if all scripts have stopped
- **1:** Runtime messages for memory usage and execution time
- **2:** Suppress logging scripts to dataflash
- **3:** log runtime memory usage and execution time
- **4:** Disable pre-arm check
- **5:** Save CRC of current scripts to loaded and running checksum parameters enabling pre-arm
- **6:** Disable heap expansion on allocation failure

---

## SCR_USER1
**Display Name:** Scripting User Parameter1Â

**Description:**
General purpose user variable input for scripts


---

## SCR_USER2
**Display Name:** Scripting User Parameter2Â

**Description:**
General purpose user variable input for scripts


---

## SCR_USER3
**Display Name:** Scripting User Parameter3Â

**Description:**
General purpose user variable input for scripts


---

## SCR_USER4
**Display Name:** Scripting User Parameter4Â

**Description:**
General purpose user variable input for scripts


---

## SCR_USER5
**Display Name:** Scripting User Parameter5Â

**Description:**
General purpose user variable input for scripts


---

## SCR_USER6
**Display Name:** Scripting User Parameter6Â

**Description:**
General purpose user variable input for scripts


---

## SCR_DIR_DISABLE
**Display Name:** Directory disableÂ

**Description:**
This will stop scripts being loaded from the given locations

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0ROMFS1APM/scripts:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** ROMFS
- **1:** APM/scripts

---

## SCR_LD_CHECKSUM
**Display Name:** Loaded script checksumÂ

**Description:**
Required XOR of CRC32 checksum of loaded scripts, vehicle will not arm with incorrect scripts loaded, -1 disables


---

## SCR_RUN_CHECKSUM
**Display Name:** Running script checksumÂ

**Description:**
Required XOR of CRC32 checksum of running scripts, vehicle will not arm with incorrect scripts running, -1 disables


---

## SCR_THD_PRIORITY
**Display Name:** Scripting thread priorityÂ

**Description:**
This sets the priority of the scripting thread. This is normally set to a low priority to prevent scripts from interfering with other parts of the system. Advanced users can change this priority if scripting needs to be prioritised for realtime applications. WARNING: changing this parameter can impact the stability of your flight controller. The scipting thread priority in this parameter is chosen based on a set of system level priorities for other subsystems. It is strongly recommended that you use the lowest priority that is sufficient for your application. Note that all scripts run at the same priority, so if you raise this priority you must carefully audit all lua scripts for behaviour that does not interfere with the operation of the system.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Normal1IO Priority2Storage Priority3UART Priority4I2C Priority5SPI Priority6Timer Priority7Main Priority8Boost Priority:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Normal
- **1:** IO Priority
- **2:** Storage Priority
- **3:** UART Priority
- **4:** I2C Priority
- **5:** SPI Priority
- **6:** Timer Priority
- **7:** Main Priority
- **8:** Boost Priority

---

## SCR_SDEV_EN
**Display Name:** Scripting serial device enableÂ

**Description:**
Enable scripting serial devices

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## SERIAL0_BAUD
**Display Name:** Serial0 baud rateÂ

**Description:**
The baud rate used on the USB console. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning1120022400448009960019192003838400575760011111110011511520023023040025625600046046080050050000092192160015001.5MBaud20002MBaud1250000012.5MBaud:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **1:** 1200
- **2:** 2400
- **4:** 4800
- **9:** 9600
- **19:** 19200
- **38:** 38400
- **57:** 57600
- **111:** 111100
- **115:** 115200
- **230:** 230400
- **256:** 256000
- **460:** 460800
- **500:** 500000
- **921:** 921600
- **1500:** 1.5MBaud
- **2000:** 2MBaud
- **12500000:** 12.5MBaud

---

**Description:**
Control what protocol to use on the console.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning1MAVLink12MAVLink2:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **1:** MAVLink1
- **2:** MAVLink2

## SERIAL6_BAUD
**Display Name:** Serial 6 Baud RateÂ

**Description:**
The baud rate used for Serial6. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning1120022400448009960019192003838400575760011111110011511520023023040025625600046046080050050000092192160015001.5MBaud20002MBaud1250000012.5MBaud:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **1:** 1200
- **2:** 2400
- **4:** 4800
- **9:** 9600
- **19:** 19200
- **38:** 38400
- **57:** 57600
- **111:** 111100
- **115:** 115200
- **230:** 230400
- **256:** 256000
- **460:** 460800
- **500:** 500000
- **921:** 921600
- **1500:** 1.5MBaud
- **2000:** 2MBaud
- **12500000:** 12.5MBaud

---

## SERIAL1_OPTIONS
**Display Name:** Telem1 optionsÂ

**Description:**
Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0InvertRX1InvertTX2HalfDuplex3SwapTXRX4RX_PullDown5RX_PullUp6TX_PullDown7TX_PullUp8RX_NoDMA9TX_NoDMA10Donât forward mavlink to/from11DisableFIFO12Ignore Streamrate:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** InvertRX
- **1:** InvertTX
- **2:** HalfDuplex
- **3:** SwapTXRX
- **4:** RX_PullDown
- **5:** RX_PullUp
- **6:** TX_PullDown
- **7:** TX_PullUp
- **8:** RX_NoDMA
- **9:** TX_NoDMA
- **10:** Donât forward mavlink to/from
- **11:** DisableFIFO
- **12:** Ignore Streamrate

---

## SERIAL_PASS1
**Display Name:** Serial passthru first portÂ

**Description:**
This sets one side of pass-through between two serial ports. Once both sides are set then all data received on either port will be passed to the other port

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled0Serial01Serial12Serial23Serial34Serial45Serial56Serial6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **0:** Serial0
- **1:** Serial1
- **2:** Serial2
- **3:** Serial3
- **4:** Serial4
- **5:** Serial5
- **6:** Serial6

---

## SERIAL_PASS2
**Display Name:** Serial passthru second portÂ

**Description:**
This sets one side of pass-through between two serial ports. Once both sides are set then all data received on either port will be passed to the other port

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning-1Disabled0Serial01Serial12Serial23Serial34Serial45Serial56Serial6:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **-1:** Disabled
- **0:** Serial0
- **1:** Serial1
- **2:** Serial2
- **3:** Serial3
- **4:** Serial4
- **5:** Serial5
- **6:** Serial6

---

## SERIAL_PASSTIMO
**Display Name:** Serial passthru timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This sets a timeout for serial pass-through in seconds. When the pass-through is enabled by setting the SERIAL_PASS1 and SERIAL_PASS2 parameters then it remains in effect until no data comes from the first port for SERIAL_PASSTIMO seconds. This allows the port to revent to its normal usage (such as MAVLink connection to a GCS) when it is no longer needed. A value of 0 means no timeout.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 120:** seconds

---

## SERVO_AUTO_TRIM
**Display Name:** Automatic servo trimÂ

**Description:**
This enables automatic servo trim in flight. Servos will be trimed in stabilized flight modes when the aircraft is close to level. Changes to servo trim will be saved every 10 seconds and will persist between flights. The automatic trim won't go more than 20% away from a centered trim.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disable1Enable:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disable
- **1:** Enable

---

## SERVO_RATE
**Display Name:** Servo default output rateÂ

**Description:**
Default output rate in Hz for all PWM outputs.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **25 to 400:** hertz

---

## SERVO_DSHOT_RATE
**Display Name:** Servo DShot output rateÂ

**Description:**
DShot output rate for all outputs as a multiple of the loop rate. 0 sets the output rate to be fixed at 1Khz for low loop rates. This value should never be set below 500Hz.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning01Khz1loop-rate2double loop-rate3triple loop-rate4quadruple loop rate:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** 1Khz
- **1:** loop-rate
- **2:** double loop-rate
- **3:** triple loop-rate
- **4:** quadruple loop rate

---

## SERVO_DSHOT_ESC
**Display Name:** Servo DShot ESC typeÂ

**Description:**
DShot ESC type for all outputs. The ESC type affects the range of DShot commands available and the bit widths used. None means that no dshot commands will be executed. Some ESC types support Extended DShot Telemetry (EDT) which allows telemetry other than RPM data to be returned when using bi-directional dshot. If you enable EDT you must install EDT capable firmware for correct operation.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1BLHeli32/Kiss/AM322BLHeli_S/BlueJay3BLHeli32/AM32/Kiss+EDT4BLHeli_S/BlueJay+EDT:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** BLHeli32/Kiss/AM32
- **2:** BLHeli_S/BlueJay
- **3:** BLHeli32/AM32/Kiss+EDT
- **4:** BLHeli_S/BlueJay+EDT

---

## SERVO_GPIO_MASK
**Display Name:** Servo GPIO maskÂ

**Description:**
Bitmask of outputs which will be available as GPIOs. Any output with either the function set to -1 or with the corresponding bit set in this mask will be available for use as a GPIO pin

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Servo 11Servo 22Servo 33Servo 44Servo 55Servo 66Servo 77Servo 88Servo 99Servo 1010Servo 1111Servo 1212Servo 1313Servo 1414Servo 1515Servo 1616Servo 1717Servo 1818Servo 1919Servo 2020Servo 2121Servo 2222Servo 2323Servo 2424Servo 2525Servo 2626Servo 2727Servo 2828Servo 2929Servo 3030Servo 3131Servo 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Servo 1
- **1:** Servo 2
- **2:** Servo 3
- **3:** Servo 4
- **4:** Servo 5
- **5:** Servo 6
- **6:** Servo 7
- **7:** Servo 8

- **29:** Servo 30
- **30:** Servo 31
- **31:** Servo 32

## SERVO_RC_FS_MSK
**Display Name:** Servo RC Failsafe MaskÂ

**Description:**
Bitmask of scaled passthru output channels which will be set to their trim value during rc failsafe instead of holding their last position before failsafe.

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0RCIN1Scaled1RCIN2Scaled2RCIN3Scaled3RCIN4Scaled4RCIN5Scaled5RCIN6Scaled6RCIN7Scaled7RCIN8Scaled8RCIN9Scaled9RCIN10Scaled10RCIN11Scaled11SRCIN12Scaled12RCIN13Scaled13RCIN14Scaled14RCIN15Scaled15RCIN16Scaled:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** RCIN1Scaled
- **1:** RCIN2Scaled
- **2:** RCIN3Scaled
- **3:** RCIN4Scaled
- **4:** RCIN5Scaled
- **5:** RCIN6Scaled
- **6:** RCIN7Scaled
- **7:** RCIN8Scaled

- **11:** SRCIN12Scaled
- **12:** RCIN13Scaled
- **13:** RCIN14Scaled
- **14:** RCIN15Scaled
- **15:** RCIN16Scaled

## SERVO_32_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Enable outputs 17 to 31Â

**Description:**
This allows for up to 32 outputs, enabling parameters for outputs above 16

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## SERVO10_MIN
**Display Name:** Minimum PWMÂ

**Description:**
minimum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 800 to 2200

---

## SERVO10_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum PWMÂ

**Description:**
maximum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

- **1:** 800 to 2200

## SERVO10_REVERSED
**Display Name:** Servo reverseÂ

**Description:**
Reverse servo operation. Set to 0 for normal operation. Set to 1 to reverse this output channel.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Normal1Reversed:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Normal
- **1:** Reversed

**Display Name:** Minimum PWMÂ

**Description:**
minimum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 800 to 2200

**Display Name:** Minimum PWMÂ

**Description:**
minimum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** 800 to 2200

maximum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

- **0:** Normal
- **1:** Reversed

## SERVO_BLH_MASK
**Display Name:** BLHeli Channel BitmaskÂ

**Description:**
Enable of BLHeli pass-thru servo protocol support to specific channels. This mask is in addition to motors enabled using SERVO_BLH_AUTO (if any)

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Channel11Channel22Channel33Channel44Channel55Channel66Channel77Channel88Channel99Channel1010Channel1111Channel1212Channel1313Channel1414Channel1515Channel1616Channel 1717Channel 1818Channel 1919Channel 2020Channel 2121Channel 2222Channel 2323Channel 2424Channel 2525Channel 2626Channel 2727Channel 2828Channel 2929Channel 3030Channel 3131Channel 32:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Channel1
- **1:** Channel2
- **2:** Channel3
- **3:** Channel4
- **4:** Channel5
- **5:** Channel6
- **6:** Channel7
- **7:** Channel8
- **8:** Channel9

## SERVO_BLH_AUTO
**Display Name:** BLHeli pass-thru auto-enable for multicopter motorsÂ

**Description:**
If set to 1 this auto-enables BLHeli pass-thru support for all multicopter motors

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## SERVO_BLH_TEST
**Display Name:** BLHeli internal interface testÂ

**Description:**
Setting SERVO_BLH_TEST to a motor number enables an internal test of the BLHeli ESC protocol to the corresponding ESC. The debug output is displayed on the USB console.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1TestMotor12TestMotor23TestMotor34TestMotor45TestMotor56TestMotor67TestMotor78TestMotor8:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** TestMotor1
- **2:** TestMotor2
- **3:** TestMotor3
- **4:** TestMotor4
- **5:** TestMotor5
- **6:** TestMotor6
- **7:** TestMotor7
- **8:** TestMotor8

---

## SERVO_BLH_TMOUT
**Display Name:** BLHeli protocol timeoutÂ

**Description:**
This sets the inactivity timeout for the BLHeli protocol in seconds. If no packets are received in this time normal MAVLink operations are resumed. A value of 0 means no timeout

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 300:** seconds

---

## SERVO_BLH_TRATE
**Display Name:** BLHeli telemetry rateÂ

**Description:**
This sets the rate in Hz for requesting telemetry from ESCs. It is the rate per ESC. Setting to zero disables telemetry requests

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 500:** hertz

---

## SERVO_BLH_DEBUG
**Display Name:** BLHeli debug levelÂ

**Description:**
When set to 1 this enabled verbose debugging output over MAVLink when the blheli protocol is active. This can be used to diagnose failures.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

## SERVO_BLH_OTYPE
**Display Name:** BLHeli output type overrideÂ

**Description:**
When set to a non-zero value this overrides the output type for the output channels given by SERVO_BLH_MASK. This can be used to enable DShot on outputs that are not part of the multicopter motors group.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1OneShot2OneShot1253Brushed4DShot1505DShot3006DShot6007DShot1200:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** OneShot
- **2:** OneShot125
- **3:** Brushed
- **4:** DShot150
- **5:** DShot300
- **6:** DShot600
- **7:** DShot1200

---

## SERVO_BLH_PORT
**Display Name:** Control portÂ

**Description:**
This sets the mavlink channel to use for blheli pass-thru. The channel number is determined by the number of serial ports configured to use mavlink. So 0 is always the console, 1 is the next serial port using mavlink, 2 the next after that and so on.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Console1Mavlink Serial Channel12Mavlink Serial Channel23Mavlink Serial Channel34Mavlink Serial Channel45Mavlink Serial Channel5:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Console
- **1:** Mavlink Serial Channel1
- **2:** Mavlink Serial Channel2
- **3:** Mavlink Serial Channel3
- **4:** Mavlink Serial Channel4
- **5:** Mavlink Serial Channel5

---

## SERVO_BLH_POLES
**Display Name:** BLHeli Motor PolesÂ

**Description:**
This allows calculation of true RPM from ESC's eRPM. The default is 14.


---

## SERVO_ROB_POSMIN

Position minimum at servo min value. This should be within the position control range of the servos, normally 0 to 4095


---

## SERVO_ROB_POSMAX

Position maximum at servo max value. This should be within the position control range of the servos, normally 0 to 4095


---

## SERVO_VOLZ_RANGE
**Display Name:** Range of travelÂ

**Description:**
Range to map between 1000 and 2000 PWM. Default value of 200 gives full +-100 deg range of extended position command. This results in 0.2 deg movement per US change in PWM. If the full range is not needed it can be reduced to increase resolution. 40 deg range gives 0.04 deg movement per US change in PWM, this is higher resolution than possible with the VOLZ protocol so further reduction in range will not improve resolution. Reduced range does allow PWMs outside the 1000 to 2000 range, with 40 deg range 750 PWM results in a angle of -30 deg, 2250 would be +30 deg. This is still limited by the 200 deg maximum range of the actuator.


---

## SID_AXIS
**Display Name:** System identification axisÂ

**Description:**
Controls which axis are being excited.  Set to non-zero to see more parameters

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0None1Input Roll Angle2Input Pitch Angle3Input Yaw Angle4Recovery Roll Angle5Recovery Pitch Angle6Recovery Yaw Angle7Rate Roll8Rate Pitch9Rate Yaw10Mixer Roll11Mixer Pitch12Mixer Yaw13Mixer Thrust:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** None
- **1:** Input Roll Angle
- **2:** Input Pitch Angle
- **3:** Input Yaw Angle
- **4:** Recovery Roll Angle
- **5:** Recovery Pitch Angle
- **6:** Recovery Yaw Angle
- **7:** Rate Roll
- **8:** Rate Pitch
- **9:** Rate Yaw
- **10:** Mixer Roll
- **11:** Mixer Pitch
- **12:** Mixer Yaw
- **13:** Mixer Thrust

---

## SID_MAGNITUDE
**Display Name:** System identification Chirp MagnitudeÂ

**Description:**
Magnitude of sweep in deg, deg/s and 0-1 for mixer outputs.


---

## SID_F_START_HZ
**Display Name:** System identification Start FrequencyÂ

**Description:**
Frequency at the start of the sweep

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 100:** hertz

---

## SID_F_STOP_HZ
**Display Name:** System identification Stop FrequencyÂ

**Description:**
Frequency at the end of the sweep

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0.01 to 100:** hertz

---

## SID_T_FADE_IN
**Display Name:** System identification Fade in timeÂ

**Description:**
Time to reach maximum amplitude of sweep

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 20:** seconds

---

## SID_T_REC
**Display Name:** System identification Total Sweep lengthÂ

**Description:**
Time taken to complete the sweep

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 255:** seconds

---

## SID_T_FADE_OUT
**Display Name:** System identification Fade out timeÂ

**Description:**
Time to reach zero amplitude at the end of the sweep

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 5:** seconds

---

## SID_XY_CTRL_MUL
**Display Name:** System identification XY control multiplierÂ

**Description:**
A multiplier for the XY velocity and position controller when using systemID in VTOL modes that do horizontal position and velocity control

## SIM_GYR_FAIL_MSK

Determines if the gyro reading updates are stopped when for an IMU simulated failure by ACCELx_FAIL params

- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Readings stopped

---

## SIM_GYR_FILE_RW
**Display Name:** Gyro data to/from filesÂ

**Description:**
Read and write gyro data to/from files

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Stop writing data1Read data from file2Write data to a file3Read data from file and stop on EOF:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Stop writing data
- **1:** Read data from file
- **2:** Write data to a file
- **3:** Read data from file and stop on EOF

**Specifications:**
- ** 4.1 and older101Custom 1102Custom 2:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning

- **1:** Yaw45
- **2:** Yaw90
- **3:** Yaw135
- **4:** Yaw180
- **5:** Yaw225
- **6:** Yaw270
- **7:** Yaw315
- **8:** Roll180
- **9:** Yaw45Roll180
- **10:** Yaw90Roll180
- **11:** Yaw135Roll180
- **12:** Pitch180
- **13:** Yaw225Roll180
- **14:** Yaw270Roll180
- **15:** Yaw315Roll180
- **16:** Roll90
- **17:** Yaw45Roll90
- **18:** Yaw90Roll90
- **19:** Yaw135Roll90
- **20:** Roll270
- **21:** Yaw45Roll270
- **22:** Yaw90Roll270
- **23:** Yaw135Roll270
- **24:** Pitch90
- **25:** Pitch270
- **26:** Yaw90Pitch180
- **27:** Yaw270Pitch180
- **28:** Pitch90Roll90
- **29:** Pitch90Roll180
- **30:** Pitch90Roll270
- **31:** Pitch180Roll90
- **32:** Pitch180Roll270
- **33:** Pitch270Roll90
- **34:** Pitch270Roll180
- **35:** Pitch270Roll270
- **36:** Yaw90Pitch180Roll90
- **37:** Yaw270Roll90
- **38:** Yaw293Pitch68Roll180
- **39:** Pitch315
- **40:** Pitch315Roll90
- **42:** Roll45
- **43:** Roll315
- **100:** Custom 4.1 and older
- **101:** Custom 1
- **102:** Custom 2

- **1:** Enabled

Precland device center's longitude

SIM_Precland extra options

- **Bit:** Meaning

Precland device orientation vector

- **4:** Back

Precland device rate. e.g led patter refresh rate, RF message rate, etc.

- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 200:** hertz

## SIM_PLD_SHIP
**Display Name:** SIM_Precland follow shipÂ

This makes the position of the landing beacon follow the simulated ship from SIM_SHIP. The ship movement is controlled with the SIM_SHIP parameters

- **ValueMeaning0Disabled1Enabled:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disabled
- **1:** Enabled

---

**Description:**
Precland device radiance type: it can be a cylinder, a cone, or a sphere.

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0cylinder1cone2sphere:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** cylinder
- **1:** cone
- **2:** sphere

---

**Description:**
Precland device systems rotation from north

**Specifications:**
- **Increment:** Range
- **1:** -180 to +180

SITL Loop rate

## SIM_RC_FAIL

- **2:** All Channels neutral except Throttle is 950us

## SIM_RFL_OPTS
**Display Name:** FlightAxis optionsÂ

**Description:**
Bitmask of FlightAxis options

**Specifications:**
- **BitMeaning0Reset position on startup1Swap first 4 and last 4 servos (for quadplane testing)2Demix heli servos and send roll/pitch/collective/yaw3Donât print frame rate stats:** Bit
- **Bit:** Meaning
- **0:** Reset position on startup
- **1:** Swap first 4 and last 4 servos (for quadplane testing)
- **2:** Demix heli servos and send roll/pitch/collective/yaw
- **3:** Donât print frame rate stats

servo filter

## SIM_SERVO_SPEED

**Description:**
servo speed (time for 60 degree deflection). If DELAY and FILTER are not set then this is converted to a 1p lowpass filter. If DELAY or FILTER are set then this is treated as a rate of change limit

Wave amplitude in SITL

Direction wave is coming from

Wave enable and modes

- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** disabled
- **1:** roll and pitch
- **2:** roll and pitch and heave

## SIM_WAVE_LENGTH

Wave length in SITL

## SIM_WAVE_SPEED

Wave speed in SITL


---

## SIM_WIND_DIR
**Display Name:** Direction simulated wind is coming fromÂ

**Description:**
Allows you to set wind direction (true deg) in sim


---

## SIM_WIND_DIR_Z
**Display Name:** Simulated wind vertical directionÂ

**Description:**
Allows you to set vertical wind direction (true deg) in sim. 0 means pure horizontal wind. 90 means pure updraft.


---

## SIM_WIND_SPD
**Display Name:** Simulated Wind speedÂ

**Description:**
Allows you to emulate wind in sim


---

## SIM_WIND_T
**Display Name:** Wind Profile TypeÂ

**Description:**
Selects how wind varies from surface to WIND_T_ALT

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0square law1none2linear-see WIND_T_COEF:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** square law
- **1:** none
- **2:** linear-see WIND_T_COEF

---

## SIM_WIND_TC
**Display Name:** Wind variation time constantÂ

**Description:**
this controls the time over which wind changes take effect


---

## SIM_WIND_TURB
**Display Name:** Simulated Wind variationÂ

**Description:**
Allows you to emulate random wind variations in sim


---

## SIM_WIND_T_ALT
**Display Name:** Full Wind AltitudeÂ

**Description:**
Altitude at which wind reaches full strength, decaying from full strength as altitude lowers to ground level


---

## SIM_WIND_T_COEF
**Display Name:** Linear Wind Curve CoeffÂ

**Description:**
For linear wind profile,wind is reduced by (Altitude-WIND_T_ALT) x this value


---

## SIM_WOW_PIN
**Display Name:** Weight on Wheels PinÂ

**Description:**
SITL set this simulated pin to true if vehicle is on ground


---

## SOAR_ENABLE
**Display Name:** Is the soaring mode enabled or notÂ

**Description:**
Toggles the soaring mode on and off

**Specifications:**
- **ValueMeaning0Disable1Enable:** Value
- **Value:** Meaning
- **0:** Disable
- **1:** Enable

---

## SOAR_VSPEED
**Display Name:** Vertical v-speedÂ

**Description:**
Rate of climb to trigger themalling speed

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 10:** meters per second

---

## SOAR_Q1
**Display Name:** Process noiseÂ

**Description:**
Standard deviation of noise in process for strength


---

## SOAR_Q2
**Display Name:** Process noiseÂ

**Description:**
Standard deviation of noise in process for position and radius


---

## SOAR_R
**Display Name:** Measurement noiseÂ

**Description:**
Standard deviation of noise in measurement


---

## SOAR_DIST_AHEAD
**Display Name:** Distance to thermal centerÂ

**Description:**
Initial guess of the distance to the thermal center

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 100:** meters

---

## SOAR_MIN_THML_S
**Display Name:** Minimum thermalling timeÂ

**Description:**
Minimum number of seconds to spend thermalling

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 600:** seconds

---

## SOAR_MIN_CRSE_S
**Display Name:** Minimum cruising timeÂ

**Description:**
Minimum number of seconds to spend cruising

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 600:** seconds

---

## SOAR_POLAR_CD0
**Display Name:** Zero lift drag coef.Â

**Description:**
Zero lift drag coefficient


---

## SOAR_POLAR_B
**Display Name:** Induced drag coeffientÂ

**Description:**
Induced drag coeffient


---

## SOAR_POLAR_K
**Display Name:** Cl factorÂ

**Description:**
Cl factor 2*m*g/(rho*S)

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **20 to 400:** square meter per square second

---

## SOAR_ALT_MAX
**Display Name:** Maximum soaring altitude, relative to the home locationÂ

**Description:**
Don't thermal any higher than this.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 5000.0:** meters

---

## SOAR_ALT_MIN
**Display Name:** Minimum soaring altitude, relative to the home locationÂ

**Description:**
Don't get any lower than this.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 1000.0:** meters

---

## SOAR_ALT_CUTOFF
**Display Name:** Maximum power altitude, relative to the home locationÂ

**Description:**
Cut off throttle at this alt.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **0 to 5000.0:** meters

---

## SOAR_MAX_DRIFT
**Display Name:** (Optional) Maximum drift distance to allow when thermalling.Â

**Description:**
The previous mode will be restored if the horizontal distance to the thermalling start location exceeds this value. -1 to disable.


---

## SOAR_MAX_RADIUS
**Display Name:** (Optional) Maximum distance from homeÂ

**Description:**
RTL will be entered when a thermal is exited and the plane is more than this distance from home. -1 to disable.


---

## SOAR_THML_BANK
**Display Name:** Thermalling bank angleÂ

**Description:**
This parameter sets the bank angle to use when thermalling. Typically 30 - 45 degrees works well.

**Specifications:**
- **Range:** Units
- **20 to 50:** degrees

---

## SOAR_THML_ARSPD
**Display Name:** Specific setting for airspeed when soaring in THERMAL mode.Â

**Description:**
If non-zero this airspeed will be used when thermalling. A value of 0 will use AIRSPEED_CRUISE.


---

## SOAR_CRSE_ARSPD
**Display Name:** Specific setting for airspeed when soaring in AUTO mode.Â

**Description:**
If non-zero this airspeed will be used when cruising between thermals in AUTO. If set to -1, airspeed will be selected based on speed-to-fly theory. If set to 0, then AIRSPEED_CRUISE will be used while cruising between thermals.


---

## SOAR_THML_FLAP
**Display Name:** Flap percent to be used during thermalling flight.Â

**Description:**
This sets the flap when in LOITER with soaring active. Overrides the usual auto flap behaviour.


---
